Report_1_Synopsis: B737 Flight Crew reported a bird strike right after takeoff; resulting in an angle of attack vane to shear off.
Air Taxi flight crew reported not hearing ATC for a while during cruise.  Reportedly; they had not been properly handed off to the next Center.

B737-800 flight crew reported encountering multiple bird strikes and upon landing; realized there was no nose wheel steering. The flight crew found out there was a hydraulic leak and the aircraft was towed to the gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger misconduct incident during flight that initiated due to non-compliance with face mask policy.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing ORD in trail of an A320.
A Center Controller reported an Air Carrier was left on a heading too long and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Pilot reported a loss of GPS signal; possibly attributed to 5G interference.
Ramp Agent reported the retaining strap between the aircraft and tug broke during towing allowing the aircraft to roll backwards on its own.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion with the LGA airport La Guardia 7 SID engine out procedures.
Pilot reported glideslope problems while inbound to GLS airport.
Flight crew reported executing a go around due to being below altitude; uncomfortable with the approach; and the airport environment.
Air Carrier Captain reported the airport chart for MMQT should be updated.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an RA fault on short final at FLL; citing possible 5G interference as the cause of the event.
Air carrier pilot reported possible 5G interference while landing at LAX Runway 25L.
Helicopter maintenance technicians reported that after scheduled maintenance they learned a pilot discovered during preflight inspection the tail rotor gearbox oil cap was not installed.
Pilot reported issues with the condition of DWA airport including faded runway and taxiway markings and a rotating beacon with a non-operational light.
Air carrier Captain reported flying into a normal controlled airport; but Tower was closed due to COVID and having numerous issues in the air and on the ground with other aircraft.

JAN Tower Controller reported the fire alarm goes off periodically for no reason.  This is a known reoccurring issue.  Reporter stated not knowing if it is a real fire or a false alarm every time it goes off is an issue which causes distraction.
B737 MAX 8 First Officer reported finding unusual wear on a main tire which maintenance deferred for a later replacement.  First Officer was informed that it was a known problem.
Flight Instructor reported experiencing a NMAC while on a base leg in the traffic pattern that required evasive action to avoid another aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported electrical malfunctions and flap disagree indications.
B777 pilot reported during taxi into gate a vehicle drove under their nose.
First Officer reported experiencing a TCAS Resolution Advisory to remain clear of conflicting traffic after departure from Newark airport.
Challenger 350 Technician reported that MEL placards were not installed after life rafts were removed for overhaul.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a bird strike while flying which resulted in damage to the UAS.

TRACON Controller and pilot reported the pilot descended through the assigned altitude and continued below the minimum vectoring altitude. The controller issued a low altitude alert and instructed a climb back to the original cleared altitude.
B737 NG FO reported his aircraft was 'significantly upset' just before touchdown at EWR by jet blast from a taxiing B777.
D01 Controller reported aircraft acknowledged traffic in sight then returned on frequency to report a NMAC.
Pilot reported Maintenance Control incorrectly placed an MEL on a discrepancy.

A Center Controller reported during a period of aircraft weather deviations into one sector no action was taken by Management or Traffic Management Unit (TMU) staff to reroute aircraft away from the sector causing it to exceed the designated maximum safe traffic numbers for that sector.
LAX Tower Controller reported an aircraft did not follow printed taxi instructions on 'the North route'. Reporter stated this has been an ongoing issue.
TPA TRACON Controller reported repeated frequency outages occur creating unsafe situations.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC vectored them to intercept the ILS approach course but the ILS was set up for the opposite runway resulting in a temporary loss of aircraft control.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an RA while descending over VNY airport on approach to BUR. Flight crew complied with the RA and continued the approach.
AFW Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited airspace design as a contributing factor.
Air carrier Captain reported an automation error between two similar sounding fixes; WITEN and WINEN; resulted in ATC issued course correction.

A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft turned to the wrong heading and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The course deviation placed the aircraft in an area of poor radio coverage where ATC could not communicate with them.
Air carrier Captain reported that SJU TRACON informed the crew that the STAR they requested was NOTAMed out of service; however; other sources did not show any such NOTAMs.

A Center Controller working combined sectors reported they became distracted and could not focus on the safe operation of their sector after being notified of the requirement to respond to an email from OPM to describe their work by the end of the day.
An air carrier pilot reported MCO ramp has a pothole that required excessive thrust to taxi out of.
TRACON Departure Controller reported an aircraft deviated from their assigned heading and flew below the minimum vectoring altitude.
ZLA Controller reported an airspace deviation when an air carrier went through two sectors undetected; until the third sector Controller identified the aircraft.
Tower Controller reported a possible minimum vectoring altitude violation due to a miscommunication in regards to the assigned altitude of a go around instruction.
A student pilot and their instructor reported the student turned the fuel selector switch off resulting in engine shutdown and a safe landing in a field.
Customer Service Representative reported personal issues with the removal of safety shields relating to health concerns.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

MDT TRACON Controller reported numerous conflicts occur between VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC and IFR aircraft on their final approach course. This is reportedly an ongoing issue and the reporter suggested the MDT airspace should be redesigned as a Class C.

Air carrier flight crew reported that the left side PFD and MFD malfunctioned at the same location on Runway 22L at ORD during two takeoff attempts. Another aircraft experienced a similar issue at same location.
Air carrier pilot reported incorrect charting for EWR taxiways A9 and A10. Pilot stated the markings on the ground conflict with what is charted on the airport diagram.
Pilot reported their navigation database does not contain the fix CADRE for the ROCKT1 SID from LUK airport.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of GPS on approach to DEN related to possible 5G interference.
Tug driver reported aircraft breakaway at gate resulted from nose gear coming out of cradle. Aircraft was stopped.
Air carrier Captain reported a lack of airport signage and runway identification markings at EIDW/DUB as the taxiway was undergoing re-painting.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.

ZMP Center Controller reported many pilots have asked for a fix on approaches to Canadian airports due to the close proximity to ZMP's airspace and boundary but the lack of information on fixes in Canadian airspace made it difficult for ATC to provide assistance.

Corporate jet Captain reported executing a go-around from approach to SMO after experiencing GPS anomalies. Reporter stated he was notified that other aircraft experienced similar anomalies in that area.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving ADS-B FAULT messages and no GPS signal on the aircraft and was later informed by ATC upon inquiry that GPS jamming was occurring in the area. The GPS signal later returned with no navigation degradation throughout the GPS disruption.
B767 First Officer reported safely returning to departure airport after a cockpit window outer pane cracked during climb.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a clearance to fly direct to BRNDN intersection when ATC's intent was for the flight crew to fly direct to the Brandon VOR; YBR. The two fixes were both pronounced the same but spelled differently.

B737 Captain reported during pushback the aircraft wingtip was very close to an adjacent pushback aircraft. Pilot lost communication with tug driver who stopped abruptly resulting in the tow bar shear pin to break.
ORD ramp worker reported returning a cargo loader to the terminal hardstand resulted in a collision with another loader parked in an inadequately lit area.
Pilot reported an FMS error caused them to deviate off course. The pilot reported prior to the deviation they were issued a reroute to avoid military airspace that is uncharted.
Pushout Driver reported the tow bar pins broke and the tow bar damaged the aircraft's nose gear.
A Flight Crew reported they initiated a go around on short final due to a VFR aircraft on TCAS which appeared to be on a conflicting course.

Flight crew reported autopilot descending below glideslope with false signals resulted in disconnect to rejoin the ILS. Captain uncertain whether this was ILS critical area incursion; or ground ILS equipment failure.
Flight Crew reported a family of Canadian Geese on the runway during landing rollout.
GA pilot reported unmarked power lines near 3KY4 airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the LAS NITZ1 RNAV Engine Out for Runway 8L page diagram depiction of FLAAR and the 12.7 ring is confusing.

Aircraft Owner with a CFI reported the aircraft ran out of fuel and the CFI landed the aircraft in a field with no damage. The owner used the incorrect fuel dip stick to check their fuel prior to departure.
Air carrier Captain reported a battery powered wheelchair that was loaded on board was not referenced in the manual. It was later determined the battery met all the requirements.
A TRACON Controller reported they lost communication with an aircraft on a vector due to a frequency outage and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier Captain reported the Engine Fail Procedure for Runway 31 out of COS may be incomplete.
Air carrier pilot reported confusing and contradictory information on MHR taxiway weight limits.
A TRACON Controller reported they turned an Air Carrier at 4;000 feet away from an unidentified VFR aircraft also at 4;000 feet averting a possible mid air collision.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a false localizer signal on approach to JAC that resulted in an early turn to final approach course; potentially putting them in conflict with terrain. Reporter disconnected the autopilot and corrected the course.
Air carrier Captain reported an unreliable glideslope signal at SJU Airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported they believe the SFO METAR reports consistently issue incorrect wind directions.
B757 Captain reported having to abruptly stop the aircraft due to observing a baggage tug with carts cross in front of the aircraft during initial taxi start.
A Corporate pilot reported GPS interference.

GA pilot reported a fueling truck at LRU airport parks too close to buildings and parked aircraft; is not properly marked as flammable; and fire extinguishers are not conspicuously apparent on the truck as required.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS interference on departure from RKSI.
A flight crew reported they attempted to taxi off the runway on a closed taxiway. They stopped when they saw cones but were still partially on the runway causing a subsequent arrival to go around.
A ramp wing walker reported they used their wands to stop a truck on a temporary roadway abeam the ramp from coming into contact with an aircraft being pushed back.

Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a ground vehicle while taxiing into the gate; while under parking guidance from a ramp marshaller. The Captain stopped the aircraft; then continued to the gate; when the vehicle turned around and the safety zone was cleared.
Air carrier First Officer reported while flying through an area of poor GPS and radio signals they missed an ATC frequency change.

B737 flight crew reported an unstable approach at night with no vertical or ground guidance resulting in an unusual attitude situation. Flight crew regained aircraft control with upset recovery procedures.

Pilot reports the condition of runways and airport facilities are falling into disrepair. Pilot states grass runway conditions are becoming unsafe for landing aircraft because the grass has grown too high.
B747 Captain reported taxiing into the gate a helicopter crossed over the wing resulting in a critical ground conflict event.

Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway excursion from Taxiway B at LAX at night in the rain. Reportedly; a lack of green taxi centerline lights and poor centerline reflectivity contributed to the event.
Air carrier pilot reported ramp exit spots on movement and non movement areas at DFW have been painted the same yellow color resulting in a taxiway incursion from Spot 32.
Flight Instructor reported an area of the taxiway was fenced off and taxi lines repainted at DXE airport. Pilot states there was no NOTAM warning pilots of the change and obstruction.
B737-800 Ground Service Technician reported that while towing the aircraft; due to the lack of proper safety envelope markings and poor lighting; the aircraft collided with the service stairs.

A90 TRACON controllers stated the Wind Shear Alert only alerted at the Supervisor desk and the Controller working the final approach could not hear the warning. Reporter stated at times it is necessary for the Watch Supervisor to not be at the desk where the alert sounds.
Air carrier Captain reported a lack of ground markings or signs while taxiing on specific taxiways at MCI makes identifying taxiways at night difficult.

I90 TRACON Controller reported that another controller had to issue a traffic alert to an airliner on final approach due to a VFR aircraft that was operating directly below the Bravo; which happens all the time.
Captain reported loss of GPS signal and VNAV on GPS approach resulted in change to a visual approach.
Non-flying pilot reported an aborted takeoff after encountering birds.
ZAB Center Controller reported planned GPS jamming in their sectors near the TXO VOR caused an air carrier and other aircraft to experience navigation difficulties.
Air Carrier Pilot reported there was no Taxiway signage for Kilo while taxing on Bravo. The pilot stated after being given a clearance 'taxi B to K'; Kilo was difficult to identify.
CL300 flight crew reported striking the rudder of a Pilatus while taxiing for parking under a marshallers guidance.
Pilot reported a red CAS message indicating high ITT on takeoff roll and rejected the takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation while on the VKTRY 2 arrival to DAL airport.  Similar sounding fix names FAWNT and PHAUP were cited as contributing to the event.
Pilot reported large dog on runway during takeoff. Pilot took evasive action to avoid hitting dog.
Pilot flying a visual approach reported a near CFIT incident in day VFR conditions.
Beech Bonanza pilot reported observing an oil leak on top of the engine cowling during climb. The pilot returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported a safety concern regarding a lot of loose asphalt and FOD being on the taxi route.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS interference in Myanmar/Yangon and China/Kunming airspace.
Large transport flight crew on final approach to PIE reported that they received a TCAS/RA Alert for a helicopter maneuvering in their flight path.
Air Carrier Captain reported a near collision with two separate ground vehicles while taxiing at night on the ramp.  The Captain abruptly stopped both times to avoid a collision.
Air Carrier Captain reported 'GNSS Not Available EICAS' message received during taxi. Local maintenance stated it was a known issue and cleared to depart.
General aviation pilot flying reported confusing a runway for a taxiway at TRK airport.
Air Traffic Controller reported trees obstruct the BGM Tower's view of the ramp area creating a blind spot that requires extra communication with aircraft.
B737-800 flight crew reported push crews lack of attention resulted in winglet damage by contact with truck ladder.
Pilot reported the airport beacon at LKU is very weak and difficult to see.
Air carrier First Officer reported loss of GPS signal on arrival into DEN; possibly related to 5G interference.

Air Carrier pilot reported an air carrier taxiing onto the runway had to take evasive action to avoid the reporter's aircraft which was at the hold short line. B737 and A320 aircraft taxiing on the new Taxiway J centerline markings at DCA do not remain clear of the north-south hold short line.
Helicopter flight instructor reported another helicopter on a converging resulted in taking immediate evasive action and a NMAC event.
Air carrier Captain reported a conflict between published crossing restrictions and the FMS database of both the KEDMA1B SID and the XUDED1B STAR at MMPR airport.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was seated in the company social distancing blocked seat.
A80 TRACON Controller reported AUO Tower's shout line has been out of service for an extended period and has made timely communication and coordination with the Tower difficult.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported a loose piece of concrete on the ramp at JVL struck and caused damage to the propeller during taxi.

A Tower Ground Controller reported tower positions were combined due to lack of staffing and no one in the tower noticed the Local Controller taxied an aircraft across a runway at the same time another aircraft was departing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion while taxiing at night at RJTT and cited the close proximity of the taxiways; poor lighting; and confusing signage as contributing to the event.

Air carrier pilot reported Runway 17R at DFW is unsafe for departures due to its shortened length and does not have enough length for potential rejected takeoffs. There was an active construction zone at the end of the runway; which added to the safety hazard for takeoffs utilizing that runway. The airport pages were also still showing the runway with its original; longer length.
Pilot reported safety concerns related to inadequate staffing that could result if employees take leave in order to not comply with the company's vaccine mandate.

Air carrier Captain reported there were no guide lines for parking or taxiing on the East Pad at SAN and it was difficult to determine if the aircraft was clear from other objects. Another air carrier approached the area and also could not determine if there was proper wing tip clearance. Airport Operations had to be called to clear the conflict.
Air carrier First Officer reported GPS and Transponder failure along with course misalignment indications on localizer.
Pilot reported a NMAC on base leg resulted in taking evasive action.
Air carrier First Officer reported a taxi error at RJTT due to taxiway sign being obscured by tall grass.

C172 Flight Instructor reported the LRY and LWC airports are referred to using the same name and share the same CTAF; with similar airfield layouts that resulted in confusion with pilots and ATC. Flight Instructor also reported these two airports are 47nm apart creating radio overlap.

Corporate jet pilot reported a near miss with an aircraft while descending on final approach which resulted in a TCAS RA. The pilot leveled off following the TCAS RA guidance; then continued to landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported during push back operations at BOS airport; a lack of communication and visual with tug resulted in aircraft rolling into tug.
SK64 pilot reported a lack of flight time limitations of U.S. registered aircraft operations in foreign countries resulted in severe fatigue situations.

Air carrier First Officer reported the flight crew loaded the wrong fix and flew approximately 8 miles off course before being corrected by ATC. The reporter stated that the fixes KP12E and KP21E are quite similar and also close in proximity; which led to the mistake going unnoticed during route verification.

Air carrier Captain reported intermittent GPS loss during cruise prior to descent and informed ATC; who also stated that other aircraft were experiencing GPS loss. There were no NOTAMs indicating GPS jamming or spoofing in the area.
Air carrier First Officer reported MYR airport diagram indicates taxiway A4 as Z.
Light aircraft Flight Instructor reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at GSO during initial climb.
F11 Controller reported an unsafe scenario with two aircraft that were in a conflict due to a STAR and a VFR practice area being in close proximity.
Air Carrier Pilot reported the taxiway signage is misleading at DEN airport.
G650 flight crew reported erroneous airspeed and altitude indications after takeoff.
A pilot reported a NMAC while practicing ground reference maneuvers in the traffic pattern of a non-towered private airport.
TRACON Controller conducting training reported the trainee descended an aircraft to an altitude 100 feet below the minimum vectoring altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the taxi line at MSLP airport is too near the grass area for a large aircraft; creating a safety concern.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing GPS jamming in the vicinity of SJN VORTAC.
Cessna 550 Captain reported the GMU PAPI appeared to be mis-aligned in the vertical path; compared to the aircraft derived vertical path while on final approach.
An aerobatic pilot who has a FAA waiver for a designated Aerobatic Practice Area reported traffic routinely flies through the airspace when it is advertised in use.
Air Carrier Captain reported passing over a significant bump on the Runway 23L surface at MMMX that caused the premature and uncommanded rotation of the aircraft as the nose gear passed over it.
GA pilot reported the SNA airport Tower was closed possibly due to COVID-19 resulting in difficulty closing the IFR flight plan.
Center Controller reported multiple read back errors while wearing a mandated face mask.

Air carrier technician reported that while reviewing the aircraft logbook of a B787 they found a carry forward item with incorrect limits applied to a high-pressure stage one turbine blade that had damage which caused an unscheduled engine change.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxi error at EWR due to new taxiways and ramp markings not being depicted on charts.
B767 flight crew reported operating the aircraft in revenue service with an incorrect fuel management panel installed. The aircraft was later removed from service and the correct panel was installed.
Pilot reported not having flown in a while; having difficulty finding an airport; and having issues with ATC.

A321 pilot reported to commend ground controller for professionalism; handling multiple aircraft due to staffing shortages. Pilot suggested that companies provide guidance to crews to be attentive and professional in situations such as this.
Light aircraft flight instructor reported an airspace violation occurred when they departed from their cruise altitude after experiencing GPS jamming in the HSV area.
B777-200 Captain reported Hazmat documents contained Hazmat cargo weight errors caused by Weight & Balance software/automation  issues.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported a Captain was reporting the failure of a GPS unit prior to oceanic airspace. The Dispatcher had difficulty convincing the Captain that the aircraft was in a known area of GPS jamming.
B737 pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter after takeoff following a vector from ATC.  Helicopter pilot descended to avoid colliding with the B737.

Air carrier flight crew reported on short final a large flock of birds climbed into the aircraft's flight path; resulting in several bird strikes and aircraft damage. The air crew continued the approach and landed without further event.
ZBW Controller reported limited radio capability to issue a climb clearance resulted in an aircraft entering a higher MVA and a CFTT event.
Pilot conducting a VOR 18 approach at night to TUP reported bright white lights to the left of the runway could be confused with PAPI lights and give a pilot a false glideslope indication.
B737-800 flight crew reported multiple bird strikes during takeoff.
Pilot reported suspect alterations to the aircraft; engine and propeller logbooks after an alternator bearing failure in flight.
Center Controller reported when they issued a clearance to CRL VOR; the pilot responded that the fix CRL was in Mexico according to their database.
Cirrus Vision SJ50 pilot reported on visual approach; observed multiple aircraft in conflict resulting in taking evasive action and an NMAC.

B757 Captain noted a loss of center system hydraulic quantity after accomplishing flight control checks.  After returning to gate crew was told by maintenance there was a leak in the tail section of aircraft.
Air Carrier Captain reported the Jeppesen FD Pro App has charting errors depicting the Anchorage Arctic FIR boundary notes and the Gander Oceanic Transition Area.
Air carrier First Officer reported spots Q and R are not depicted on the AMM at IAH Airport.

Air carrier ground employee reported an aircraft that was parked at the gate with nose wheel chocks installed rolled back about 1.5 feet because deice fluid on the ramp allowed the chock to skid. Chock was reinserted and no damage reported.
Air carrier Captain reported that glide slope anomalies on approach to SFO ILS 28R led to an unstabilized approach and a low altitude alert from ATC.
General aviation First Officer reported a near miss with a departing aircraft on a visual approach.
Air carrier Captain reported similar sounding fixes on the AMA RNP Z 22 approach; PULBE and TELVE; which caused a clearance and heading deviation.

ZDC ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and separation was lost with another aircraft. The ZDC Controller reported their Standard Operating Procedures cause TCAS/RA events to occur.
B737-800 technicians reported that as the tail jack failed in the middle of jacking the aircraft; the aircraft tail dropped hard on the tail jack; causing significant damage to the tail section.
Flight instructor reported the aircraft's wheel got stuck in a taxiway crack that had just been tarred over.
Corporate pilot reported a taxiway incursion; indicating non-standard faded taxiway markings as a contributing factor.
Air carrier pilot reported conflicting information concerning the JFK WA taxiway operational status as identified in a NOTAM; and the Jepp Pro AMM taxiway chart.
Air Carrier flight crew reported the aircraft left main landing gear left the taxiway while the aircraft was on the taxiway center line at BTR airport.

A320 Captain reported 2; N1 fan blades bent and not noted in logbook. Maintenance showed as reported and deferred several times. Captain stated they have no access to history of previous reports or deferments; to determine airworthiness.
B737 Captain reported approaching ramp at night; a passenger bus failed to yield to aircraft resulted in abrupt braking to avoid a collision.

Air carrier Captain reported that the Park Visual Runway 31 and RNAV (GPS) X Runway 31 approaches at LGA are both selected using RNVX 31." However; using that designation only loads the RNAV (GPS) X approach; making it impossible to load the Park Visual approach. The reporter states that the two approaches use different fixes and missed approach procedures; which can lead to confusion for pilots."
Air carrier Captain reported the FliteDeck Pro application's airfield diagrams for MMGL are outdated and do not match with the taxi information that ATC provides.

C172R pilot reported a near mid-air collision with a Cessna that departed in front of them while on short final approach.  Pilot went around and established lateral offset from the other aircraft; re-entered the pattern and landed normally.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a near miss with a helicopter during cruise flight.

Pilot reported a GPWS terrain warnings on final approach twice; with no published approaches but recent runway construction. After conducting terrain escape maneuvers twice; pilot landed after third approach.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they had control issues with certain flight controller modes during takeoff. Pilot overcame the issue and was able to safely continue the flight.

Technician reported that when using the maintenance application for electronic log book sign offs; it allows the use of 'Not Applicable' for entries; which is not allowed by the Engineering Authorization.
Air Carrier First Officer reported confusion regarding the taxi instructions from MMGL ground control and the taxi chart.

Air carrier flight crew reported a traffic conflict with ground vehicles after push back and before taxi from the gate. The vehicles suddenly moved in front of the aircraft as the parking brake was released to taxi and the aircraft remained stationary to avoid the conflict.
Center trainee reported they observed a MSAW Alert for an aircraft too low for radio coverage that was above their Minimum IFR Altitude but on the boundary of another facility.
Air carrier First Officer reported glide slope and takeoff configuration warning anomalies on arrival into MCO; citing possible 5G interference as the cause of the issues.

A TRACON Departure Controller reported a departing aircraft entered the subsequent sector without a handoff and into possible confliction. The reporter was unfamiliar with the newly designed RDVS and could not accomplish the required coordination in a timely manner.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid an aircraft with loss of power returning to field.
Pilot reported that charts for PGSN airport advise there are VASI lights; but there aren't any and there is no plan to install them.

B737 Captain reported a loss of control on ramp when plane rolled back into a ramp depression at the gate.  While attempting to pull further forward Captain noticed a ramp person with chocks under his window prior to it being safe to approach the aircraft.
Air taxi First Officer reported having communication problems with ANC Ground Control that may be due to interference from construction causing a radio dead zone on the ramp area.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC and had observed their solo student on short final with another aircraft also on short final abeam them.
Fractional jet Captain reported a GPS jamming event while on a RNAV arrival in domestic airspace. The Captain changed to heading mode and notified ATC; then the GPS jamming cleared before landing.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a light aircraft on descent into PVU.

Air carrier pilot reported they were assigned a gate at MBPV/PLS airport that was not listed on Jeppesen charts. Pilot also reported confusing taxi instructions along with a lack of ground support created multiple hazardous situations.
An air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming.
GA pilot reported a loss of communication with the ATC Center resulting in an ADS-B traffic alert.  Reporter cited possible COVID-19 related ATC staffing issues may have contributed to the event.
A Customer Service/Gate Agent reported yellow stow circles and other paint lines on the ramp have faded away which could result in an improper jet bridge positioning.
Air carrier Captain reported abruptly stopping the aircraft due to ground vehicle on service road failing to yield to the airplane.

Air carrier Captain reported descending too low on the approach and triggering a ground proximity warning. The glideslope at the airport was out of service and the flight crew missed that information in the NOTAMs.
Pilot reported a taxiway incursion at CRQ resulted from confusion of a blue box" with a 'blue line' to taxi to the customs ramp."
Flight Instructor reported when lined up on runway for takeoff; a NORDO aircraft overflew them and landed. Pattern traffic warned instructor to not takeoff.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported having a near miss with a flock of birds and flew above their authorized LAANC approval altitude to avoid a collision.
Air Carrier pilot taxiing to the ramp at SLC reported Tower taxi instructions did not correlate with the 10-9 chart depiction.
Air Carrier Captain reported temporary ADS-B EICAS advisory and GPS failure from suspected electronic jamming event.
Air Carrier Captain reported the runway hold short markings for BUF Runway 32 are faded on Taxiway A.
Air taxi Captain reported faded hold short markings and lack of warning lights resulted in a runway incursion.
Fractional jet Captain reported they experienced GPS jamming over Myanmar. All systems recovered to normal operations after leaving the jamming area.

A320 Captain reported the aircraft was about to descend below the JABBR crossing restriction on the ANTHM4 arrival to BWI until they intervened and stopped the descent. Captain indicated they had encountered this issue three separate times in different A320 aircraft.
Flight Instructor reported a confusing taxiway configuration near the approach end of Runway 31 at CVH airport needs better signage and as well as clarification on an airport diagram.
Pilot reported possible 5G interference on short final landing in IND.
EMB-145 First Officer reported receiving a GPWS during approach descent.

General aviation instructor pilot reported descending low over a densely populated area to remain below Class B airspace at night on a visual training flight. The flight descended below FAA minimum altitude for a congested area while lined up with the runway but far from the airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported difficulty understanding Center controllers and believes the problem maybe the controllers are wearing face masks.
Pilot reported overshooting an altitude on departure and attributed the event to lack of recent flying and poor use of HUD.
Two Jacksonville Center Controllers reported FAA Command Center would not comply with their request for traffic flow restrictions for known weather; causing operational challenges.
Light aircraft pilot reported confusing signage at DVT airport led to a taxi deviation down a vehicle roadway.

Recreational/Hobby UAS pilot reported flying at an uncontrolled airport; conducting a photo shoot of a manned aircraft. Prior to the flight; the Airport Manager told the pilot that UAS were not allowed at the airport but the reporter cited regulatory guidance to the contrary and conducted the operation as planned.
Air carrier Captain reported that during pushback the aircraft became disconnected from the tow bar; and was brought to a safe stop using the aircraft brakes.
SLC Tower Controller reported they issued instructions to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Safe Altitude.

Part 107 UAS pilot reported they conducted a photo mission near a building and were approached by a public official. The pilot learned of nearby non-charted UAS prohibited areas they may possibly have entered.
Air carrier Captain reported it is difficult to receive the DFW ATIS due to ORK airport ATIS being on the same frequency.

Air carrier First officer reported confusion regarding Hazmat documents provided to the flight crew. After further collaboration with Operations personnel it was determined that it was safe to continue to destination.

Air carrier Captain reported the EMZOH3 and OAKES2 arrivals into OAK airport result in aircraft going below a Class B shelf and at risk of violating the 200 knot airspeed restriction in that airspace.
Air Carrier flight crew reported there is no maximum tail height charted for Concourse A bridge at LGA.

Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming that was first noticed at the departure gate in LTFM; and they later regained functionality with one GPS but not the other. GPS abnormality continued throughout flight.

Air carrier pilot reported during initial climb an object passed across the windscreen and collided with the aircraft. The flight crew returned to land at departure airport and believe they struck a UAS while in flight.
A319 Flight Crew reported soon after takeoff the aircraft alerted a VENT SKIN VALVE FAULT. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a taxi deviation due to poor marking and signage on this taxiway in LAS airport.

BNA Tower Controller reported an aircraft stopped on the runway after confusing two unmarked access roads for possible taxiways causing another aircraft on final approach to go around. The Controller stated this is a common occurrence with these unmarked access roads.
General aviation pilot reported receiving an audible terrain warning during approach; resulting in a missed approach.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a bird strike during landing. Flight crew executed a missed approach and returned for a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported there are two L1 taxiway markings to the gate area in EWR.

Technician reported ambiguity in MEL process during trouble shooting of a thrust reverser unlocked indication. The MEL did not specifically prohibit dispatch with an unlocked indication present with the thrust reverser locked out.

Air carrier First Officer reported GPS jamming during cruise in an area known for jamming; but the GPS did not recover when exiting the area of GPS jamming. The flight coordinated with ATC for a clearance with the reduced navigational capability in oceanic airspace.

Air carrier flight crew reported the First Officer was hit with a laser strike during approach to EWR airport. The First Officer experienced strained vision; but was able to continue without further issue.
Pilot reported a diversion caused by additional NAV systems inoperative and not addressed in an MEL applied to a GPS system.
Part 107 pilot reported using meters instead of feet to determine altitude leading to a clearance violation.  The pilot recognized the error and descended to appropriate altitude.
C90 TRACON Controller reported there is a recurring equipment issue with the frequency 125.0 that is repeatedly reported about but nothing is done to fix the issue.

B737 Captain reported while taxiing out from ramp a baggage cart failed to stop resulting in a prompt stop to avoid the ground conflict.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a ground proximity warning while conducting a visual approach.

Air carrier Dispatch reported confusion with the MDE airport NOTAMs that were issued for a pothole located near the threshold of Runway 1.
Flight Attendant reported possible 5G interference with the aircraft from passengers not setting phones to aircraft mode as instructed.
Air carrier Captain reported it is unclear if the pavement west of Runway 8R at ELP is designated as a displaced threshold. There are no markings on the pavement or airport chart.

UAS flight crew member reported GPS interference errors with several UAS during the flight and chose to land all of the UAS.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported NOTAMS were not updating through the company's system.
Captain reported loss of GPS signal and ADS-B fault.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported the Dispatcher's Hazmat computer records were not retrievable until after flight departure.
RSW Tower Controller reported radio issues at the facility contributed to a conflict that resulted in one of the aircraft's executing a go-around.
Air carrier pilot flying reported experiencing potential GPS jamming at cruise altitude over Kentucky and momentarily lost GPS signal.

B787 flight crew reported a GPS jamming and/or spoofing event that resulted in the loss of some communications; GPS malfunction and failure; and inaccurate clock times. Additionally; there were false EPGWS warnings that had to be disabled during final approach.

Flight Attendant reported the left wing came into contact with a building during push back from the gate.

Cessna 210 pilot reported they were already deviating for obstructions when ATC relayed instructions through another aircraft to avoid the obstructions. The reporter stated this area has poor radio reception which 'could have led to a disaster.'

Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming over the Black Sea.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a terrain alert during approach. Flight crew corrected course and continued to landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported a bird strike during approach which resulted in a strong odor throughout the aircraft. The flight crew completed a safe landing with the ground personnel providing appropriate support.

Air carrier Captain reported a runway incursion resulted from incorrect NOTAM; incorrect runway markings and lighting.
ZDC Center Controller reported an aircraft resumed clearance to PAAZZ after deviating for weather but ATC had cleared them to PAACK. The reporter stated the similar named fixes caused the confusion.
Ground employee reported a ground conflict between aircraft and ramp vehicles. The reporter stated this is a common occurrence at this location in the airport ramp.
Air carrier Captain reported the Jeppesen FD-PRO is missing the engine out procedure due to terrain for ITO airport.
EUG TRACON Controller reported vectoring an aircraft below an unlabeled MVA. Controller stated that display MVA labeling should be improved.
Controller reported military aircraft flew below the Minimum IFR altitude after permission for special activity was denied.
Air carrier Captain reported an inability to verify a taxi clearance due to ATC's use of a taxiway designation not charted on the airport diagram.

B737-900 Flight Crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into SEA.
ZSU Controller reported chronic transceiver malfunctions resulted in serious unsafe air traffic situations.

First Officer reported a loss of GPS signal to both GPS receivers at cruise in a known area of GPS jamming. The crew was unable to recover the GPS signal for the remainder of the flight and continued to destination.
Check Pilot reported after landing on Runway 26R in Atlanta the crew had difficulty finding Taxiway E37 because of faded paint.

Air Carrier Maintenance Inspector reported while performing a borescope inspection the number two engine; found a one-inch crack on the convex side of the stage two high pressure turbine nozzle guide vane. The engineer recommended grounding the aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported calling in fatigued due to several maintenance related delays and company scheduling techniques that push the limits of legal rest periods.
B777 First Officer reported battery powered wheelchair documents contained errors and also missing required Hazmat labels.
A321 Flight Attendant stated the aircraft has been reconfigured; but the required reference documentation had not been updated.

Cirrus SF50 jet pilot reported multiple warning messages after takeoff during the initial climb which resulted in the pilot returning to the departure airport and landed. The pilot discovered the pitot tube cover was not properly removed before flight.

Flight crew reported their aircraft turned the wrong direction when intercepting final. ATC advised the reporters that three other aircraft did the same thing leading the pilots to think it might be 5G interference.
B777 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL320 in trail of a B747.

Air carrier flight crew reported during push back from gate the ground crew prevented a ground conflict with another aircraft that was pushing back behind them by proactively pulling them back into the gate.
Flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning on the RDG RNAV (GPS) Runway 31 approach for nearby towers even though they flew the approach accurately.
HLN Tower Controller reported a NMAC between two opposite direction aircraft.
C680 First Officer reported while conducting a Visual Approach they turned to avoid a tower and received a low altitude warning from ATC and a TCAS/RA for traffic climbing off an adjacent airport.

Tug Driver reported another tug collided into the reporter's vehicle and continued driving away; despite the reporter stopping to allow the other vehicle to pass. The reporter stated the path is too narrow for two tugs to pass and has insufficient lighting.
Air carrier Captain reported difficulty seeing the spot locations at CLT due to a lack of lighting at night and expressed concern about minimal clearance between aircraft on designated taxi lines.
Pilot reported waypoints dropped out of the aircraft's Honeywell FMS. The reporter suggested upgrade from current software due to 'known Issues' with the current software.
EMB-145 flight crew reported responded to EGPWS on a night RNAV backed visual approach. Crew reported ambiguity with company page and procedure.
B767 First Officer reported operating an aircraft in revenue service with the incorrect fuel management panel installed. The aircraft was later removed from service and the correct panel installed.
Air carrier Captain reported failure of GPS and ADSB inbound to DEN.

Air carrier flight crew reported they lost their GPS signal in Korean airspace due to GPS jamming; and the GPS system never returned to normal; even after leaving the area where jamming was occurring. Loss of GPS reportedly led to a minor course deviation at destination.
Pilot reported inadequate radio reception below 8000 ft. while flying through ZBW airspace on airway T216.
A319 Captain reported First Officer descended below glidepath resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
Helicopter pilot reported a bird strike that resulted in a forced off field landing.
An A11 TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft not communicating with ATC flew into conflict with two separate aircraft conducting instrument approaches. One of the conflicts was a NMAC.

ZMA Oceanic Controller reported radar and communication equipment failures led to a loss of non-radar separation between multiple aircraft. The reporter noted this is a recurring issue and needs to be repaired without delay.

ZAB Center Controller reported transmitter sites routinely fail and the equipment issues are not being addressed appropriately creating unsafe situations. An additional site is needed to ensure ATC communications.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported the company manuals do not state the specific dry ice limitations for the B787 model aircraft.
Pilot reported landing without use of PAPI lighting while landing at PTKK airport.  PAPI's were out of service but a NOTAM had been not issued.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a conflict between an IFR arrival and a high speed highly maneuverable aircraft just clear of the PSP Class Delta/TRSA.

Air carrier First Officer reported a near midair collision with a silver object; possibly a weather balloon; while they were on descent.
Medevac pilot reported they flew off course on their departure and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC as they were below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A321 flight crew reported inexperienced ground personnel could not disconnect the towbar from the nose gear. During the final attempt the aircraft disconnected from the towbar and rolled onto the towbar.

SR22 pilot reported smoke in the cabin due to a failed PFD panel.
Pilot reported sun in eyes interfered with seeing the altimeter; resulting in a descent towards the ground at low altitude.
TRACON Controller reported their trainee issued a descent clearance to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Attendant reported concern after a passenger stated he and his wife had recently recovered from COVID-19.

General aviation pilot reported the DYO NDB on the RUT ODP is incorrectly listed as a fly-by waypoint instead of a flyover waypoint; and could cause the GPS to direct the aircraft into the path of mountainous terrain.

Gulfstream G650 pilot reported a false localizer capture resulted in a course deviation and a terrain warning from ATC.
C152 instructor pilot reported their feet became entangled in their headset cord while taxiing and they could not stop the aircraft at the hold short line they were supposed to hold short of.

ZJX Center Controller reported insufficient ATC staffing and lack of sector flow management initiatives despite requests from Controllers resulting in sectors overloaded with traffic; frequency congestion; and Controllers refusing to take handoffs. Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an amended clearance due to a ATC Center going ATC Zero and questioned if they had violated an FAR by flying in uncontrolled airspace.

Air carrier flight crew reported possibly flying through a thin layer of volcanic ash during cruise. The crew maintained the flight plan buffer distances and submitted a PIREP.

A Center Controller reported two VFR military aircraft from Eglin AFB entered a holding pattern at non VFR altitudes (14;000 and 15;000 feet) adjacent to R2915 and in conflict with the major traffic flows through the sectors. A321 First Officer reported uncommanded aircraft movement due to towbar breakaway during pushback resulted in loss of aircraft control and damage to nose gear and towbar.

Student Pilot with an Instructor reported colliding with a Mylar party balloon on final approach. Minor damage resulted. A few scratches and a small dent near the tail section of the aircraft. There was no damage done to the control functions.

E145 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on short final.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a military helicopter while entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. Air carrier flight crew reported autoflight anomalies in cruise flight in the vicinity of MEI VORTAC; citing 5G interference as possibly contributing.  Reporter noted that two other air carrier flights in the same area were experiencing similar issues.

Air carrier Captain reported the IAD Ramp area; multiple taxiway lines; and the lead-in line and gate centerline for multiple gates are obscured and difficult to see when it is wet and/or during nighttime conditions.

Air carrier pilot reported the ramp crews are not compliant with new push back procedures at many different airports.

TRACON Controller reported during that OJT an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

MIA TRACON Controller reported a potential loss of separation between two aircraft at a hotspot for VFR traffic near the OPF airport.

B737-800 flight crew reported of being informed several days later of failure to report aircraft damage that was obscured on preflight.
