Challenger 650 pilot reported a runway excursion due to crosswind created by a passing train.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a bird strike on departure which resulted in engine damage and a return to departure airport.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported they were notified that HAZMAT they scanned into the computer system did not get properly processed in the system.
GA pilot on short final to MTJ; a non towered airport; reported an NMAC with an air carrier on short final for an intersecting runway not communicating on CTAF.
Small jet flight crew reported loss of directional control during taxi in snowy and icy conditions at BED that resulted in an excursion off the taxiway.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported the stop marks and VSR are faded and difficult to see in rainy and night time conditions.
Air carrier First Officer reported they observed jet blast blowing dust and debris onto construction workers and their equipment across from Concourse A on Taxiway AS at DEN.
ORD Tower Controller reported aircraft data tags frequently drop off their display while aircraft taxi across an area of the airport.
CE-680 Captain reported experiencing GPS anomalies apparently related to solar activity.
Pilot reported an NMAC during approach to BLM airport.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a terrain alert during missed approach.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft wingtip came into contact with a blast fence during pushback at LGA even though the pushback was accomplished correctly to the right spot.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of GPS signal while in descent to Denver airport.
AT6 pilot conducting aerial application reported a NMAC with another aerial spraying aircraft requiring them to take evasive action which resulted in a possible encroachment into Class C airspace.
PA 28R instructor pilot and student reported a landing gear malfunction which resulted in an airspace incursion. The pilot exited the airspace; then landed safely.
Pilot reported a navigational problem with the Honeywell Epic system. The pilot states ATC and Honeywell are aware of the navigational issue.

UAS Visual Observer reported that after liftoff the UAS began climbing rapidly and was at risk of exceeding 400 ft. AGL. The UAS was landed and it was learned the autonomous flight plan was created using meters instead of feet.
Air carrier pilot reported the taxi diagram at MSLP is not correct.
Pilot reported an observed discrepancy between the depiction of Taxiways A1 and T from Runway 3L at PRC and the actual taxiway location.

A BNA TRACON Controller reported they had to vector an arriving aircraft away from a conflicting VFR aircraft not in contact with ATC. The reporter states this is an ongoing problem and the '...VFR traffic around BNA has become unsafe and too much for safe passenger travel.'
Air carrier Captain reported loss of ADS-B 1 and 2 on departure out of DEN. Reporter cited 5G as possibly contributing; and the systems returned to normal a few minutes later.
Flight crew reported receiving a 'caution terrain' aural warning message due to an antenna near the FAF to ORF airport.
Air carrier captain reported the ground equipment safety lines at a gate are missing or need repainting.
Twin Engine Pilot reported the BKL taxi chart does not show taxiway G as part of the ramp area. This caused confusion and a possible runway incursion.
Pilot reported a terrain warning during visual approach into ROA Airport.
Pilot reported the airport diagrams for ANI airport is missing Taxiway G and Taxiway TA.
Houston Center Controller reported unsafe practices regarding Management and weather.
Air carrier Captain on approach reported low altitude alert from ATC due to glideslope anomaly.  Flight crew corrected and landed normally.
B737 MAX 9 Captain reported the shear pin broke during pushback and they failed to have maintenance inspect the aircraft as required.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a foreign air carrier does not include crew rest time during flights as flight time and is not complying with FAR's regarding crew rest after long overseas flights.
Eyewitness Pilot reported observing a critical ground conflict during taxi when an aircraft taxiied onto a runway while another aircraft was on takeoff roll at ORD airport.
TRACON Controller reported numerous issues while working traffic and problems associated with the workload and staffing.

Air carrier Captain reported after conclusion of spoofing airspace procedures; the flight crew was unable to recover both GPS units; had ADS-B In problems; no SATCOM voice; and also eventually lost CPDLC capability.
A student pilot reported a NMAC in the VFR pattern at a non-towered airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPWS terrain warning on approach believed to be a system malfunction.
Pilot reported accidentally taxiing onto an unmarked service road on a large paved area at SYR after mistaking it for a taxiway.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a Terrain Proximity Alert on final approach.
Pilot reported loss of control while conducting an ILS approach to a non towered airport.  The reporter believes the event was caused by a vehicle driving by the ILS critical area.

TRACON and Tower Controllers reported traffic landed on closed parallel runway at night; without a clearance. Controllers reported that the lit X closure indicator was not in place and it was difficult for tower to determine aircraft runway alignment at night.
A Part 107 pilot reported accidentally flying their UAS into small tree branches causing it to fall into a lake.

Air carrier Captain reported not performing the Before Taxi Checklist and subsequently had not set the flaps to the takeoff setting prior to moving the aircraft.  Reporter cited distraction from dealing with face mask issues in the cabin area may have contributed to the event.
Air Carrier Captain reported responding to an RA during approach and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.

B737 First Officer reported after pulling to the gate a Ramper alerted the flight crew that a ground power receptacle was on fire; which made part of the aircraft catch on fire as well. The flight crew requested assistance from Ramp Control and told the flight attendants to get the passengers off through the jetway.
TRACON Controller reported having to repeat transmissions to pilots because they could not understand the instructions; due to reporter wearing a face mask.

B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft after the previous Captain had also refused the aircraft. The aircraft had a deferred air conditioning pack and required repair. Ground temperatures were very high.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain chose not to wear a mask for safety of flight concerns while wearing glasses.
Air carrier Captain reported not making a crossing restriction after realizing they had overflown the top of descent.
B737 First Officer reported during final approach they were instructed by ATC to go-around due to traffic not clearing the runway quickly enough.

A Center Controller reported a conflict between an air carrier departure and a satellite airport skydiving operator who was conducting jumps in the vicinity of charted departure and arrival procedures. The reporter stated they anticipate the same situation will become a recurring problem.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in international airspace at cruise altitude. The Captain indicated the GPS jamming lasted for approximately 20 minutes.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC during arrival with what was possibly a UAS. No evasive action was taken.
EWR Tower Controller reported solar glare approximately 1.4 miles S/SE of EWR Tower.
Pilot reported inadvertently using a paved area for taxi rather than the correct taxiway due to a lack of clear markings at a non-towered airport.
Pilot reported reoccurring difficulties in communicating with ZMP ARTCC via multiple frequencies in the vicinity of MBL airport.
HA420 flight crew reported performing a go-around due to traffic conflict and upon being in the traffic pattern for another approach; may have violated Class C airspace.
Air carrier First Officer reported there are two possible boundary lines for the pad area at ORD airport and the chart does not specify which one to use.
EMB-175 Captain reported confusion during taxi into the gate because of multiple lead in lines and marshaller standing by while the crew used Safedock.

Air carrier Captain reported during arrival on PHX BRUSR 1 STAR they were given a confusing clearance for a visual approach for Runway 8. Flight crew was using RNAV Runway 8 approach which reportedly does not share a common fix with BRUSR 1 STAR to provide continuity; but the ILS Runway approach 8 does. The Captain stated the approaches are so similar; the inbound fixes should be the same.
Pilot taxiing from FBO at LUK mistakenly entered Runway 21. The reporter cited confusing signage as one of the factors.
General aviation pilot preparing for departure at CLS reported communication with Seattle Approach was extremely poor on the ground and remained poor even after departure.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported anomaly with FMS equipment possibly due to 5G interference during climbout.
Reporter states that due to congestion on the hangar floor; a larger man lift was used to conduct maintenance and resulted in damage to the aircraft.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an RA event and blocked ATC communications due to possible COVID-19 ATC staffing issues.
B737 MAX First Officer reported the maintenance procedure to manage electric hydraulic pumps is not included in pilot manuals; resulting in non standard brake accumulator pressure variance.
D01 TRACON Controller reported a busy session with another sector controller that led to communication issues and a TCAS RA/NMAC.
Tower Controller reported low staffing levels during periods of complex traffic combined with undue outside pressures have created an unsafe situation.
An air carrier pilot descending into MCO reported they deviated from their assigned clearance due to navigating to NICKI intersection instead of MICKI Intersection.

Technician reported an aircraft returned to the gate after flight control checks revealed both Out Board Ailerons were not functional. Maintenance reset circuit breakers and the plane blocked back out.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a flock of birds circled the UAS during its descent. One of the birds struck and cracked a single prop. The UAS landed without further damage.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS interference and the GPS began to malfunction in cruise; and ATC advised the flight crew of GPS interference. The GPS returned later in the flight and RNP was back to normal.
Air taxi Captain reported fixes on the approach were not loaded and they descended below published altitudes resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.

Pilot reported routinely losing communications with the NCT sector south of the KLIDE intersection in flight. After regaining contact; a conversation with the Controller revealed this is a known problem in the area.
Pilot reported safety concerns landing at night on Runway 30 at CVN due to a powerline and tall fence near the approach end which does not have VASI equpment.
Pilot reported discrepancies between a NOTAM and the 10-7 page showing the opening time of the airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported losing both GPS signals shortly before the top of descent into ABQ and did not regain GPS signal until final approach.
Flight crew reported being confused by the poor taxiway signage at DAL Airport and joined the wrong taxiway while taxiing to the runway.
A Center Controller reported Iowa City VOR and Ottawa Canada VOR identifier is IOW which resulted in a navigation error for an aircraft.
B737 Captain reported an upset climbing through FL364 after encountering wake turbulence from a B777 at FL370.
GA pilot reported a taxiway incursion at TYR airport attributed to no hold lines or taxiway signs as depicted in the AIM or airport diagrams. Pilot also had communication problems with ATC.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a missing engine out procedure for RWY 27 BOS. Pilot reports the procedure was in charts previously and matched the APS chart for BOS.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft turned the wrong direction and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Captain of a training flight reported lights near HEF Airport caused confusion during an IFR approach.
Maintenance technicians reported that while they were working on an aircraft with a flap issue the company management was not supportive of AMT's working to find root cause of the problem.
A330 Captain reported concerns over loss of crew coordination and situational awareness during a rejected take off event.
Captain reported a near miss with a ground vehicle on the runway; while on landing rollout in reduced visibility conditions at a non-towered airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a recent change in altitude crossing restriction on the LAX 11-7 page; ILS 25L requires a steep descent between waypoints that is impossible to accomplish. Additionally; this causes multiple steep descent" messages to generate after loading the approach into the FMC."

Tower Controllers and a flight school instructor reported the instructor initiated a go around from short final due to another flight school aircraft being on the runway. The Controllers reported a mix up in communications; their inability to see the runway involved; and no ground radar contributed to their lack of awareness of an aircraft on the runway.
Part 107 UAS crew reported completing a mission with the UAS.
E175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into SEA in trail of a B737.
CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported an engine oil malfunction during cruise flight. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed and a hydraulic leak was discovered as well.
B737 MAX Flight Attendant reported confusion over seat pocket safety cards being the correct type or not for the aircraft.

Air carrier flight crew reported confusion at a taxiway intersection based upon a mismatch between what was visually seen and the chart depiction; exacerbated by a lack of signage. Ground Control helped provide assistance and the flight continued taxiing to the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported a large bump on the runway during the takeoff roll causing a jolt and startle effect. The airport NOTAMs did not identify this specific runway surface issue.

GA pilot reported they lost communications with ATC while on approach to VPZ in SBN TRACON airspace. The reporter was issued a new frequency to re-establish communications and was advised by ATC that communications loss in this area is a known problem.
Captain reported receiving a terrain alert during climb out. Flight crew continued climb via the departure.
Air carrier First Officer reported engine control malfunction inflight.
Low wing aircraft Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid traffic resulted in a NMAC event.

TPA TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft was crossing the localizer while an IFR aircraft was on the localizer and had the IFR aircraft maintain altitude to avoid an NMAC. The reporter noted that VFR aircraft routinely traverse the localizer at the glide path altitude for the Runway 18 localizer at PIE Airport.

HPN Local Controller reported RA response with non communicating traffic resulted in an low altitude alert and CFTT event. Controller also reported the traffic volume exceeds requirements for Class C which will prevent these incidents.
Air Carrier Captain taxiing for departure from PHL reported confusing taxiway signage.
Pilot reported issues related to flying into PTKK Chuuk International Airport; Weno Island; Micronesia; at night.

Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway excursion while taxiing for departure and returned to gate for aircraft check. Flight crew stated the taxiway markings do not allow for adequate room to maneuver and recommended a taxi line be moved to provide more clearance.
Air carrier Captain reported of being fatigued by company schedule.
Air Traffic Controller reported the interior working area in the BHM Control Tower has a configuration that blocks the Ground Controller's view of the airport movement areas.

Air carrier Captain reported a taxi chart discrepancy at LAX identifying a specific intersection on the taxi chart in the EFB. The performance data identified the intersection but the Captain indicated the taxiway intersection was not identified on the charts.
Ramp personnel reported during pushback the aircraft rolled back and then forward over the towbar. Aircraft was towed back to gate for Maintenance inspection for possible damage.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming and spoofing during cruise in a known area or reported GPS interference. The GPS signal was restored after transitioning the area and the flight continued to destination.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a taxiway deviation. The FO stated the chart listed a routing different than as assigned by ATC and suggested a notation for clarity.
ABQ TRACON Controller reported they did not detect an aircraft read back the wrong altitude assignment resulting in the aircraft descending below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A flight instructor conducting an instrument approach to MQY airport reported flight school aircraft from an adjacent airport flew into potential conflict with their approach course. The reporter had an NMAC with one of the aircraft requiring them to take evasive action.

Air carrier flight crew reported an inadvertent runway incursion was the result of confusion regarding the hold short lines on Taxiway A for Runway 7 at Montego Bay/Sangster International Airport. The lines had been painted over with black paint and are more prominent than the current hold lines.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported the ASOS at TIST is malfunctioning and the airport does not have a legal weather reporting system.
Air carrier pilot reported ORD ILS 09C glideslope oscillated when a taxiing aircraft crossed the runway at Taxiway Z resulting in them flying below the glidepath.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft went NORDO and flew into a higher MVA.
Medium Transport Crew reported conflicting departure instructions issued by ATC verses the MDW3 SID narrative; resulted in a deviation.
A TRACON Controller reported a NMAC between an aircraft on an approach and an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.
Flight Crew reported an air turn back after birds were ingested by the right engine on takeoff.

B737 Captain reported stopping the aircraft during a taxi due to uncertainty about wing clearance from a helicopter's moving rotor blades. Clarification was requested with Ground Control; but ATC was also unsure. The helicopter pilot then used the rotor brake to stop the rotors and it was then that the reporter determined clearance to be good enough to continue the taxi.
Corporate jet Captain reported there is an altitude discrepancy on the STAR FROGZ3 arrival into MIA. The Captain stated the chart is also confusing with too many overlapping courses.
B737-800 pilot reported an aircraft refusal during flight planning when assigned to fly a ferry flight to reposition the aircraft with a known engine defect.
Air carrier Captain reported the taxiway lines are faded and confusing from taxiway N up to and including taxiway LL at ORD Airport.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported one of the assigned aircraft reported a NMAC and the pilot crew would submit the necessary reports.
Center Controller reported descending an aircraft into another Controller's airspace; thinking the D-Side had pointed the aircraft out.

Flight Attendant crew reported a Self Balancing (Hover Board) device in the overhead during boarding. The device is not permitted per company policy and the passenger chose to deplane with the device.

Air carrier Ramp Agent reported being unable to close out and contacted the Load Planner for assistance; who was able to close the flight out. The flight then returned to the gate and the DG had to be moved to a different location.
Eugene TRACON Controller reported an unsafe operation with numerous VFR aircraft at the CVO uncontrolled airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft while intercepting the final approach course. Captain stated no evasive action was needed.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the Jeppesen approach chart for DEN RNP Runway 26 has an unusual hard speed requirement at the Initial Approach Fix which causes operational issues.
An air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming.
AFW Tower Controller reported an unsafe event where a VFR aircraft is transitioning an arrival path for air carriers outside of Class Delta causing an airborne conflict.
Ramp workers reported they used the wrong tow bar for a B737 MAX 9 aircraft and when they removed it the aircraft rolled because the brakes were not set.
First Officer reported a taxiway excursion resulted after following the appropriate yellow taxi line while making a turn from B to A taxiways at GKY airport.
Flight crew reported during taxi they observed a service vehicle driving fast and coming towards them. They had to apply brakes to avoid a collision.

Corporate Captain reported the poor; faded runway markings at PDK Airport make landing at Runway 21L difficult. Wet and low light conditions at the airport also add to the difficulty during takeoffs and landings.
Air Carrier Captain reported the temporary lighting setup at JAN airport in the gate area is extremely bright and a safety hazard.

A RAL Tower Controller reported Runway 16/34 south end is not visible from the Tower so the FAA prohibits the use of this runway resulting in aircraft having to land in very strong crosswinds when the wind is from the east. Previously a camera was installed to view this area but is out of service and FAA has not repaired the camera.
Corporate Captain reported hitting a bird and then having to deviate.
Falcon 7X flight crew reported observing B737 over runway hold line resulted in rejected takeoff.
A321 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into PHX in trail of an MD-11 that resulted in a 45-degree bank.
Captain reported missing signage for Taxiway P at AUS airport contributed to a taxiway deviation.
B737 pilot reported a NMAC during approach resulted in a TCAS RA event.
Air carrier pilot reported that during taxi; they had to quickly stop aircraft due to vehicles not yielding to the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported during taxi observing another aircraft taxing towards them resulting in the Captain taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.

YNG Tower Controller reported an aircraft entered Class D airspace without contacting ATC and landed at Griffin/Sloas (800H) airport which is uncharted. Controller recommended the private airport be added to VFR Sectional Chart to raise awareness of required communication with ATC.
A ZID Center Controller reported a loss of separation between two aircraft when one of the aircraft cleared direct KENLL mistakenly programmed the fix KENLN into their navigation.
ZAB Controller reported frequent radio equipment failures with limited coverage and failure of MSAW feature resulted in an aircraft entering a higher MVA at too low of an altitude.
Ground personnel reported the taxi lanes at LGA are difficult to see in inclement weather.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control following pushback and tug disconnect.
Air carrier Captain reported having to abruptly brake to avoid collision with a vehicle who failed to yield to the aircraft.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the assigned taxiway Alpha was not on the EFB Jeppesen 10-9 airport chart.

Air carrier Captain reported ATC did not provide guidance on how close aircraft can be and slightly prolonged the approach and landing to avoid multiple aircraft in the area. The reporter also noted that the RWSL appears to have been disabled for Runway 13 at LGA.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming.
Air carrier Captain reported the newly opened taxiway at MDST Airport is not charted on the Jeppesen charts or noted on the 10-7 pages.

Small transport aircraft pilot with a trainee reported disorientation and confusion during taxi at SMO. The reporter stated the lighting and taxiway markings are poor in this area of the airport and they taxied onto an unmarked ramp at night.

B-737-900 Flight Attendant crew reported a communications breakdown between Flight Attendant crew and Customer Service Agent; Captain; Company SOP's/ Manuals regarding the air carrier's policy of transporting Lithium-Ion powered Hoverboards in passenger cabin.
Front Line ATC Manager reported an airborne conflict and cited staffing levels as a contributing factor.
Air carrier pilot monitoring reported the MRLB/LIR PAPIs were not illuminated and was informed by ATC that they have not worked for a decade.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on an approach and an unidentified VFR aircraft had a NMAC when the VFR aircraft climbed towards the aircraft on approach outside of the Class B airspace.
Air carrier pilot reported problems with the B737-800 weather radar not working correctly on numerous aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported a near collision with a van on the ramp at CLT airport while turning into the parking gate. The Captain indicated the van was speeding and he had to brake hard to avoid a collision; then continued to the gate.
Captain reported the pilot controlled lighting system malfunctioned at a FOK. Reporter stated they advised ATC but no NOTAM has been issued.
Air carrier First Officer reported a possible conflict and loss of GPS on approach to DEN.
TEB Controllers reported poor runway markings almost led to a collision on the ground with a taxiing aircraft and a departing aircraft.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported certain 737 MAX aircraft may have programming errors that cause the aircraft to change from the programmed descent speed to the 240 knots / 10;000 feet restriction too early.
Pilot reported OBE; X21 and X06 airports are assigned the same CTAF frequency which results in frequency saturation; interference; and general communication problems.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into PHX in trail of an A320 that resulted in the temporary loss of LNAV.
Pilot reported that during taxi-out; the morning sun interfered with being able to see and they inadvertently taxied onto a runway at MYF airport.
A319 Captain reported the need to apply heavy braking to avoid a collision with another aircraft that had been taxiing too quickly and without regard to other traffic.

Maintenance Technician reported misapplication of an MEL was made for an ELEC light that remained on during revenue service. It was discovered that the standby Inverter had failed; causing the ELEC light to remain on instead of going out.
General Aviation pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from a military transport in the vicinity of SUU airport.

SFO Controllers reported the building's windows fogged up so the outside became obscured and the ground radar also malfunctioned; and with no traffic management program; the reporters felt it was unsafe to be working traffic at normal speeds.

Air carrier Captain reported a track deviation during arrival to ORD after turning left at CLUSO Intersection rather than KAYTO Intersection as cleared. Captain stated CLUSO and KAYTO can be similarly pronounced and therefore confused by crews.
Air carrier First Officer reported service truck crossed in front of aircraft at a high speed; resulted in Captain forced to brake hard to avoid a collision.
Air carrier pilot monitoring reported GPS jamming in an area west of ELP but all systems returned to normal after flying out of that area.
B737-900 Captain reported a ground conflict during taxiout and stated low visibility winter conditions contributed to the event.
Air carrier Captain reported the dark lighting conditions; wet surface; and dull paint markings at ORD made following the taxiway centerline difficult.
Air carrier pilot reported a new engine out procedure from SBGR is not charted in Jeppessen PRO software.
Air carrier First Officer reported proceeding to the wrong fix; caused by a miscommunication that occurred due to the fix names sounding similar to one another (RADDS/RAPZZ).
An air carrier flight crew conducting a Visual Approach reported they received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
B787 flight crew reported roll and yaw controlability issues and elected to jettison fuel and perform an air turn back.

A ZJX Controller reported they vectored and descended an aircraft in an attempt to avoid a conflict but the heading and descent placed the aircraft into conflict with a third aircraft. The reporter was working a sector containing more aircraft than guidelines deem operationally safe.

Air carrier First Officer reported inadequate lighting in the vicinity of taxiway A and T2 at FSD airport; resulted in difficulty seeing taxiway markings creating unsafe taxi conditions in the ramp area.

Air carrier Captain reported a sharp acrid odor during cruise. After conferring with Dispatch the odor was determined to be caused by a volcanic ash event which was remedied by climbing to a higher altitude.
Captain reported being advised that a passenger told the Flight Attendant they may have been exposed to COVID-19.

Air carrier pilot reported during landing at BJX airport they had to avoid a makeshift barrier on the runway. Reportedly; there was no information on the ATIS nor was there a NOTAM advising of the barrier.
General aviation pilot reported a traffic conflict during taxi with another aircraft and highlighted the lack of clearly marked taxiway yellow lines as a contributing factor in the taxi incursion.
Ramp agents reported during pushback another aircraft's jet blast pushed one of the ramp agents towards the pushback aircraft's running engine.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft's nose may have potentially crossed over the hold-short line after taking the high speed Taxiway V at LAX Airport. The reporter noted only Taxiway Y has a warning on the chart; and suggested there should be a warning applied to all taxiways exiting Runway 24R.
Air carrier Captain reported possible jamming or interference resulted in GPS failures and reliance on INS; inertial navigation.
Air Carrier pilot reported the LAS FRNCK1 SID does not have an Engine Out Procedure.
TBM 940 pilot reported two separate incidents in which the G3000 autopilot initiated an uncontrolled pitch up attitude resulting in an unsafe speed reduction during the initial climb phase.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in the HECC FIR through OJAC FIR that continued even after landing at OJAI Airport.

SCT TRACON Controller reported re-occurring airborne conflicts between VFR aircraft executing aerobatic maneuvers and IFR arrivals into PSP.  Reporter advised they deal with these types of situations every day; which could be easily avoided by having non-voluntary protected airspace.

Air carrier Captain reported confusion with the setup of the special engine-out approach at SKRG; as there is some ambiguity that may lead to the use of the wrong VOR frequency and misidentification of the critical DME point.
Air carrier Captain reported no NOTAM was issued for runway painting and closing at MLI airport. ATC stated to the pilot that MLI does not issue NOTAMs for runway painting and closing.
Air carrier captain reported a new taxiway; B; is not depicted on the airport chart for MMGL; Guadalajara; Mexico.

SAT TRACON Controller reported an unidentified aircraft departed 5C1 airport underneath the SAT ILS final approach course and climbed into confliction with an Air Carrier. The Controller climbed and vectored the Air Carrier to avoid the traffic but the Air Carrier responded to a TCAS/RA and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Two air carrier pilots on break reported noticing the effects of possible wake turbulence on their trans-Pacific flight.

Pilot reported an unexpected track heading deviation during departure from APC airport while on the NAAPA4 SID.  Reporter stated the SID was properly loaded in the FMS; yet the aircraft turned the wrong heading.
Air carrier Captain reported the constant construction and poor signage at WSSS/SIN Airport led to the flight crew passing the point where ATC wanted the aircraft to stop.
ZMA Center Controller reported repeated radar and communication site outages in the Oceanic Area create unsafe situations. The reporter states there are no backup systems to the main sites.

Air carrier Captain reported a frequent runway icing hazard on LGA's runway 04-22 that is not depicted in charts or NOTAMs. The Captain states that due to the unique construction of the elevated runway over water; a section of the runway freezes earlier than the rest creating a reduced braking action hazard.

Air carrier technician reported during scheduled maintenance they discovered an oil leak on #1 engine in the turbine rear frame area. The oil leak was caused by an oil inlet cover bolts that backed out due to not being safety wired per AMM.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion over NOTAM clarity and an ATC clearance resulted in an incursion to a closed taxiway at HNL.
Air carrier Captain reported using Taxiway RB instead of the cleared Taxiway RA due to the concern that the wingtip clearance via Taxiway RA for widebody aircraft may be insufficient.
An Air Carrier pilot reported the auto park system failed while parking. When they initiated parking again with ramp agents the aircraft rolled backwards one to two feet.
Ramp Agent reported a malfunction of the push back tug capture system resulted in damage to the aircraft.
Air taxi flight crew reported the hold-short and runway clearance markings are close to each other on Taxiway Echo at HNL and they had crossed the hold-short lines without realizing it.
An air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming affect of aircraft systems resulted in multiple navigation system malfunctions.
Air carrier maintenance store personnel reported an ongoing Hazmat violation due to the lack of proper storage cabinet during Hazmat handling.
B737 pilot conducting pre-flight reported crew oxygen system not working. Maintenance discovered oxygen bottle valve closed.
A319 First Officer reported being alerted by TCAS and noticed VFR traffic in the approach corridor.
An Air Carrier pilot reported the Tower hours of operation for STT are not accurate in the Jeppesen Chart and company pages.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming during cruise which resulted in the failure of RNP requirement. ATC was advised and flight continued to the destination.

Pilot reported being instructed to communicate with DAB tower through the frequency 118.1 instead of the published frequency 120.7 and that it is common local knowledge to routinely use the unpublished 118.1 frequency.

General aviation pilot reported not being able to see the runway numbers at PMP while on final approach and inadvertently overflew the runway and became disoriented. After safely landing; the reporter confirmed with a line person that there have been issues with the runway numbers which would be repainted soon.
B737 Flight Crew reported two large birds hit the nose cone during departure; resulting in an airspeed disagree message. The Flight Crew completed an air turn back to landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported a banging sound was heard during initial descent and during the subsequent aircraft pitch changes that followed. Upon arrival; Maintenance found a cargo pallet had shifted from one zone to another.
Air Carrier First Officer reported issues at MYNN with taxiway signage and faded hold short lines; resulting in confusion about the aircraft's position at a runway.
Chicago Center Controller reported an aircraft deviated from direct MOBIL clearance because they entered the fix MOBLE in their FMS which is also a fix in the NAS.
B777 flight crew reported a rejected take-off due to inoperative airspeed indications. Post flight inspection found blockage of pitot tubes.
Air carrier Captain reported having to divert due to fuel issues related to ATC Zero Center closure and re-routes.
B777 flight crew reported encountering severe wake turbulence 20 miles in trail of another B777 on their trans-Pacific flight.

CRJ Captain reported a CFIT event during an unstable approach which was followed by a safe approach and landing. The Captain added that the approach for aircraft type should be modified for the specific model.
First Officer reported charting error on page 10-9 on the Airport Diagram.

A320 Flight Crew reported a bird strike during takeoff resulting in a crack on the First Officer's lower front edge window.  The flight crew informed ATC and Dispatch of the situation and decided to return to the departure airport.

Air taxi Captain reported crossing a LAHSO line at HPN after following the yellow taxi lines and having to perform a tight turn. The reporter's larger aircraft did not have the turn radius to properly complete this turn.
Air taxi Captain reported lack of clarity and EFB display of airport taxiway restrictions resulted in a taxiway incursion.  Captain also reported the need for clarification of taxiway restrictions.
B767-300 crew reported a bird strike prior to V1; causing high vibration readings on engine. Crew returned to departure airport.

ZNY ARTCC Controllers reported the wrong aircraft was given a climb instruction due to being on the incorrect frequency and indicated similar frequencies at Boston ARTCC and New York ARTCC causing communication errors is a frequent occurrence.
An Air Carrier Captain on short final to Runway 1 at Las Vegas reported strobe lights from a street event were a distraction.

Air carrier First Officer reported seeing a lighted UAS off the right side of the aircraft during final approach into a Class B airport during night VMC conditions. The flight reported the UAS to the control tower and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported identifying two separate NOTAMs amending the minimums for the same approach resulting in confusion.
Reports of PAPI lights to PTKK inoperative but not NOTAM'd out of service. Reporter states PAPI and VASI do not operate at PGSN but Jeppesen charts state VASI is available.
GA pilot reported RIC airport taxiway signage confusing around HS1 resulted in a taxiway incursion.
Air carrier Captain reported flying VOR Runway 25 approach to MRLB airport and being 5 degrees or more off centerline and offset to the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported spurious aircraft ADSB and GPS faults occurred during descent into DEN.

Although they were originally cleared 'as filed' and; thus; via the BRDJE RNAV STAR to DFW the flight crew of an A320 was cleared '...direct to SEEVR; descend via' the SEEVR arrival. Aware that SEEVR was a fix on their filed route AND on the BRDJE STAR they mistakenly assumed the FMGC was properly programmed and planned to cross SEEVR between FL230 and FL200. As they approached SEEVR ATC advised they looked to be too high and; only then; did they realize that--although sub-titled (SEEVR.BRDJE1)--their latest clearance was for the SEEVR RNAV STAR--sub-titled (SEEVR.SEEVR1)--and they were to cross SEEVR between 16000 FT and 13000 FT.
Air carrier flight attendants reported having to care for a passenger who had several incapacitating medical problems; but was allowed to travel alone.
Flight Attendant reported cleaners came on board before passengers were off and started spraying the aircraft with a reportedly toxic product.
Captain reported conflicting company policy and FAR duty-day limitations.

Ramp Agent reported an accident between a catering van and a tug pulling a cart.
EMB-175 Captain reported the taxi diagram in ORD can be misleading and suggested a HOTSPOT should be added to the airport chart.

An Air Carrier pilot reported after successfully landing a CATIII approach the crew discovered the runway centerline lights were NOTAM'd out of service at the time they began their approach but began working by short final.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an incorrect passenger count at the gate.

Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer did not want to wear a mask in the cockpit. The Captain decided due to concerns about exposure to COVID-19; to take the only option given of dropping the trip.
Dispatcher reported the flight was re-routed because ATC was closed due to COVID-19.

JAN FLM reported of a problem with the facilities Airport Surveillance Radar (ASR) generator. When turned on during bad weather it has a 50% probability of working. Tech Ops is aware of the problem; but is unable to fix it. FLM is concerned something bad might happen related to the ASR being out of service.
The A330 crew departed without their final weight and balance.
Bonanza 36 pilot reported confusion with ATC while conducting and approach to a Towered airport with the intention of breaking off when in VMC to proceed to a nearby private airstrip.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion due to an ambiguous taxi clearance and poor airport signage and charting at DTW.
ZJX Controller reported a shift working combined sectors due to lack of staffing during periods of weather deviations that created an unsafe workload.
A Trainee Center Controller reported allowing two aircraft to enter an adjacent sector without a handoff. The Trainee recommended new procedures for the arrival flow through the sector.
ZLC Center Controller could not vector an aircraft away from conflicting VFR traffic.
Ramp personnel reported concern regarding unnecessary delays regarding Hazmat (Biologicals/Blood) cargo from Coronavirus Testing. This cargo has a limited risk of spillage.
A IAH Ramp Agent reported that his company is slow to clear ramp personnel to safety with lightning in the vicinity even when it is close; over head and within the airport area.
B737 Captain reported that a sole Controller was working all positions; from taxi to departure at MSY.
SCT Controller reported difficulty guiding descents into SNA that meet the revised MVA limits.
A321 Captain reported disagreement in procedures between MEL and PRC when attempting a manual air start on engine.
An Embraer 145 Flight Crew reported that they received a 'Terrain-Terrain Pull up; Pull up' warning during climb while following ATC instructions.
Air carrier flight crew on approach to ORD had difficulty picking up the runway lights after breaking out of the clouds due to the sporting arena sign west of the airport.
Maintenance personnel reported that the incorrect circuit breakers were pulled when deferring a Vacuum Waste Blower.
B777 Captain reported the cabin crew was fatigued due to a mechanical delay plus an early schedule call; however; scheduling did not understand the fatigue situation.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence 3 miles in trail of a B757 shortly after takeoff from DEN.

The Captain of a cargo aircraft and the Dispatcher responsible for the flight; reported a deviation of company policy regarding communicating an additional fuel upload to dispatch in a timely fashion.
GV flight crew reported that the aircraft's wing hit a hanger while taxiing.
Dispatcher reported a continual shortage of dispatching staff and possible violation of FAR 14 CFR 121.395.
C172 pilot reported communication issues with remote JYO Tower Controller and traffic conflicts while flying in the VFR pattern.

A small aircraft pilot towing a banner had a near miss with a parasail. His evasive actions prevented a collision. He suggests that parasail operators need more training and that parasail operations should be regulated.

Air taxi flight crew reported being informed by their company after the event that they violated noise standards during arrival to OAK. The crew was not aware of the procedures and was following published arrival procedures and ATC vectors until cleared for the visual approach.
Cessna 172 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from an opposite direction B787 in SEA airspace.
A pilot who had flight plan approval to operate in a special use airspace reported having their flight plan cancelled by ATC after stopping on the ground for a short period of time.

CE560 Captain departing Runway 30 at SUA experiences lost comm with PBI Departure on 132.8; with the crew able to hear Departure but Departure could not hear the crew. No problems were experienced on Tower frequency prior to or ZMA after the event. The exact same scenario repeated three days later.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS equipment failure alerts. While evaluating the alerts with the flight crew and operations personnel; a NOTAM detailing GPS outages for the area was discovered.
Dispatcher reported being overwhelmed with aircraft due to staffing shortages and computer system difficulties.
Pilot reported attempting an RNAV Approach using their Electronic Flight Book data for navigation but were off course so ATC vectored them to a Contact Approach.
P80 Controller reported an aircraft departing HIO; on initial contact; was in conflict with non-participating VFR traffic. The Controller reported this as a frequent issue.
Air Carrier pilot reported the airline schedulers routinely schedule pilots from night flights to morning flights which causes pilot fatigue.
A320 Captain reported an unstabilized approach resulted from a higher than normal approach in an effort to avoid wake turbulence.
Flight Instructor encountered possible skydiver activity.

BUF TRACON Controller reports of a communication problem that he is associating with the radios at the facility. Transmissions are made and not heard at times; making the controller repeat them. Controller describes a situation where there was an airborne conflict and he had to repeat transmissions and is not sure if there was a loss of separation due to the radios.
Captain reported fatigue and confusion resulted in a delay due to misapplication of an MEL.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude overshoot due to a light aircraft.
An air carrier pilot reported ATC advised them they were 5 miles south of course. The pilot verified they had the correct route programmed and their instruments showed them on course.
Air carrier First Officer reported the company flight planning system failed to provide sufficient routing instructions from point of non-redispatch to an intermediate dispatch airport.
A Maintenance Technician reported that when he removed a pitot cover on a B767-300; a rubber piece was left in the end of pitot tube.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
A319 flight crew reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL370 from an opposite direction B747 that was 1000 feet above them.
Captain reported a missing or expired NOTAM concerning an Arrival Procedure into San Juan airport.
ATC Center Supervisor reported arrivals routinely experience laser events but no action has been taken to resolve the problem.
P50 TRACON Controller reported receiving a jet departure on a conflicting route overtaking a turboprop departure from the Tower which was permitted according to current facility directives.
PA32 pilot reported an NMAC with a Cessna 172 during simultaneous practice approaches to FDK which does not have access to radar service.
A Ramp Agent reported some newly purchased ground tugs have faulty driver seat bushings which have failed. A driver may fall backward off a failed seat and into a towed cart's path.
CRJ-900 Captain reported that due questionable radio reliability the aircraft was not accepted and refused.
Technicians reported misreading an ECO; resulting in non compliance with established procedures.
GSP TRACON Controller reported an aircraft deviated from its assigned course.
Falcon 2000 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into MIA 17 miles in trail of an A330.
Air carrier flight crew reported equipment problems and having to change destinations due to COVID-19 restrictions.
Flight crew reported flying Standard Instrument Departure (SID) from ABQ were issued vectors due to proximity of terrain.
NCT TRACON Controller reported a go-around was vectored below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Center Controller reported descending an aircraft they handed off to Approach to an altitude contrary to the procedure in the Letter of Agreement and discovered that the Approach Control's altitude minimum is higher than the Center's minimum altitude in that airspace.
A pilot on short final to ORD Runway 27R reported distraction caused by a bright electronic sign in the sporting arena parking.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported their flight tracking tool displaying an airborne aircraft's position was off by over a thousand miles from their correct location.
A Flight Crew reported receiving a GPWS warning during a Visual Approach but continued since they had the terrain in sight and were stable.
Flight attendants on an international flight reported their company forced them to remain working beyond their legal duty time due to an extended ground delay.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxi chart error at SUX airport.

TYS Controller reported of a loss of separation with a small aircraft behind a heavy. Controller was charged with a 'Wake Remnant' separation error. Controller would like a wake remnant indicator to be able to be used by controllers.

Controller working Ground and Local combined reported a conflict resulting in an aircraft having to go-around.  Reporter stated Tower does not need to be departing westbound aircraft off the east side of the airport.

Aircraft landing at SMF delayed runway exit at taxiway. Following arrival noticed the aircraft still partially on the runway and initiated a go-around. The Tower Controller had failed to turn on the taxiway lights after sunset causing the first aircrafts confusion and delay exiting the runway.
A B737NG Captain noted [an] often reported L-GPS FAIL alert by crews on approach to MMMX between the MAVEK and PLAZA waypoints.
B737 NG flight crew was cleared for a LAS 'RNAV Visual 19R Approach'. The approach was selected in the FMC but not found in the iPad database.
PDX Tower Controllers reported an arriving airliner responded to a RA and executed a go around due to VFR traffic in the pattern at nearby VUO.
Air Carrier Captain reported a taxiway incursion and stated Taxiway T2 which was included in their clearance is not charted.

B737 Captain reported that one of the Company Tailored Jeppesen charts for Runway 31L at PSP erroneously indicates that there are VASI lights to the left of the runway which may lead to Taxiway C being mistaken as a runway.
B737 flight crew reported they could not comply with MEL procedure to verify leading edge flap position in flight because they could not see the device from the cockpit.
A319 pilot reported that they did not hold short of Taxiway F while taxiing to Runway 25R at LAX.
SCT Departure Controller reported a departing aircraft was in immediate conflict and received a TCAS RA with VFR aircraft transitioning the airspace not in communication with ATC.
A pilot reported that the navigation database confused the VOR identifier MGM with a heliport known by the same name.
B757 First Officer reported refusing flight due to lack of clarity with MEL and QRH regarding fuel leak checklists.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported experiencing faulty GPS signal to navigation displays. ATC was informed and provided heading clearances.
RNO Tower Controller in Charge; CIC; reported due to sun glare blocking controller's view of final; traffic landed on wrong runway resulted in traffic executing a go around.

Air carrier Captain is dispatched on a transpacific flight with the APU deferred; which it had been for four days. Upon arrival at the gate; ground power is connected and the engines are shut down. Ground power quickly fails and cannot be reestablished leaving the aircraft on battery power alone. After five minutes the battery is turned off leaving the aircraft in darkness.

The TRACON Controller observed an unidentified VFR aircraft depart an airport on a converging course with an air carrier on a visual approach to an adjacent airport already in communication with the Tower. The TRACON Controller pointed the traffic out to the Tower Local Controller. The air carrier responded to a TCAS RA.
Pilot reported the flight was not accepted by TPA Approach Control due to staffing/workload issues. The Captain circumnavigated the area and proceeded VFR.

Captain reports that deferral of the Engine AC Generator Bearing Annunciator light with a 20-flight hour continued operation should not be allowed in the MEL for Cessna CE-560XLs. Primary Bearing failure of the Right Engine AC Generator after only three hours operation since deferral caused an electrical fire that burnt and scorched the nacelle; AC Generator; and wires during taxi-out. Pilot suspects the engine fire bottles would not have been sufficient to extinguish that type of fire in flight.
Aircraft Technician working on EMB-145LR was not provided correct safety equipment for fuel tank entry.
A B767 FMC SEEVR TWO RNAV lists the NECKK altitude constraint below FL280 while the Jeppesen chart NECKK constraint is between FL280 - FL240.
Mechanic reported being unknowingly exposed to a COVID-19 positive employee and not being notified of the exposure by company management.
Ground employee raised concerns with the health effects of people wearing a COVID-19 type mask for long durations of time.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported some shortcomings with their software tool.
B767 Captain reported possibly completing MEL process paperwork incorrectly across different platforms.
Dispatcher reported that an aircraft was fueled with fuel transferred from another aircraft; resulting in a policy violation.
A319 flight crew reported that ATC commanded the flight to go missed.
A321 Flight Attendant reported she wanted to reseat a passenger who was using a cane from the emergency exit row; but was overruled by customer service.
A320 flight crew encountered a bird strike after takeoff.

A twin engine heavy jet flight crew departed RNO 16L after discussions about the max power takeoff and the aircraft's performance. After takeoff the hill off the end of 16L unexpectedly activated the radio altimeter with an EGPWS alert at 500 feet climbing.
B767 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL320 in trail of a B777 departing EGLL that resulted in injuries to a Flight Attendant.
ORD Tower Controller reported an aircraft conducting a go-around flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude because they did not hear their go-around instructions the first time they were issued.
DEN Tower Controller attempted to use a new procedure that allowed a helicopter and arriving aircraft to get too close causing the helicopter to take evasive action.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence seven miles in trail of a B747 on a visual approach to LAX.
A Regional Carrier Pilot reported they did not know their destination had an operating Control Tower until checking in on the UNICOM frequency.
Air Carrier First Officer pilot reported that after resting and getting ready for an early morning flight; scheduling called and pushed back the show time past the maximum duty day.
Local Controller reported during receiving communications transfer an airborne conflict resulted in a NMAC.
Technician reported not having enough air stairs and having to wait to accomplish a task.
Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict between an IFR arrival and a VFR aircraft while working combined Local positions.
Tower Front Line Manager reported a runway incursion and concerns with the Local Controller not issuing a go-around instruction to an aircraft landing on the same runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported negative training due to maintenance issues and malfunctions with the fixed-base training device used for transition training to a new aircraft.
GA pilot reported being unable to contact ATC due to possible Controller under staffing resulting in a Class C airspace violation.
Ramp worker reported that a tailstand was not available to support the plane while passengers were deplaned from the front of the aircraft.
A Center Controller reported they cleared a corporate jet aircraft for an approach to an airport that only authorized Air Taxi operations.

PDX Tower Controller and an air carrier pilot reported a go-around event at PDX due to a TCAS/RA triggered by a light aircraft in the pattern at an adjacent airport underneath the instrument final approach course.
A Maintenance Technician reported that the flight crew inaccurately wrote-up the crew oxygen required servicing after it was signed-off as being within limits.

SCT TRACON Controller reported losing the data tag; secondary beacon and primary target on an aircraft on downwind. Pilot reported transponder failure when questioned; Controller wondered why all contact was lost and an assumption that it had to do with STARS Radar was made.

A CRJ-700 Captain rejected that takeoff for the TAKEOFF CONFIG alert because of a miss set trim. The First Officer complained the new Takeoff Checklist forces heads down; task saturation operation before takeoff.
B737-800 Captain reported being pressured by company personnel to lower minimum dispatch fuel request.
Air carrier pilot questions the classification of glufosinate ammonium that was loaded as cargo on the flight; as non-hazmat.
ZDC Controller reported during a period of weather deviations lack of Traffic Management flow initiatives caused an unsafe workload.
S46 TRACON Controller reported multiple incidents where VFR aircraft have caused airborne conflicts in the BFI area.
Air carrier First Officer and D10 TRACON Controller reported that a clearance to descend was given; but due to a congested radio frequency the instruction was misunderstood.
A ZMA Controller descended one aircraft and climbed another aircraft head on. The Controller issued turns to the aircraft but had less than required lateral separation.
Small aircraft instructor reported ATC informed of Tower LOA traffic pattern altitude which is not published or available on pilot resources.
Air carrier First Officer reported being high on arrival due to ATC being overwhelmed working several frequencies.
ALO Controller reported being unable to hand off an aircraft due to an erroneous beacon code between separate HOST computers.

SFO Local Controller reported that certain aircraft which taxi low to the ground have difficulty receiving transmissions in a Runway 10L dead zone along portions of Taxiways S and S2. Antenna location seems to be the problem.
EMB-145 flight crew reported that confusion caused by an outdated taxiway map resulted in a taxiway incursion.

The Local Controller did not notice an aircraft entering downwind was in close proximity to another aircraft already on downwind. Due to workload the Controller had requested an assist position to help them but the request was denied.
Maintenance Technician reported during maintenance preflight a tow crew member went to the cockpit and turned off the battery power without authorization.

Certified Professional Controller; CPC; at Denver TRACON (Pueblo Sector); reported a recurring automation failure between D01 (PUB Sector); and Springs departure; COS.  Automation handoffs received; do not transfer necessary aircraft data to provide required service. Further interfacility automated handoffs to Denver ARTCC; ZDV; do not display and transfer correct data.
Airbus Captain reported experiencing GPS interference during cruise.

A dispatcher was concerned that the increased workload resulting from the surprise assignment of additional flights to/from stations with which she had not previously worked; was excessive and compromised her ability to provide proper flight planning and support.
B757 Captain reported the current use of the Electronic Logbook (ELB) makes it difficult to look back at the aircraft's history; therefore the current process compromises safety.
A320 Captain reported while entering a high workload environment an ACARS message came requesting additional flying.
Aviation Maintenance Technician reported concerns about the company imposing a corporate culture on maintenance personnel that puts an emphasis on business goals and may be contrary to safety.
Air carrier Captain reported that charts do not show the correct location of gates.
Pilot reported flying without proper registration.
ZMP Center Controller reported an aircraft had not been handed off to the adjacent facility due to new symbology in Center radar data tags.
Flight Attendant reported that upon arrival at destination; a COVID-19 positive passenger on board as well as the crew; had to be medically assessed before being allowed to leave the aircraft.

B757 Captain accepted the duty time extension available under FAR 117. After the flight; the pilot felt in hindsight that by accepting the extension; crew scheduling had filled their coverage issue; and they were no longer concerned about possible fatigue issues.

Air carrier Captain reported attempting to cancel a trip due to safety concerns from a forecast of hazardous air quality related to wildfire smoke and being penalized for not wanting to conduct the flight.
B777 First Officer reported communication difficulties with Amazonica Center led to a traffic conflict at FL380.
B737 flight crew experienced a GPS outage during the VOR DME RWY 05L/R approach at MMMX. An overshoot at PLAZA resulted but the crew was able to land visually.
GA pilot reported that most commercial operators flying to MYEF use the wrong frequency for traffic advisories on UNICOM versus the correct AFIS frequency.
B777 Captain reported that the CDU indicated different altitude restriction than the arrival chart; resulting in a crossing restriction error.
An air carrier Dispatcher reported that important NOTAM information regarding a runway closer was not published in the company's flight planning software.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger that had a valve on their mask was asked to replace the mask or cover the valve.
FWA TRACON Controller reported losing radar on an aircraft when it went over the radar site.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported an unexpected communication issue with company personnel due to equipment problems.
ZLA Center Controller working sectors with new arrival/departure procedures discovered an error in one of the routes.
A319 First Officer reported that recent EFB updates have negatively impacted usability.

An air carrier gate agent reported the leveling system of the jetway he was utilizing to deplane a B737 malfunctioned; resulting in it sinking below the passenger entry/exit threshold and potentially impacting the fully open cabin door. After calling for maintenance the reporter implored the cabin attendants to expedite the passenger deplaning.
ZLC ARTCC Controller reported they were handed off an aircraft below their radar coverage on a random direct route instead of an approved non radar route.

ZDC Controller reported of an aircraft that transited in RVSM airspace; but was not qualified to be in that airspace. Controller reported that there was a loss of separation due to the aircraft not being RVSM equipped. Controller thought someone upstream should have verified if the aircraft was RVSM qualified.

An Air Carrier Ramp Lead expresses his safety concerns and also describes his work environment where company ramp personnel are being assigned to work a Regional Operator's CRJ-700 aircraft that they are not qualified or trained on. Company Management have been unresponsive to his concerns for safe operations.
A Maintenance Technician working on a B777 was asked to install an incorrect part number seal by a customer representative.
Technician reported difficulty in locating correct reference for replacing the Rudder Trim Knob.
ZDC Center Controller reported the Traffic Management Unit did not effectively manage traffic flow during weather deviations allowing the sector to become overloaded.
ZAU Controller and pilot reported the aircraft was at the wrong altitude due to an incorrect altimeter setting.
ZME Center Controller reported exposure to Methyl Ethyl Ketone fumes in the facility for two days.
Air carrier Captain reported being issued the wrong frequency to activate pilot controlled lights at an airport where the Tower was closed.
A Flight Attendant reported not being given information or notice when deadheading as the only passenger on a ferry flight which had to return to the departure airport.
B747 Captain reported a rejected takeoff when the STAB CONFIG warning message appeared.
Customer Service Agent reported concerns with the daily under staffing in the airport lobby and the difficulties it is creating during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Distracted by a landing gear that failed to retract; the flight crew of a BAE-125-850XP noted a gaggle of geese too close ahead to avoid; and felt one or more strike the aircraft. The impact was followed by erratic indications and a report of smoke from the left engine heard over the radio. They performed an inflight shut down; and returned to their departure airport for an uneventful landing.
A321 flight crew reported inadvertently flying below the floor of the DEN Class B airspace sector due to unclear charting.

PHL Tower Controller reported an aircraft conducting a Visual Approach to a runway which the PAPI's have been inoperative for more than four years was lined up with the parallel taxiway; but the ASDE-X never alerted.
Pilot reported the IAF for the ILS approach to Runway 32 at DIK is not in the FMS database.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that a new procedure resulted in increased workload causing temporary loss of operational control.
Dispatcher reported noticing that the PGV airport AWOS was transmitting incorrect information. Dispatcher stated the PGV AWOS is frequently unreliable.
Aircraft Mechanic reported that management stated that a Functional Flight Check was not required after working on flight control components.

CRP Controller reports of the lack of a data link between his facility and NGP. Both facilities use STARS but the system does not communicate and the controller has to make manual hand offs on all inbound traffic. Controller reports this is a safety risk.

Pilot questions why their dispatcher(s) are not getting a copy of their Maintenance Check Flight form prior to planning a Functional Check Flight (FCF) flight plan. He also questions the practice by the Director of Operations of determining; once airborne; where the airplane will go after a pass or fail Functional Check Flight becomes known. The perceived secrecy does not allow the check flight crew to effectively plan the flight and leads to degraded ATC coordination.
G450 flight crew reported they offset to the left on an oceanic route due to an A380 1000 feet above them to avoid potential wake turbulence.

Maintenance Technician reported many part numbers are incorrect due to the 'harmonizing' of part numbers for a B737-900 Illustrated Parts Catalogue. An installed battery charger that was supposed to be compatible failed the test due to this problem.
A320 Captain reported UAV less than 1 mile abeam the ILS.
A Corporate flight crew on the TEB 19 ILS at 2;000 feet received a TCAS TA from an inbound EWR jet at 3;000 feet and a TCAS RA CLIMB from VFR traffic 400 feet below.
Center Controller reported being overloaded with traffic; MAP [Monitor Alert Parameter] was in the red; having a loss of separation; and an airspace violation due to COVID-19 staffing.

BTR Controllers report of an aircraft being too high for the approach. The pilot requested a 360 turn to get lower. This was approved by Developmental; then corrected by him to fly runway heading. The aircraft making missed approach now becomes a conflict with another aircraft. Controllers' report of having an ARTS failure during this operation.
G-V flight crew reported responding to a TCAS RA on departure from TEB.

MDW Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation when an aircraft lined up with a taxiway instead of the runway.  Controller reported this approach is likely to be problematic because the angle it brings in aircraft on; lines up with Taxiway Y; until the last minute.
B767-300 Captain reported that company Contract Maintenance are not afforded direct access to the company reporting system.
Dispatcher reported that random windshear alerts occur at RIC airport when no significant weather exists.
Pilot reported flying twice with an out of date registration.
Pilots reported routing and fuel problems associated with a ARTCC airspace closure.
Captain reported that the First Officer's tablet had locked up. A Maintenance Technician corrected the problem; but did not make a logbook entry.
Air carrier Captain descending on a Visual Approach to ABE reported they received a Terrain Alert.
B747 flight crew reported that the Captain became unresponsive; causing the crew to divert.
Air carrier flight crew reported losing communication with ARTCC. The reporter stated they were on the appropriate frequency the entire time; but may have been in an area of limited radio coverage.
B767 First Officer reported executing a go-around after receiving a GPWS terrain warning on an RNAV GPS approach to SFO Runway 10L.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with COVID-19 guidelines on blocking or not blocking seats and making reference to Flight Attendant/passenger safety.
The procedures in the MEL for deferring the potable water system were not accomplished completely which led up to the canceling of the flight.

ZDV Center Controller reported trying to coordinate traffic flows to the D01 TRACON during a period of weather deviations. The Center Controller was receiving conflicting instructions from different people at the TRACON. The Center Controller reports this is an ongoing problem when trying to coordinate with the TRACON.
A Flight Attendant reported that a passenger requested to get off the airplane without claiming their luggage.
Center Controller reported a hand off issue with adjacent airspace resulting in an airspace violation.
EMB-145 Captain reported rejecting takeoff due to jammed rudder pedals. Taxied back to gate for maintenance corrective action.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported a teen unaccompanied minor had a doctor's note saying he had small seizures but weren't anything to worry about. Flight Attendant reported that during the flight the teen had 3 seizures and was foaming at the mouth.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger stated that another passenger had not been wearing a mask.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with re-qualification training courses after not flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier pilot reported there is no safe aircraft emergency egress while parked at IAH with the terminal and ramp doors in the jetway both locked.
B737 pilot reported exceeding the aircraft's maximum tire speed during takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were given taxi instructions that did not agree with the airport chart.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported excessive workload related to insufficient staffing.

A pilot reports about cracks that develop in a bracket attached to the Nose Landing Gear (NLG) strut that are not deferrable on Hawker HS-125-800 XP aircraft; causing delays and cancellations. Bracket supports a squat switch that controls the wing landing lights and taxi lights.
Ramp Agent reported incorrectly operating the cargo loader and made reference to being off of work due to a leave of absence for COVID-19.
Air taxi Dispatcher reported company Part 135 dispatchers are allowed to work very long hours and that fatigue has compromised safety.
An air carrier Maintenance Technician reported a health and safety concern over a delay in addressing mosquitos in the cabin after a flight from Buenos Aires.
Air Carrier Captain reported he has observed pressure from his company on flight attendants to work even if they are sick.
Pilot reported being pressured by a flight school owner to fly an unairworthy aircraft.
B767 Maintenance reported that the hoist equipment failed when raising a main landing gear actuator.
B737NG flight crew reported the BNA CHSNE 2 RNAV Arrival design keeps aircraft high on the arrival making the crossing restriction at BGSTR difficult.
S46 TRACON Controller reported having to vector an aircraft off their approach to avoid VFR traffic not in communication with ATC.
B737 NG Captain reported the lack of NOTAMs regarding the shortened length of Runway 27 in SAN.
Air carrier pilot reported their FMS could not navigate the LAS RNAV-V 19R Approach properly due to the fixes on the SITEE Arrival not blending into the approach.

SCT Controller thought an aircraft that was on an IFR flight plan was on a VFR flight plan. The controller used 500 feet vertical separation in a holding pattern instead of 1000 feet. The targets on the STARS radar display were jumping back and forth from their actual position.

During the takeoff roll a flight crew receives an advisory message yet continues the takeoff. Once at altitude it is discovered that the message should have warranted a rejected takeoff procedure and the crew should have remained on the ground.
EMB-175 First Officer reported that Maintenance and the flight crew disagreed on the tire damage limits.
Controllers had a disagreement over the requirement to hand off a VFR aircraft from a TRACON to an ARTCC.
PA28 pilot reported a NMAC with a UAV.
An ARR Tower Controller reports of staffing issues at the Tower. Reporter sites a specific day where he thought they did a good job with the short staffing; but management thinks they did not.
ZLA Center Controller reported losing separation due to traffic volume; watching too large of an area and the COVID-19 configuration.
Air carrier First Officer reported that after setting up his EFB tablet to display the QRH; all the tabs had closed at pushback.
B777 Captain reported that the reserve First Officer had not flown in 8 months and was not proficient in the aircraft.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported ground service personnel attempted to open the galley door when it was still armed shortly after arrival; and explained they could not see the flag through the window because of sun glare.
A single engine pilot reported approach control vectored a military aircraft over his aircraft which the pilot felt was unsafe.
B737 Captain found the new cold weather section of his operations manual to be very confusing; especially the section on freezing precipitation.
ZLA Center Controller reported an aircraft was cleared via an OAPM STAR; but the aircraft had an extra fix in their database compared to the chart.
GEG Radar Approach controllers reported their sector vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Four reports from an SCT Controller providing training; his trainee Controller and the crew of an airline aircraft documenting a near mid-air caused by an aircraft coming out of the Banning Pass and conflicting with the airliner turning final for PSP.
Ground Personnel reported that a lack of training on new jetbridge equipment created a ramp safety hazard.
Captain reported GPS jamming at this location.
Technician reported that task cards for servicing the APU of aircraft in storage do not have the correct reference number.
B737 Captain reported the flight plan they were on did not require an alternate due to good weather; when they arrived the weather was at minimums with no alternate.
Pilot reported unsafe flight condition due to racetrack lights pointing directly into the sky disorienting the pilot.

A Center Controller reported a neigboring Center Sector allowed an aircraft to fly through a corner of their airspace without coordination and then observed the same Sector handoff two aircraft on converging headings at the same altitude.
First officer reported incorrect wording on Jeppsen chart departure.
First Officer reported that the PAPI (Precision Approach Path Indicator) is located on the left side of the runway whereas it's listed on the approach plate as being on the right side.
B737 First Officer reported three TA alerts while on approach to Runway 10L at PDX due to pattern work at VUO.
DFW Tower Controller mistakenly departed aircraft using RNAV departures rather than vectors. The information display system wasn't showing the types of departures in use.

A revision to a Letter of Agreement (LOA) was published. Previous preapproved coordination rules were accidentally omitted from the new LOA. The rule allowed TRACON controllers to descend aircraft while still in ARTCC's airspace. Controllers were not briefed on the omission and continued to operate under the previous rule which no longer existed in the new publication.

Two Center Traffic Management Specialists and a Controller report the Command Center ignored requests for weather reroutes; causing numerous Center sectors to be dangerously overloaded with traffic deviating for weather.
A319 Captain reported a contradiction from standard operating procedures when asked to release the brakes prior to push clearance to facilitate use of a new style tug.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported a hazardous condition at a maintenance facility which could result in injury or death.

ZNY ARTCC Controller reported a TRACON Controller entered a remove strip message on a flight plan. The Controller had to override the flight plan control to enter the message. An aircraft with the same call sign was active in ARTCC airspace. The data tag on the ARTCC Controllers radar disappeared from the display.
Air Carrier Captain reported experiencing a momentary stall warning after encountering wake turbulence departing RJAA in trail of a heavy B787.
Commercial fixed wing pilot reported a taxiway incursion due to poor signage and pavement markings at LAX.
Flight Attendant reported that the aircraft did not have adequate cleaning/sanitizing supplies on board and the aircraft did not appear to have been properly cleaned.
Eugene air traffic control Tower operator reported Tower lighting panel out of service; hindering IFR operations.

Air carrier Captain reported being 100 ft left of the runway when breaking out of the clouds on a DAL Runway 31R ILS Approach.
E175 flight crew reported jet blast from another aircraft on the takeoff roll caused loss of the flight director vertical and lateral modes.
Three ZHU Controllers reported a loss of separation event.  Controllers reported factors were high volume; weather; and no traffic management program in effect.
Air carrier Captain reported his company regularly dispatches two flights with similar call signs at the same time every day.
A 777 first officer reports that a new taxi procedure has increased distractions and confusion 'several times over.'

Air carrier flight crew reported being notified in flight that the destination city required a COVID-19 test upon arrival. Based on multiple concerns with the testing process; the crew elected to return to their departure airport.
ATL Tower Controller reported working combined positions during a busy period while the unqualified Operations Manager performed some of the Clearance Delivery duties.
While performing maintenance on an EC135 T2 Helicopter the Mechanic neglected to secure a panel in the lower half of the fuselage.
Dispatcher reported that a flight was delayed without Dispatch being notified.
Ramp Agent reported that an aircraft was being pushed from the gate with vehicles in the safety envelope zone.
B757 First Officer reported finding broken glass in crew meal.
