BOI TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation where it was too busy to split the position.
B737-800 flight crew reported receiving TCAS 'descend' commands on a parallel approach to DFW that took them down to 1500 FT and prompted a low altitude alert from Tower.
C172 pilot reports a Class B airspace incursion event.
A Controller reported observing an aircraft descend below its assigned crossing altitude and into an underlying sector.
CRJ flight crew reported a birdstrike to right engine on departure.
Center Radar controllers reported an aircraft was allowed to fly through a hot Restricted Area that the Assist controller had muted the warning system.
A320 Captain reported being pressured by his company to take an aircraft which he refused due to an inoperative APU.
Air carrier Captain reported similar sounding fixes on the NIIXX 1 [now NIIXX 2] arrival into Denver which might be confused or misheard.
PA-28 Instructor Pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from the preceding C172 in the pattern at OCF.
Pilot reported the P50 TRACON controllers appear to be working unreasonably large areas and expressed concern over COVID-19 related cutbacks in ATC staffing.
PA-28 pilot reported his wing was damaged as a result of an inflight goose strike.

B737 flight crew reports being confused by the AMRST4 departure from CLE; with the top altitude on initial departure listed as 3;000 feet and 5;000 feet in different places. A NOTAM apparently cleared up this confusion but the crew did not notice it. ATC made the crew aware when they checked in climbing to 3;000 feet that 5;000 feet was the correct altitude.
CRW TRACON Controller reported the minimum vectoring altitude radar display maps are out of date.
Air Taxi pilot reported bright lights from a hangar made it difficult to see the taxiway lights and markings at STL airport.
GA pilot reported radio reception issues Northeast of CHS (Charleston) on frequency 119.3 MHz.
ZKC Controller reported a departure on the CHIEF7 SID navigating to KATTS instead of the published SID fix of CATTS. Both fixes are phonetically identical and very similar spelling.
Air carrier First Officer reported BNA Runway 31 ILS hold short line is wrongly depicted on the chart when compared to its actual location on the airfield.

ZJX Controller reported of a search function while using user request evaluation tool had too many aircraft in the list. Controller reported of seeing too many aircraft and aircraft from other sectors displayed in the list.

B777 First Officer's lament the lack of standardized contingency plans for route planning in all areas of South America that details procedures for engine failure or depressurization in the vicinity of high terrain.
DA42 flight crew reported aircraft was deliberately serviced with the incorrect fuel; causing an immediate diversion.
Air carrier Captain experienced a sink rate warning when the glideslope failed during ILS approach to PGV. A go-around was initiated and the second approach attempt was successful.
ZSE Controller issued an IFR clearance altitude below the Minimum Enroute Altitude for the airway.
Air carrier Captain reported a new illuminated billboard adjacent to the approach end of Runway 18R along Interstate 485 is very distracting during night and IMC approaches.
Air carrier First Officer reported communication difficulties with the Tower Controller due to the Controller wearing a mask which possibly muffled the transmission.
BUR Tower Controller reported SCT relayed a missed approach altitude below an MVA that the aircraft may have entered while turning away from it.
ZMA Center Controller reported being unable to manage sectors well due to poor or non-existent radio coverage.

Flight Attendant reported concerns regarding the COVID-19 pre-takeoff PA instructing passengers to give the FAs trash during the safety compliance check. Flight Attendant believes this detracts from the main reason for the safety check.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported the stick shaker was activated on initial climb out of ORD when they encountered wake turbulence in trail of an A319/320.
A Maintenance Technician reported not having the proper equipment to gain access to the upper rotating beacon on an Embrarer aircraft.
An Air Carrier Captain reported after refusing an aircraft due to multiple maintenance issues the company did not do a good job coordinating the repairs and notifying appropriate personnel.
A general aviation pilot reported receiving traffic notice by ATC but difficulty in seeing the traffic in a congested area.
Technician reported that the pitot static system drain line required removal of contamination per policy. However; no one was trained to accomplish task.
Air Carrier Captain reported the crew made the decision to suspend in-flight service for COVID-19 social distancing reasons contrary to company policy.

Air carrier flight attendant reported that items were missing from the aircraft's medical kit. In addition; it was noted that some commonly used medical testing devices were not included in the air carrier's medical kit inventory.
An air taxi aircraft struck a bird on the takeoff roll from PHMU and continued to their destination where Maintenance found bird strike evidence on one of the propeller blades.
An observer reported that Ham Radio Antennas are blocking a water tower obstruction light.
A90 Approach Controller reported that a sector could not accept a handoff; causing the aircraft to fly below the MVA.
Pilot reported the Notam is for another airport located to the northeast.
Flight Attendant reported lack of disinfectant wipes and cleaning agents on the round trip flight.
ZTL ARTCC Controllers reported a loss of communications on primary and backup frequencies; resulting in an NMAC.
CRJ First Officer reported an unstable approach due to heavy workload and miscommunication with ATC that led to a taxiway incursion.
Air carrier ramp employee reported a co-worker was struck by lightning because; in his opinion; the company failed to close the ramp during a weather event in a timely fashion.
PA-24 pilot reported a lack of support from ATC led to a possible Class B airspace violation.
B737 Captain questioned the wisdom of a new APU procedure within the ETOPs area in flight.
Air Carrier Captain reported there is not a NOTAM or company flight plan note regarding runway construction.
Air Carrier Captain reported a laser incident while climbing out of SBGR.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported extreme workload due to weather causing major delays.
A Controller reported that the Traffic Management Unit routed aircraft through areas of known severe turbulence.

GSO Controller reports of a busy session working a combined position due to a training class. Controller had a loss of separation with two aircraft and attributes it to not being a strong controller and being unable to work busy sessions with non-combined positions. Controller indicates it was the lack of staffing that led to combined positions; being overloaded; and having a loss of separation.
Captain reported confusion regarding a main tire and wheel assembly replacement and the logbook entries that resulted in a flight delay and maintenance action.
An air carrier pilot reported they missed a crossing restriction and did not reduce to 200 knots below Class B airspace.

Flight Attendant reported there were no masks on the plane for flight attendant use and was concerned that the gloves and alcohol-free hand sanitizer were not adequate as personal protective equipment.
A flight crew reported they had to shut down an engine but continued to their destination instead of landing at a closer airport as stated in the QRH.

Air Carrier flight crew reported they rejected a takeoff at LAX when the Tower Controller cancelled takeoff clearance and called abort. The crew reported the ATC ground surveillance radar had sounded a false alarm.
Dispatcher reported that too many flights are being assigned to each desk; leading to heavy workload and safety concerns.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion regarding Type IV fluid holdover times when mixed types of precipitation are present.
A Tower Controller had to stop the descent of an aircraft on a Visual Approach for VFR departure from a satellite airport on a converging course.
S56 Tracon Controller reported vectoring an aircraft encountering icing and unable to maintain altitude and airspeed into lower terrain and out of the conditions.
A Ramp Agent discussed the physical hazard's ramp workers encounter when hot liquids are poured into aircraft drains while the aircraft are parked at the gate.

King Air 200 pilot experienced loss of GPS signal after departing ELP and passing through FL250. The failure was due to NOTAMed GPS jamming in the area and ATC assigned a heading to destination. With the loss of NAV capability the autopilot also lost altitude capture; resulting in an 800 foot overshoot of FL270.
Aircraft technician reported nitrogen bottles improperly painted as oxygen bottles.
PA-28 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from an air carrier aircraft in the vicinity of TKI airport.
Dispatcher reported working an overloaded desk and overall staffing issues.
KZAK Controller reported that a course deviation by one aircraft resulted in an airborne conflict.
A pilot reported skydiving operations continue when jumpers must pass through clouds during descent.
Boeing 737 Captain reported erratic ILS course and glideslope indications while proceeding inbound in visual conditions.

Air Carrier First Officer reported not obtaining an ATC departure clearance prior to pushback; and cited a nonstandard pre-flight briefing given by the Captain and lack of flying as contributing factors.
A319 Captain reported returning to departure airport after birds were ingested into Number 1 engine shortly after takeoff; resulting in catastrophic engine failure.
GA pilot reported being distracted by an unlatched cabin door and climbed into Class B airspace. This situation was exacerbated by an incorrect altimeter setting.
A Controller lost radar contact with an aircraft. The Controller was not sure if they maintained required separation standards between this aircraft and another aircraft which was in radar coverage.
ZMA Center Controller reported several near losses of separation and possible airspace violations due to the workload.
ATC reported an aircraft under the controller's area encountered a NMAC.
Air carrier Captain reported an anomaly in the NOTAMs depicting an area of taxiway construction.
Center Controller reported that the adjacent Center handed them off two aircraft on converging headings at the same altitude; causing an airborne conflict.
EMB-140 Flight crew reported a Terrain warning during visual approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported LGB 10-9 Page does not depict the Runway 26L Approach hold short line.
GA student pilot reported frequency communications issues while flying to an airport whose Tower services were not in use.
Air carrier Captain reported an EGPWS TERRAIN alert during a stabilized; daylight visual approach to ABQ Runway 26.
GA pilot reported the approach for Runway 30 at FCH is unsafe because the PAPI lights are non-operational.
B737 Captain reported they refused aircraft after Maintenance misapplied an MEL to the left ram air door.
BE35 pilot reported sighting a UAV (drone) near UGN in very close proximity to his aircraft requiring evasive action.

Air carrier First Officer reported being assigned to fly with a Captain whose previous First Officer co-pilot called in sick with COVID-19 symptoms.  Reporter described concerns with management's handling of the situation stating it goes against all the guidelines delineated by the company.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with having to wear a face mask and the use of hand sanitizers.
SCT Controller reported descending an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
The Captain of a Falcon 2000 reported that many write-ups were sign-off but never repaired by Maintenance.
Center Controller reported working combined sectors and not noticing an aircraft on a route through Special Use Airspace (SUA).

The pilot reported that nonstandard phraseology for a critical flight management switch was being used by instructors in the training center environment. The phraseology used was a slang term; and does not comport with the airline's Flight Manual.
Air carrier pilot on approach to BUF Runway 23 was confused by the presence of a PAPI because the Jeppesen 10-9 Chart does not indicate a PAPI installed.
B767 First Officer reported an airspeed deviation during arrival on a STAR that contained vague charting.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing three minutes of GPS jamming while flying at FL340 in Iraqi airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant did not agree with the Captain not wearing a mask in the cockpit.
Captain of a fractional operation commented on fatigue issues with scheduling flight crews in 135 and 91 operations.
ZTL Controller reported the Traffic Management Coordinators were not complying with requests during a busy period with weather deviations causing them to miss several point outs to adjacent sectors.
C172 Pilot Flying reported FBO filling CTAF with excessive information and blocking critical pilot communications.
After the fact; a B757 First Officer was made aware that he and Captain taxied with a ground crewmen head set still attached to the aircraft. Neither pilot saw the person.

During a scheduled ASDE-X outage this Tower and the TRACON also experienced Primary Radar software outages on some of their radar displays requiring the back-up system to be necessary.The reporter states that the appropriate maintenance office was not notified of the Radar display outages. The reporter wanted to know if this was a coincidence or a known issue that the controllers should have been briefed on.

A B737 flight crew reported receiving the HAZMAT NOTOC report which they examined and discovered lithium batteries and flammable liquid in the same cargo compartment. Upon questioning Operations they were told the flammable liquid was removed. Upon arrival; the crew discovered the flammable liquid and lithium batteries were still on board.
EMB-175 Captain reported system anomalies were annunciated after encountering wake turbulence 5 nm in trail of an A321 on approach to LAX.
EMB-145 Captain reported a terrain issue associated with the RNAV Runway 33 Approach into Bangor; Maine.
Captain reported DIK airport is under construction; the runway lights are inoperative and there are no NOTAMs regarding these conditions.
A-321 First Officer reported a wake turbulence encounter while climbing out from SFO.
Business jet Captain reported an encounter with an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV); at approximately 2;600 feet; during initial approach to LAX Runway 24L. No evasive action was required.
Flight Attendant reported problems associated with wearing the required COVID-19 type mask while performing duties.
NCT Controller reported that a trainee's failure to follow instructions caused an aircraft to descend below MVA.

VFR aircraft requested an altitude to drop parachute jumpers. Controller restricted the aircraft 1;000 feet below its requested altitude and advises there will be a delay for approval for jumpers due to traffic. VFR aircraft releases parachute jumpers anyway and advises Controller to move traffic away from the jump area.
A319 flight crew reported an altitude overshoot on descent due to confusion with the charted transition level.
An Air Carrier Captain reported during a flight crew switch and cabin distractions they did not comply with a crossing restriction.
DAB TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict was exacerbated by faulty voice communications equipment.
EMB-145 Captain reported the BUF Runway 5 ILS signal is weak and unreliable.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an RA alert during initial approach to BUR airport.
Pilot reported issues with the service provided by SLC Tower and cited Controller workload as a contributing factor.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of GPS signal apparently due to jamming from White Sands.

A First Officer questions whether a logbook corrective action entry by Maintenance was appropriate or just ridiculous. He found the emergency brake and landing gear pneumatic vent lines dripping hydraulic fluid during a post flight walk around of their Cessna Citation X aircraft. Maintenance had disconnected; vacuumed; cleaned with alcohol; and re-secured the lines.
Air carrier First Officer reported calling in fatigued for an assignment that followed a six-day trip which included interrupted off-hour sleep and multiple time zone crossings.
Center Controller reported working 3 sectors combined during the COVID-19 pandemic and did not point out an aircraft resulting in an airspace violation.
B737 First Officer reported that they deviated from clearance due to flight management system not accepting new arrival.
B737-800 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after ingesting birds into the right engine.

Air Carrier pilot reports of incorrect data on the LIDO chart that they were using to fly to the destination airport. The frequency of the ILS was in error on one chart; but correct on another chart. The change had taken place two years ago.
Pilot reported a non-towered airport's publications and ASOS broadcast have not been updated to reflect a recent name change; with the inconsistency in naming causing NMAC events.
Air carrier flight crew reported exceeding the speed restriction that limits aircraft to 250 knots until 13;000 feet on the ORD2 SID.

After deadheading in; running late for departure; heavy snow; finding a cold unpowered airplane already being boarded by passengers and dealing with a harried; uncooperative gate agent all combined for a rushed and incomplete preflight environment during which the flight crew failed to note there was no weather packet contained in the flight papers until after takeoff.
ZFW Controller reported of flow control problems into the sector and bad radios in this same area.
San Juan Center Controller reported radio problems and similar sounding aircraft call signs that led to an aircraft take another's descent clearance; which then became an airspace violation.
Technician reported confusing dates relating to work that needed to be performed on an aircraft that had been in storage.

Air carrier Captain reported while on a vector to ZSPD ILS 17R; the crew did not get a response from approach so they switched to the Tower frequency on the approach plate (124.35) and did not get a response either. Captain reported approach gave the crew a different Tower frequency than what was listed on the approach plate.

BFL Approach Controller reported of not being able to see an aircraft's transponder. The Controller gave the pilot the option of being routed non-radar or cancelling IFR. Pilot cancelled IFR and Controller tried to provide flight following. Controller reported that ZLA and FAT Approach could see the aircrafts transponder. Controller requested the radar be updated during NextGen technology.

SCT Controller reported of a target making a hard turn and descending into a lover MVA. Controller couldn't tell if the aircraft was in fact acting erratic or an issue with STARS. Controller stated he and others at SCT would like to see a return to CARTS.
Flight Attendant at jump seat position was struck with shards of dry ice from bag exploding.
ZOA ARTCC Controller reported that while conducting training during a high volume; and complex period; a VFR pop-up was issued an IFR clearance below the MVA.
ZAU Center Controller reported that an aircraft had to go-around after being issued an RNAV Approach due to equipment and personnel on the runway.

B777 flight crew is informed by ATC of a volcanic ash plume ahead that will require deviation to miss and the crew accepts a revised route. This particular volcanic ash plume had not been part of the Dispatch Release and the crew only became aware when informed by Magadan Control.
Piper 32 pilot reported an NMAC with a B737 aircraft.
Technician reported uncertainty in processes in determining whether or not to replace a crew O2 bottle that was five PSI over the minimum limit due to a restraining order in place.
A Pilot reported that the MEL requires the fuel quantity to be read by sticking the tanks; but the MEL does not include a fuel stick chart.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on final approach for BFI conflicted with VFR aircraft transiting the approach course not in communication with ATC.
Tower Controller reported a ground conflict and cited reduced staffing due to COVID-19 and training at combined positions as contributing factors.
ORD Tower Controller reported a recurring problem with momentary loss of runway lights.
Air carrier storekeeper reported that the new Parts Inventory Management System has raised safety concerns among parts delivery personnel.
Flight Attendant reported multiple concerns with wearing a face mask.
C90 TRACON Controller reported there was no Traffic Management Coordinator on their shift during a period of weather causing delays and deviations.

Technician reported that after checking several aircraft; he found them to have malfunctioning RH ram air door actuators requiring replacement and made logbook entries; after which Company Management berated him for doing so.
Maintenance Auditor reported critical airplane component failures are not being reported as required by the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR)'s.
A320 Captain reported an EGPWS alert during approach to ONT airport.
ATC Center Supervisor reported aircraft on the wrong routings and in conflict with other aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported landing instead of initiating a go-around despite receiving a brief wind shear warning on final.
A320 Captain reported the procedure to perform a flight control test per the MEL was incomplete.
SAT Approach Controller reported a B737 on final approach received an RA for unidentified VFR traffic departing an adjacent satellite airport.

A Pilot reports of an airspace issue relayed to him by approach control. The aircraft departed OSU airport and climbed to 3;400 feet; made a right turn to a 240 degree heading. Approach stated that the aircraft entered into the Class C airspace of CMH and they had to restrict a carrier above this aircraft.
One TRACON Controller did not realize an aircraft would be below the MEA; while the center controller accepted the handoff with the aircraft below the MEA.
ZLA ARTCC Controller reported a loss of separation between a flight of military aircraft and an arriving descending air carrier aircraft.
ZJX Center Controller reported an adjacent military ATC facility handed an aircraft off to the wrong sector while it flew through his airspace.
Maintenance Technician reports that 737-800 tire pressures are reading low after landing.
An aircrew experienced a CSD (Constant Speed Drive) failure and mistakenly used the wrong checklist to remedy the situation.
B767 Captain reported inadvertently shutting down the APU after pushing the battery switch because it indicated off when it actually was on.
Dispatch Supervisor sent staff home and dispatchers were over worked and understaffed.
Air carrier pilot stated hotel was not keeping crews separate from each other on crew transportation from the hotel.
Ramp employee reported a string of baggage carts rolled onto the runway when the brakes failed on the lead cart.
Air carrier Relief Pilot reported the Captain and flying First Officer were not climbing as instructed by ATC due to inadvertently being in altitude hold during departure.
Gulfstream flight crew reported hydroplaning during their landing rollout which led to a runway overrun.
Maintenance Technician reported confusing documentation in regards to Supplemental Oxygen Units.
Traffic Management Coordinator reported an Operations Manager assign a non certified Supervisor to work a sector during an aircraft emergency event.

A Flight Attendant (FA) noticed an ill passenger during the boarding process. An investigation revealed that the passenger had recently travelled to Africa. The Passenger was observed to be sweating and vomiting. The FA notified the Lead FA and Captain of the situation. A Complaint Resolution Official (CRO) was called and the passenger was removed from the flight. The reporter was concerned that no precautions were taken for the crew and that the aircraft was not sufficiently cleaned and disinfected upon arrival at the destination.
B747 flight crew reported that poor weather caused a diversion; and hazardous cargo without proper paperwork created an issue when parking.
A Tower Controller reported Tower Supervisors did not comply with runway use directives during a period of snow and freezing rain.

Air Carrier First Officer reported that on a coupled approach to the ILS Z RWY 8 BUR above the NA coupled altitude the aircraft was at half scale deflection right trending further right. First Officer reported disconnecting autopilot and correcting for an uneventful landing.
Air carrier flight crew and a Controller reported an unsafe situation where an air carrier and a sky diving aircraft became to close; resulting in a Resolution Alert for the carrier.
An Air Carrier First Officer descending on a Visual Approach reported receiving a GPWS alert even though in their present position they were at an appropriate altitude to avoid terrain.
EMB-190 Captain reported on descent to LGA he took evasive action from a VFR aircraft that was supposed to maintain visual separation.
Ramp personnel reported a B757 being parked at a gate that was not designed for that aircraft type.
ZOB Controller lost their main radio transmitters. The Controller was working two aircraft head on at the same altitude. Both aircraft responded to TCAS/RA alerts.
SCT Controller reported of targets jumping in a certain area on his radar scope. Facility is aware of the problem. Controller wants the problem fixed.
Ramp Agent reported issues with temperature check procedures at work and the company's lack of communication regarding employees that may have contracted COVID-19.

Air carrier Captain unfamiliar with MDPP noted that because of high terrain west of the airport; a pilot could fly the Runway 8 RNAV/GPS Arrival then fly from EMIVO direct to Runway 8 and pass 0.72 miles from the highest point.
Air carrier First Officer reported Chart+ software crashing and inaccurate information on the charts.
ZDC Controller reported having issued altitude below Minimum IFR Altitude to light transport.
B767 Captain reported that he has found crews carrying electrical cords with dual outlets; non-Apple chargers and using them in the aircraft cockpit.
GA pilot reported lighting at DVT airport is too bright at night and could cause loss of night vision.
Instructor pilot reported confusion with a similar fix name that caused the flight to be off course.
Flight Instructor reported violating Class B airspace after evading an NMAC.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict between an air carrier IFR aircraft on the RNAV 34L Approach to PAE airport and a VFR helicopter.
Citation pilot reported an altitude deviation due to a loud window leak noise interfering with radio communication and altitude warnings.
PA28 pilot reported a wake turbulence encounter with a military transport aircraft in the vicinity of MCN airport.
Challenger 300 flight crew reported filing and flying the Cottonwood Departure out of EGE which is not allowed by the company's policy.

A Dispatcher reported the Automated Delay alert once activated cannot be easily overridden and after an aircraft out time is established the flight's clock is running even at the gate which can affect FAR 117 crew legality as well as other company and FAA considerations.

General Aviation pilot who operates in the Anchorage Alaska terminal area reports complying with local Special Federal Air Regulations places aircraft in a position that they are unable to safely reach an airport to land in the case of an engine out landing.

A319 Dispatcher was informed of a possible bird strike during takeoff by one of his flights with no apparent ill effects. Airport personnel discovered aluminum debris at the end of the departure runway. The crew was informed and elected to continue. Post flight revealed missing pieces of acoustic liner on the number two engine.
Air carrier Captain reported significant safety compromises with wearing face masks during the COVID-19 pandemic and associated problems with oxygen masks.
A small aircraft pilot intercepting V21 southwest bound from HND was temporarily blinded by the Ivanpah Solar Field reflective glare.
CL350 Captain reported parachute jumpers landing outside published landing zone creating a safety hazard for arrivals and departures.
Air carrier Captain reported that the PAPIs on left and right side of Runway 28 at SBGL gave two different indications.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with company's actions relating to COVID-19 possible exposure.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and below the Minimim IFR Altitude.

An aircraft attempted two approaches to an airport but could not activate the airport/runway lights via Unicom frequency. The controller found out through the local Flight Service Station and Airport operator that the runway was closed due to snow on the runway and other equipment outages. The pilot stated none of these airport status issues were available to him from the Notams he checked prior to flying. The FSS also did not have this information in their Notams. The airport is in Canadian airspace.

SUX Tower Controller reported an aircraft taxied onto a closed taxiway due to the airport diagram chart not being updated and ATC being instructed not to include the taxiway change on the ATIS broadcast.

Air carrier Captain reported a complete loss of GPS signal in the vicinity of Manila (RPLL). This anomaly was observed both during the arrival and subsequent departure to the airport and was not mentioned in neither the NOTAM nor ATIS information.
B737 Captain reported concern that supplemental B737 Max Angle of Attack Disagree guidelines are contained within a Bulletin rather than the aircraft Quick Reference Handbook.
The pilot of a PA46 landed on a closed runway at a non-towered airport. Although the runway had an 'X' over the runway number; it was reported to have been very difficult to see.
A Boeing 777 Maintenance Technician reported that it was unsafe to install a broadband antenna without an overhead hoist.
Single pilot reported an airspace deviation occurred while attempting to get an IFR clearance. Pilot was unable to get an IFR clearance due to ATC workload and told to try again in 30 minutes.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a bird strike near rotation and the subsequent return to the departure airport minutes later.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that a washer was not installed after a main tire and wheel assembly was replaced; causing aircraft to be operated while un-airworthy.

ZLA Instructor and Developmental Controllers report a loss of separation due to an aircraft that had an autopilot failure. The aircraft advised ATC and the Developmental descended the aircraft out of RVSM airspace; but ended up less than the required 5 miles away from another aircraft.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported concern that minimum FAR staffing was inadequate for proper cabin operations.
PHL Tower Controller reported an aircraft was squawking 7500 when it actually had a radio problem. Reporter was concerned that the aircraft was allowed to land at a major airport.
A319 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant sustained minor injuries in a wake turbulence encounter in cruise flight at FL350.

EMB145 First Officer refused to continue flying an aircraft with both ACARS and the FMC on MEL after being told it would be taken out of service after return to base on the basis of safety of flight issues.

A11 Controller and A321 Captain reported a CFTT event occurred while the flight crew was flying the Anchorage 8 SID procedure. Controller stated aircraft on the Anchorage 8 SID often fly it incorrectly; resulting in low altitude alerts.  Controller suggests a SID redesign.
A300 Flight Crew reported they had a flap problem but continued to their destination then later discovered they were not in compliance with the MEL.
LAX Tower Controller reported of a loss of separation when a SMO departure was too close to an LAX departure.
Captain reported fatigued resulted in a go around due to aircraft not being properly configured.
HOU Tower Controller states that that he has been controlling for two weeks with wind indication inoperative due to parts availability.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported a disembarking passenger stumbled and fell through a space between the jet bridge and parked aircraft onto the pavement below.
A Helicopter pilot landing on private property reported their downwash blew a chair over which hit a bystander.
B787 Captain reported continuing to a long landing following speed deviations on short final following gusty winds and possible wake encounter.
B737 Captain reported the location of the Lima Pad at STL is incorrectly depicted on the 10-9 Airport Diagram.
P80 Approach Controller reported an unidentified VFR target not displaying a transponder created a conflict with several air carriers on final approach.
MD-11 flight crew reported that after loading UFOB into the FMS; a Vr mismatch message was received due to the UFOB information not being shared with the other FMS.
Captain of B737 reported the lack of visual cues needed to safely park the aircraft in the presence of close proximity ground equipment and material.
TUS Tower controller reported not noticing a rejected takeoff; due to heavy workload from combined sectors and lack of staffing; and landing traffic causing runway conflict with the RTO aircraft.
B757 Captain reported that an engine reverser has been deferred for an extended period. The Captain also stated the deferred issue reoccurs quite often despite maintenance action.
ZBW Controller reported having to work combined sectors with no assist position during busy complex traffic periods due to weather deviations and lack of controller staffing.
FWA TRACON Controller reported potential loss of separation due to a late handoff.
First Officer reported that a taxi driver driving recklessly and possibly under the influence caused the crew to be unfit for flight.
Two separate pilots reported communication issues with ATC while attempting to cancel IFR flight plans during approach to C09.
Air carrier First Officer reported not recognizing a charted speed restriction on departure out of DAY.
A Gate Agent reported being asked to operate the jet bridge to the aircraft during severe lightning.
SLC Airport Diagram Taxiway G is closed north of Taxiway V. Taxiway H also shifted west; north of Taxiway H11. Current NOTAMs do not reflect Taxiway G and Taxiway H changes.
A pilot reported they had to respond to an RA for known traffic that was restricted above their assigned altitude.
Flight Attendants reported COVID-19 seating related issues involving flight attendant pre-assigned seats.
B737 flight crew reported an abrupt stop during pushback due to tow pin failure resulted in a flight attendant injury.
Air carrier First Officer reported that while on the MSO RNAV Runway 30 approach; it was discovered the waypoint ROKNY is misspelled as RONKY on the Jeppesen chart.
Corporate jet flight crew reported a heading deviation while on the JAWBN SIX ARRIVAL to BFI.
Flight Attendant reported feeling pressured to sit in a cabin jumpseat near passengers assigned to company blocked seats. Flight Attendant was later informed of exposure to COVID-19.
Air carrier Captain questioned the legality of a new electronic tablet case that is magnetic and could affect the magnetic compass.
Captain reported accepting an overwater clearance that exceeded the 162NM limit due to inability to verify the route on charts or the FMS.

A B737 crew reported a ground taxiway incursion while taxiing for takeoff because the First Officer was heads down with checklist duties; out of the situational awareness loop and the Captain is not aware of checklist status.

PA-28 pilot successfully used all weather reports available to select best airport for landing to wait out passing thunderstorms. The pilot chose the airport because the ground station reported the airport as VFR clear. Hours later; the pilot discovered that the ground station had a NOTAM for being unreliable on cloud clearances.
ZSE Controller reported an aircraft flying below the Minimum IFR Altitude after relieving another controller.
A Boeing Captain reported the need for additional training on new checklist procedures.
PA28R pilot reported a NMAC with a medical helicopter while setting up for a flight maneuver.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving erratic signals from the CKB Runway 21 ILS while on approach in IMC. The crew disconnected the autopilot and the approach was completed uneventfully. Ground personnel mentioned to the Captain that several pilots referenced the irregular quality of the ILS signal at CKB.
B737 Captain reported his concerns with his company's new policy of entering weight and balance information into the FMC during taxi instead of prior to leaving the gate.
Airbus Captain reported cabin crew not sufficiently trained for Airbus safety procedures.
MD-83 Captain reported he felt his operational authority was being infringed upon while dealing with a mechanical issue at the gate prior to departure.

Flight crew reported that while on a night arrival to LFPG they were issued a new arrival by ATC. They had difficulty finding the new arrival because; recently Jeppesen Charting expanded the category APPROACH to include RNAV ARRIVALS and RNAV NIGHT ARRIVALS which are not approaches. Reportedly the RNAV; NIGHT NAV distinction appears arbitrary and confusing.

A B737 Captain reported the recent preflight checklist response changes contained some system setting and selection responses which were not completed or were inaccurate. This situation lead him to feel the policy was promoting a normalization of deviance.
SCT TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft went through the localizer and ended up in a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude then was issued.

A CLT TRACON Controller reported that he had issued an approach to an aircraft that reported the ILS out of service. The Controller thought the ILS was operational even though it was not because of information he observed on the National Information Display System (NIDS). The Controller reported the NIDS does not effectively show the status of each runway.
B737 flight crew reported that a wake turbulence encounter resulted in altitude; track; and speed deviations.
Air carrier Maintenance Technician reported Functional Flight Check not accomplished per Company Maintenance Manual requirement.
Cessna reported flat-spotting the main gear tires after applying brakes with too much force following a wake turbulence encounter from a B767 landing on a parallel runway.

A Captain flying the NAT C oceanic route westbound discovered the iPad JEPP PRO incorrectly depicted GAPLI as the Oceanic Gateway waypoint when DINIM at the EISN to EGGX airspace transition is the correct Gateway waypoint.
Air carrier First Officer reported a full bus of people on the employee bus which put everyone at risk of COVID-19 and increased risk for injury on the bus ride.
A321 Captain reported that the ferry flight checklist was inadequate; causing an unsecured galley cart to impact the forward bulkhead during landing.
Flight Instructor reported that the close proximity of several airports using the same CTAF causes frequency jamming.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a passenger's portable battery overheated so the Flight Attendant placed the battery in a water container as per company procedure.
Dispatcher reported having to work too many flights during shift.
Ground employee expressed concern that the aircraft pits are not being sanitized; potentially exposing workers to COVID-19.
Small aircraft pilot inadvertently entered a sporting event TFR; near BFI;  even though he was working with ATC and had previously checked the NOTAMs.
Ground employee reported concerns with being notified that someone he had contact with tested positive for COVID-19.
B737 flight crew reported similar sounding fixes on the SERFR STAR caused confusion and navigation to the incorrect fix.

A crew flying the ORD WATSN 2 RNAV VOLGR transition to ILS Runway 27L failed to selected the 281° heading at WATSN to intercept the localizer because the RNAV procedure did not indicate the transition. The transition was only on the ILS 27L chart.
Fractional Captain reported departing non-towered airport without IFR clearance.
Trainee Controller reported they observed an aircraft cleared for an approach fly off course and below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger sprayed Lysol on and around his seat.
Flight crew reported ATC commented on a missed crossing altitude that may have been a due to clearance confusion.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a Tower called them repeatedly on a direct line instead of a line through the loudspeaker while it was busy causing them to miss aircraft transmissions.
PWM TRACON Controller reported he observed a NMAC reported by an aircraft with an unidentified VFR target during a partial radar outage.

Captain reports about a B777 aircraft with a chronic history of Left Engine Bleed Trips after takeoff. He questions why the Left Engine Pressure Sensor that is causing all the in-flight Bleed trips remains deferred and not fixed? Also; why was the Left Sensor deferral NOT on the Pilot's Dispatch flight plan; instead of being hidden deep in the paperwork?
Center Controller reported they did not recognize the route an aircraft filed placed it below the Minimum IFR Altitudes.
Maintenance Technician reported the flight department communicated to pilots that maintenance is not to be involved in engine fuel leak check involving an engine idle run.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an airborne conflict with a UAV in the airport traffic pattern.

HCF TRACON Supervisor reported a go around and a departure from parallel runways were cleared to conflicting headings with one of the aircraft assigned an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported difficulty with their available tools increase their workload.
Center Controller reported an aircraft left its assigned altitude and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A ZJX Center Controller reported the Traffic Management Unit did not manage the flow to another sector causing Warning Area airspace violations and a traffic confliction during a period of weather deviations.

A Controller had two aircraft cleared to land on parallel runways. One aircraft was lined up with the wrong runway. Later; the same Controller was working Ground Control and received approval for an aircraft to cross the runways.
PA28 instructor reported three traffic conflicts requiring evasive action.

B737 Captain reported two pushback events at the same airport during which different tug drivers placed the aircraft's nose gear in a jack knifed position severe enough that a more experienced ramp agent was required to reposition the aircraft properly. Poor training was cited.
TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited traffic volume being very high for one Controller.

ZBW controller reported of a similar sounding fix that the pilot had entered and was flying towards. Aircraft was questioned and the confusion of the fix name was determined to be part of the problem. ATC had one clearance the crew had a different one.

Large Transport Aircraft Captain described a sports team charter that he believes highlights the company's lax attitude toward these operations in general and weight and balance calculations in particular.
Cessna 310 pilot reported TRACON advised him he had entered BUR Class C airspace.
Maintenance Technician reports Air Carrier tries to prevent maintenance personnel from completing safety reports through harassment and intimidation.
CDW Tower Controllers reported traffic off of nearby N07 airport entered Class D without communications; which conflicted with ATC controlled pattern traffic.
Air carrier First Officer reported a brief terrain warning while descending into a mountainous area airport in VMC conditions.
B747-400 First Officer and relief pilot reported executing a go-around after an altitude deviation on approach to WRI when the ILS signal malfunctioned.
B737-700 Captain reported having several FMC malfunctions and questioned whether the GPS MEL used in this event fully covered all the FMC issues encountered during this flight.
Air carrier First Officer reported FAR 117 violations due to a change in Company policies.
ZOB Controller observed a false aircraft target at 29;000 feet in the path of another enroute aircraft.
A Center trainee controller reported they waited too long to climb an aircraft to a higher altitude to stay above the Minimum IFR Altitude for its route.
B777 First Officer reported that the flight had difficulty with Dispatch in locating their position with the company system.
ZLC Center Controller reported the need to delay issuing IFR departure clearances due to the sector being overloaded with traffic with no Assist position open due to lack of staffing.

A CL604 Captain reported an airborne conflict after receiving clearance for an ILS approach. The reporting party stated that the airspace is poorly designed to protect the ILS approach and should be reviewed.
A Flight Attendant reported on some confusion about whether a Portable Oxygen Concentrator was FAA approved.
Air Carrier First Officer reported their flight had been rerouted but the reroute had never been passed to the crew; causing confusion when they checked in with Potomac Departure.
Pilot reported that ATC issued a terrain clearance warning while aircraft was turning in the vicinity of mountains.
Corporate Captain reported that the published NOTAM was ambiguous using two airports rather than one.
Air Carrier Captain reports Departure procedure does not specify correct departure runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported having a problem communicating with Tower Controller at RDU because of a NOTAM'd outage of the frequency.

Air carrier Captain reported a disabled aircraft on intended landing runway resulted in their flight being vectored away from their approach path.  The additional vectoring caused them to be concerned with their fuel state and made a request to be cleared as soon as possible.  The crew was cleared for another approach and landing and reported being unstable/high before safely landing the aircraft.
Dispatcher reported having to work too many aircraft due to staffing related issues after several dispatchers called in sick.
Ground employee reported wanting to know why the airline is using aircraft that do not have a functioning Auxiliary Power Unit.
Flight Attendant reported that another Flight Attendant refused to occupy the appropriate jumpseat due to social distancing concerns.
A Controller In Charge also working control positions in the Tower reported not noticing the Local Controller clear an aircraft for takeoff with a vehicle on the active runway.

An aircraft was cleared for a Visual Approach. The pilot made the approach to the wrong airport to a closed runway. Pilot narrative states the airport was closed however the airport was not closed only the runway they made the approach to.

Flight crew received a late runway and arrival change to LAX. When they programed the new data into their flight computers they experienced difficulty determining the final published altitude. They attributed this difficulty to a design flaw in the way altitude restrictions were depicted on the chart.

Pilot reported going to the de-ice pad; but not knowing the de-ice pad they were supposed to go to was further down the taxiway. Reporter claims the further east de-ice pad is not displayed on the DTW 10-9 page.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach into ATL.
A B737-800 Maintenance Technician reported an MEL is confusing when relating to 'MINIUM MANEUVER SPEED' Indication.
B747 Captain reported extended maintenance delay and extreme heat prior to departure leading to exhaustion and a fatigue call.
Technician reported concerns with company procedures related to notification of possible exposure to other employees who have tested positive for COVID-19.
Tower Controller reported the facility was not staffed sufficiently resulting in the Controller having to work a sector longer than the mandated time on position.

Captain reported contradictory and confusing information contained in the PSP approach charts and the airport diagram regarding the use of Taxiway C as temporary Runway 13R. This resulted in an unstable approach while correcting to the temporary runway.

A TRACON Departure Controller reported two aircraft flew dangerously close conflicting courses due to one aircraft taking the instruction of another aircraft with a nearly identical call sign departing at the same time.

Flight Attendant observed two passengers wearing face masks who were seated by exits and asked them to remove the masks during takeoff and landing in the event they needed be heard while giving commands during an emergency.

A CRJ-700 First Officer noted a specific aircraft's FMS loaded both north and south JAWBN STAR waypoints with discontinuities even though only the north transition was selected. This aircraft's FMS software is apparently unique to this aircraft and creates an additional distracting workload.
Technician reported that confusion over a bleed valve part number caused an incorrect part to be installed.

EUG TRACON Controller reported being distracted by a data block on the wrong target and failed to realize traffic he thought was a departure was conducting a Missed Approach. Controller allowed it to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

An aircraft was in a Controller's sector with only a 'limited' data block displayed. The Controller did not notice the target or realize the aircraft was on their frequency and flying through their airspace.
Center Controller reported that an aircraft that did not display a radar data block flew through a corner of the airspace without coordination.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns regarding the products used to disinfect the cabin.
ZJX Center Controllers reported sector workload was excessive due to lack of effective traffic flow management during a period of weather deviations.
Flight Attendant reported that Embraer ERJ Captain left the cockpit momentarily while on the taxiway; leaving the cockpit door unsecured.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft repair was accomplished in violation of proper procedure to avoid affecting aircraft schedule.

Flight attendants are now required by the Company to carry iPads to perform preflight and safety duties. The Flight Attendant reported that there is no place to store them near the jump seats or on their person.
Airbus Captain reported experiencing an uncommanded heading change and navigation display disruption due to GPS signal interference.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a publication date discrepancy on an approach chart.
Flight Attendant reported that the Braille Safety Guides installed on the aircraft lack Covers; Table of Contents; and are printed in an obsolete format and require upgrade.
Technician reported an improper windshield change lead to aircraft's inability to pressurize.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstablized approach and cited fatigue; time pressure and reduced flying as contributing factors.
Maintenance Technician reported discovering wrong part listed in Illustrated Parts Catalog; error was fixed and correct part was installed instead.
B767-300 flight crew reported a bird strike on takeoff roll; resulting in aircraft damage.
B757 Captain reported a track deviation on a holding pattern that was not depicted on the fix and not visible during the close-in EFB view.
Air carrier Captain reported a charting issue with Taxiway T2 at BNA.
Helicopter pilot reported SGF airport was reporting incorrect weather information due to possible equipment failure.
Air carrier Captain reported runway and taxiways signs at MMUN Airport were not illuminated at night.
Air carrier Captain reported the KAENA2 RNAV Arrival to ILS Runway 8L at HNL shows MAKOA to be crossed between 3;400 and 3;700 feet while the ILS 8L shows above 3;300 feet at MAKOA.

ZFW Controller reported of not issuing an aircraft 'resume the arrival' clearance. The aircraft had been left on a heading and the Controller didn't realize it until later. Controller tried to point out aircraft and advise sector; but someone else appeared to be talking to the other Controller about the aircraft in question. Controller did not issue correct instructions to aircraft leading to a LOA and airspace violation.
Pilot reported the AWOS at OAR airport was not reporting accurate weather.
Air carrier First Officer noted his company's iPad charts do not include suitable emergency diversion airports in the area between DEN and SLC.
Air carrier Captain reported unsafe parking procedures occurred when pulling into the gate.
A319 flight crew reported being low altitude alert on approach due to heavy workload on final.

B737-700 flight crew reported the crew oxygen pressure dropped significantly during the preflight procedure. The valve was found to not be fully open; however the signoff did not indicate so; and the delay was charged to the crew.

A Dispatcher reported the Lido weather forecast process apparently does not update to current conditions if an airport is already below Lido minimums during the dispatch process. A destination change to below minimums was discovered on a National Weather Service forecast.
Technician reported confusion over a check interval required on a component not being present in a check package.
B737 Captain reported the flight crew did not receive an important message via ACARS from the company until airborne.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reports that company violates procedures regarding security seals and stickers.
A300 flight crew reported a MEL discrepancy regarding an inoperative interphone system; resulting in maintenance action.

Air carrier Captain reported that FAR has a new taxiway that has been open for several months; but is still not shown on the airport diagrams (NACO and Jeppesen). The new taxiway connects the terminal ramp to Runway 9 at Taxiway C5.
Flight Attendant reported being told by Gate Agents and Managers to allow catering to bring carts down the aisle after passengers are boarded in violation of company safety policy.
GA pilot reported a NMAC with a powered parachute at 52F airport. Reportedly; this is a common occurrence around this airport.
A Ramp Agent reported an Ground Power Cable hoist broke and fell next to a ramp employee narrowly missing him.
ZSE Center Controller reported that an aircraft deviated from its assigned vector into a higher MIA area.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at cruise altitude from a crossing A320.

An air carrier crew failed to select the TCAS TA Mode on approach to DEN Runway 16L. After a RA; the First Officer remembered the Company TA Mode policy. He reminded the Captain who selected TA and continued the approach.
ZKC Center Controller reported that an aircraft with no transponder caused a NMAC.
Pilot reported missing the OAKLAND FOUR SID restriction due to published procedure design.

B737 flight crew reported executing a missed approach due to confusion over which runway was assigned to his aircraft. The confusion was attributed to ATC workload and communication issues exacerbated by radio congestion.

After intercepting the glideslope and localizer for Runway 28R at ORD; a Captain experienced a dramatic pitch over as the autopilot attempted to follow a rapidly descending glideslope indicator. The reporter disconnected the autopilot and commenced a climb back to the appropriate altitude at the same time the Tower issued a low altitude alert. Tower indicated that a heavy jet entering Runway 28R was the likely cause of the signal disruption.

ZHU Center Controller and AUS TRACON Controller reported two aircraft conflicted with each other due to a different interpretation of their responsibilities according to the LOA between the facilities.
Air carrier Captain reported a Runway 22 localizer anomaly at LIT airport.
DH8-400 Captain reported difficulty understanding the MEL procedure and the need for instruction.
B737 Maintenance Technician reported they did not have the proper diagnostic tool to test the air conditioning system as required.
Air carrier ramp agent reported that aircraft parking procedures do not have adequate safety constraints.
B737 Captain reported a cabin fire extinguisher leaked out all its pressure.
A320 First Officer reported that the aircraft was dispatched with numerous MELs which caused extra workload for the flight crew.
Dispatch is not being informed of changes in fuel loads.
B737NG First Officer reported new checklist procedures increased their workload while conducting a Bleeds Off takeoff from a busy airport.
PAO Tower Controller in Charge reported the adjacent Tower facility would not delegate needed airspace to them so aircraft experienced departure delays.

The pilot of a Caravan with a TKS system reported concern over the lack of sufficient fluid supply to provide options during icing conditions. In addition; the pilot commented on TKS odor and effects.
Air Carrier pilot reported their initial request for extra fuel was denied by Dispatch which was not in accordance with Company Policy.
ZBW Center Controller reported not having enough controllers on duty to open all the sectors and their workload reached unwanted levels.

B737-800 flight crew reported being informed by the lead Flight Attendant of an overheated battery charger. Fifteen minutes later he was informed that some of the flight attendants were experiencing headaches and nausea and the Captain elected to divert to a suitable airport.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a GPWS terrain waring while on a visual approach to GEG Runway 21. Terrain awareness had been briefed.
An A320 pilot reported receiving a GPWS alert while on ATC vectors and descending to an ATC assigned altitude.
ERJ-145 Captain reported that a bird strike on takeoff resulted in a precautionary landing.

B737-800 First Officer reported a possible bird strike at 2;000 feet in the left engine during climb out causing a popping noise and some vibration. An odor is also detected and the crew elected to return to the departure airport.
Flight attendant reported several passengers not properly wearing face masks.
A Center Controller reported attempting to climb an aircraft entering higher terrain; but lost radio contact with the aircraft.

After a commuter flight crew landed at UNV and were taxiing clear of the runway; they were queried by operations personnel if Center 'had advised us of the 15 minute PPR.' They told him that they had not been advised. They were unable to find the NOTAM and the Center controller was also unaware of the NOTAM.
B737MAX Captain reported confusion regarding switch function and display annunciations related to 'poor training and even poorer documentation'.
SCT Controller reported a known recurrent anomaly; which renders the Downey sector frequency unusable. Maintenance has failed to fix this.

Air carrier First Officer reported a bright billboard with flashing lights on the final approach to Runway 18R at MEM. The billboard is very distracting and about one mile from the end of the runway along I-240 facing north.
Air carrier Maintenance employee reported adverse health symptoms after exposure to an aircraft just cleaned using electrostatic spraying.
Flight Crew reports confusion with intersection naming same as navaid.
Pilot reported the company provided face masks do not fit the reporter's face; causing fogging of glasses.

Flight Attendant reported that her company's A321s have only two aft cabin jumpseats; but during flight three flight attendants work in the aft. When turbulence was encountered a Flight Attendant was without a jumpseat and harness.
EMB-145 Captain reported they did not receive localizer signal until 3 miles from the FAF. Reporter stated this was a common occurrence on this approach at EWR.
A300 Captain reported the aircraft pitched over abruptly following a false glide slope; resulting in a low altitude alert from the Tower.
Pilot reported miscommunication with ATC; causing ground conflict and a possible airborne conflict when preparing for takeoff.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported the overhead bins being heavy to close due to weight of numerous heavy bags in the bin.
A300 flight crew reported refusal to transport HAZMAT cargo due to faulty HAZMAT container.

An Atlanta TRACON (A80) Controller reports of having to use two different radar systems at one position to monitor traffic safely. The reporter states that it is stressful on all of the controllers involved.
Helicopter pilot reported a large bird struck the aircraft shattering the canopy.
ZBW Controller reported high volume of traffic and not enough people to work the sectors correctly.
MRI Tower Controller reported the Approach Control was not complying with an automated point out procedure causing unnecessary delay and flight time over water for aircraft.
Dispatcher reported that Maintenance Operations authorized a non check Captain to do a dual engine change In flight Functional Check; who was unsure of requirements for this type of flight.
ZJX Center Controller reported the facility controlling Restricted/Warning Areas approved point outs for weather deviations than called back to tell them to exit the areas for traffic.
ZOB Controller reported problems associated with reading and understanding NOTAMs that may or may not be pertinent to the operation.
A pilot makes commentary as to the challenges with RNAV descents for aircrews.
An ERJ145 First Officer reported they declined to take an aircraft with a fuel leak in spite of pressure from management to fly it.
An air carrier Captain at 4;000 feet near LEE reported numerous hang gliders at or near his altitude which posed a collision hazard. ATC had no information about the glider activity.
Air carrier crew reported confusion about DAL Taxiway Q versus P after pushback from the terminal north side gates.
Air carrier Non Flying First Officer reported a near taxiway incursion due to Jeppsen 10-9B chart not depicting the island at spot as is depicted on 10-9.
As an A320 approached a known 7;840 foot obstacle on the MMGL ILS 10 approach; the EGPWS alerted CAUTION; TERRAIN nearing the 8;400 foot descent constraint.
SEA Local Controller reported an aircraft executing a missed approach was involved in an NMAC with another aircraft on final approach to SEA.
Maintenance Technician reported that the company did not follow the manufacturer's guidelines concerning loading passengers while maintenance was performed on the aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported a row that is typically blocked for Flight Attendant use was released for passenger seating.
A B737-700 Captain taxied to very end of SJC Runway 30L on Taxiway A1 at which point both pilots noted how close the wingtip passed by the blast fence. Runway 30R was closed for construction.
C402 Captain reported his aircraft was rolled into a 90-degree bank from wake turbulence at 300 feet AGL on approach into BOS 4 miles in trail of an A340.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger who had previously violated the face mask policy was allowed to board this flight.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering fatigue due to flying schedule.

Due to a radar outage; K90 TRACON was receiving their radar information from ZBW ARTCC. The ARTCC has to make a data entry of the current altimeter for the TRACON airspace yet the ARTCC did not realize this requirement. Therefore the Mode C readouts of aircraft in the TRACON airspace were 400 feet off from the actual altitude of the aircraft.
Air Cargo Captain reported that after landing on a runway contaminated with sand the aircraft filled with dust that had been ingested by the packs following use of reverse thrust.

A320 flight crew reported encountering a bird strike at high speed during the takeoff roll. The takeoff was continued; however a significant vibration in one engine was noted. They reduced power on the affected engine and safely returned to the departure airport; where major damage to the engine was discovered.

Captain reported consistently receiving a clearance route different from the one normally filed by company Dispatch. Captain recommended that the normal route filed by Dispatch be changed to that assigned by ATC.
AS350 Technician reported that the tail rotor was not properly inspected after it encountered debris from landing in a grassy field.
N90 Controller vectored an aircraft for spacing on the final approach course into the path of an aircraft in the adjacent sector.
B767 First Officer laments the lack of information provided by his company on the location of volcanic ash departing SCEL and the company's response to his earlier report on the subject.

ARTCC Controller reported of a staffing issue on a particular day. No overtime was called in and the sectors were combined at times in ways the reporter had never seen before. Reporter thought it was due to short staffing and the micromanagement of the Operations Manager that lead to all of the problems.
B767 Maintenance Technician reported that an incorrect O-ring was installed on the engine's oil Magnetic Chip Detector.
Captain reported experiencing intermittent GPS/ADS-B signal interruption during ILS approach.
GA pilot reported parachuting activity in the vicinity of E60 airport near a private airport that is not located on aviation charts.
Pilot reported the rotating beacon on top of the new JLN Control Tower is hard to see at night.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger complained about another passenger coughing and sneezing on the flight and not wearing a face mask.
Air carrier Captain reported about the limits of single FMC/ACARS operations.
Maintenance technician reported that during a landing gear tire change; the aircraft was hoisted using incorrect jacks; resulting in a safety risk.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with being in close contact with a Flight Attendant that had called in sick the day before for COVID-19 like symptoms.
Air carrier flight crew reported a GPWS activation on short final to JAN at night in marginal VFR conditions.
Air carrier pilot reported asymmetrical thrust on start of takeoff roll resulting in a rejected takeoff.
A320 Captain reported that with the maintenance history concerning a system that was deferred he did not accept the aircraft.
B767 Captain stated he feels his company's policy of flying some East Coast to Europe North Atlantic flights with just two pilots is unsafe.
SFO Tower Controller reported a radio 'dead spot' identified at the approach end of Runway 1R that continually affects lower profile jets when in position on the runway.
A Center Controller recieved a handoff from the TRACON of an aircraft below their Minimum IFR Altitude.
MKC Tower Controller reported receiving an aircraft on a Visual Approach descending into a conflict with VFR traffic.
C-182T Pilot Flying reported a possible encounter with hypoxia.
Pilot reported receiving a low altitude report from ATC while on published SID.
B777 Captain reported acrid cabin odor during cruise most likely due to sulfur dioxide gas from a volcano eruption in the vicinity.
A P80 TRACON Controller reported vectoring an arriving air carrier off its approach to avoid conflicting unidentified VFR traffic.
EMB-175 First Officer reported observing a UAV near their course while on short final.
A TRACON Controller reported a photo mission aircraft flying an unsafe route was allowed to proceed through the airspace.
Maintenance Technician reported the C560XL Maintenance Manual and Illustrated Parts Catalog are not clear on what parts are required or procedures when replacing elevator trim cables.
B737 First Officer reports his air carrier's Before Takeoff Checklist is too long and beginning the checklist late into the taxi flow means something is bound to be missed.
While replacing the #1 and #2 main landing gear wheel assemblies on a commercial aircraft; the technicians did not find any washer under the axle nut for either wheel assembly.

MD-80 Captain reported a main cabin door problem was not documented by the off going crew and entered the discrepancy in the aircraft maintenance log. A mechanic verified the problem; but the Supervisor overrode the decision resulting in the crew refusing the aircraft after enduring pilot pushing and threats from maintenance.

Captain lamented the training he received for new procedures instituted by his company in order to mirror the procedures of a main line carrier. He also does not believe that the CRJ200 to CRJ900 differences training is adequate.
ATC and B767 pilots reported a security threat from a passenger that prompted an air return.

A supervisor did not meet their Area currency requirements and did not realize it. The Operations Manager did not advise the supervisor of their currency default. The supervisor worked for several months without meeting currency requirements.
B757 Captain reported the need to remind pilots it is in violation of the FAR's to text and fly. The Captain suggests the company shut off the free Wi-Fi inflight.

A Honolulu Control Facility (HCF) Controller reports of losing a data tag with an aircraft on a 9 mile final. The Controller coordinates with tower; but doesn't know why the data tag went missing. The Controller assumes someone dropped the data tag because of a different flight with the same call sign.
Pilot reported confusion between the difference in Out of Service and Unserviceable in NOTAMs.
Maintenance reported that 'unserviceable' tags were removed from several tow bars and; the tow bars were put back into service without being repaired.
Ground operations employee reported that Boeing 737 flight crews are told not to write up engine leaks.
Air Carrier Captain reported a ramp incursion due to inadequate ramp chart.

B747 Relief Pilot described a bird strike on takeoff that damaged the number four engine. After consulting with the company the crew elects to return to the departure airport after dumping fuel down to maximum landing weight.
MRI Tower Controller reported LOA problems with the overlying A11 TRACON.
Flight Attendant reported the gate agents tried to board the passengers right after the aircraft was sanitized; disregarding the mandatory wait time.
Flight Test crew experienced engine fire warning after takeoff due to borescope plugs not being installed before flight.
PHL Tower Controller states his facility Letter of Agreement with Ramp Control is not being complied with and is unsafe.
Dispatcher reported confusion over a Dispatcher desk being closed and who was responsible for certain flights.

Air carrier First Officer reported chronic anomalies with domestic clearances (KUSA) obtained via the Harris CPDLC-DCL (Direct Clearance) system which resulted in incomplete or erroneous route clearances on more than one occasion.

TPA TRACON Controller reported of losing a data block along with the primary target. The aircraft was in conflict with the downwind traffic. Reporter advised the FLM; who told him this had happened several times before he took the position. Controller would like this looked into to avoid it from happening again.
General aviation pilot reported severe interference from a commercial radio station in the vicinity of PGV airport affecting communication with Washington Center and PGV Unicom.

A pilot reported that the Tower Controller advised them they penetrated an adjacent Class C airspace. The pilot states according to his GPS and reviewing his route on an aviation software program they did not enter the Class C airspace.
B737 Captain reported refusing an aircraft that had come out of maintenance with flight control anomalies.

Air carrier Captain reported that the crew was transported to the aircraft in the same bus as the passengers to a remote hard stand; and that a separate bus would lessen the risk of exposure to COVID-19.
B737-800 flight crew reported striking a large flock of birds during takeoff; resulting in damage to the Number 2 engine; and an immediate return to the departure airport.
Maintenance Technician reported air carrier released aircraft after replacement of critical flight control items without a pre-revenue check flight.
Air carrier Captain reported that one Controller was working combined frequencies during low visibility conditions at the airport. Reporter stated safety concerns with the situation.
While accomplishing preventive maintenance on an aircraft recovery kit Maintenance found that the airbags were out of time limits.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported navigation charts had incorrect and out-of-date information.
Center Controller reported they forgot to climb an aircraft in a timely manner and it flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
An air carrier being vectored onto the final approach to DAY took evasive action to avoid a VFR aircraft not displaying a transponder or in communication with TRACON.
Customer Service Representative reported a crowded and unsafe area in the lobby; which did not allow for proper social distancing.
Maintenance Technician reported that a Task Card is missing a second Signature Block.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported difficulty intercepting the ORD Runway 27R localizer due to a misprogrammed FMS. At the same moment; an issue with the airport's ILS system prevented them from reintercepting the localizer.
Air carrier pilot reported that climb gradients to DRSET should be published for Runways 13L and 13R on four SIDs at DAL.
B737-800 flight crew reported during departure they noticed a smoky odor after a bird strike. The flight returned to departure airport. After landing; damage to #1 engine fan blade was found.
A B777 Captain reported that the new procedure as to when to use the landing checklist has increased the workload during approach.
A330 Captain reported they lost communication with ATC and did not realize it until they observed unidentifiable traffic near their aircraft.
A320 Captain reported questioning the engine out missed approach procedures specified by his company which requires the SFO VOR which was NOTAMed out of service.
B737 First Officer reported a rejected takeoff after the red runway lights illuminated when the power was advanced.
ATC Center controller reported vectoring several aircraft for sequencing to an airport unnecessarily due to faulty instructions from the Traffic Management Unit.
MAF Controller reported VFR aircraft were allowed to conduct operations in an IFR Class C airspace.
An MD-80; prepared to push back; was evacuated at the gate after the flight attendants reported smoke in the cabin. Maintenance believed the APU was the smoke's source.

The flight crew of a MD11 reported that they departed with an aircraft with an MEL item that prohibited them from entering icing conditions. It turned out; the weather was not as forecast and they had to deviate several times to avoid icing.

B777 Flight Officer mentions improper flight planning policy with regards to terrain clearance/avoidance for Depressurization scenario due to high terrain along their route in South America. Crew contacts Dispatch while enroute asking for a new route to properly avoid terrain in case of a depressurization issue and is left on their own to file a new route after Dispatcher and on duty Chief Pilot question the flight crew's reasoning. New route is filed by crew and accepted by Colombian ATC some 3 hours later.
S46 TRACON Controller reported their sector reached an unsafe workload level during a runway change operation because they were working combined sectors.

An Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) pilot reported his aircraft and a law enforcement helicopter had a near miss at 550 feet even though his UAS computerized flight plan's altitude restriction was 400 feet. The QNH was 30.40 at the time.

B747 flight crew reported loss of Number 1 hydraulic system in cruise after being dispatched with Number 1 engine driven hydraulic pump on MEL. Flight crew returned to departure airport with limited flaps.
B777-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding wide body aircraft in cruise flight over the North Atlantic.
SR22 pilot landing #6 in a flight of 7 reported encountering 'very significant' wake turbulence during the landing flare.
A TRACON Supervisor reported a light aircraft in a satellite airport airspace reported a wake vortex encounter with a Super aircraft on approach to another airport.
A Center Controller reported they had difficulty communicating with an aircraft that lost an engine and was diverting due to poor radio coverage at low altitudes in their sector.
Flight Attendant reported problems with procedures and company safety culture that could result in a safety issue.
ZLA Center Controller reported a radar site was producing radar track jumps and dual radar targets on their radar displays.
B737 First Officer reported his concerns with what he sees as an inadequate recurrent training program at his airline.

A B737 Captain reported that when he refused to fly an aircraft to SNA because of heavy light aircraft traffic and an inoperative TCAS his company 'shopped' the aircraft to other flight crews hoping someone would take it.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported difficulty communicating with a flight that was experiencing an engine problem and was returning to departure airport.

Flight Attendant reported having to explain to a passenger that there were no exemptions to having to wear a face mask.  Passenger and two others traveling with him were not compliant during the flight.
EMB-135LR First officer reported an NMAC event during arrival.
Vehicle Driver reported striking and injuring a pedestrian.
Air carrier Captain reported questioning Company Management's policy of not reporting certain aircraft maintenance issues.
Air carrier flight crew reported being out of contact for ten minutes in Fukuoka airspace due to a late hand off from Manila ATC and an error in ATC frequency information in the Jepp-Pro application.

A Dispatcher reported his company's delay activation authority to many users/stations in addition to Dispatch is a possible FAR violation regarding control of a flight's progress by other than Dispatch. The procedure has safety implications.
Medium transport Captain reported losing contact with ATC for approximately 4 minutes shortly after takeoff from EWR.
Airbus Captain reported receiving a SMOKE AFT CARGO ECAM during deplaning. It was later determined the alarm was caused by insecticide sprayed into the compartment by ground personnel.
ZAB Controller reported an E175 11 miles behind a B737-700 encountered wake turbulence causing a 35 degree un-commanded roll.

A Dispatcher reported a flight crew checked their communications to Dispatch via the Iridium SATLINK system while on a polar route over ABERI intersection. The crew's message took 50 minutes to arrive at Dispatch whereupon the Dispatcher immediately issued a reply to the aircraft.
