Twenty minutes after the Dispatcher sent the reply; the crew sent back confirmation that they had received the Dispatch reply.
B787 flight crew reported that the information for two deferrals for the same system were very confusing.
Air carrier ramp employee reported becoming ill after cleaning up a hydraulic spill without using a respirator or mask.

B777 flight crew reported being unable to receive a rereleased fuel required at their planned redispatch point due to the fact that the flight planning software was currently down. The crew was told to continue toward their destination and the required fuel was finally sent 16 minutes past the redispatch point.

A TRACON Controller reported they had to cancel an aircraft's approach clearance and subsequently the aircraft responded to an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.
B747 First Officer reported a course deviation resulted when the ANC 07R localizer signal displayed anomalies. Reporter stated he has seen this issue in the past.
An air carrier First Officer on an ILS final approach reported responding to a RA and taking evasive action to avoid an unidentified converging VFR aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported that re-qualification training was inadequate.
A Center Controller reported they observed an aircraft fail to intercept an airway and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

S46 Approach Controller reported a conflict between an air carrier on an ILS approach to BFI and unidentified VFR traffic crossing the final approach course. Controller immediately turned the air carrier to the right in order to resolve the conflict. Reporter stated this is a common occurrence with VFR aircraft transiting a very busy and narrow corridor without communication with ATC.

GA pilot reported during last snow and ice event in the Midwest many smaller airports were not listed as closed on the NOTAM list even though flying over many he confirmed they were indeed closed. The pilot reported selecting a larger airport that was reporting conditions.

An Air Carrier crew flying the SLC NORDK3 RNAV arrival had a track deviation because of confusion about the two D Ball Notes with separate instructions to either Runway 16L or Ruways 16R; 17. The SKEES3 Arrival caused the same confusion.
EMB-135 flight crew reported that due to a communication error between pilots and ATC there was confusion when instructions stated to join the approach not clearance for the approach.
ZMA Center Controller reported managing aircraft conflicts caused by difficulty coordinating with adjacent airspace.
NCT TRACON Controller used insufficient spacing for an Opposite Direction Operation at SCK.
A320 flight crew reported sighting a UAV in the vicinity of TEB at 3;300 feet.
B767-300 Captain reported incorrect bulbs were installed in cockpit switches; causing lights to illuminate incorrectly and requiring a diversion.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reports that a Maintenance Manual procedure is unsafe.
Air Carrier pilot reported the company failed to update aircraft navigation database.
B737 NG Captain reported diverting to an alternate when the crew oxygen pressure dropped below 500 PSI while enroute.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft they had just shipped to Tower went around in response to a TCAS/RA for an unidentified VFR target in the vicinity of the Final Approach course.
ZZZ Center Controller reported that an adjacent area went ATC Zero; resulting in a complex and difficult traffic situation.
B737 Captain reported that the navigation database in the FMS was in conflict with the approach plates for La Aurora Airport (MGGT).
First Officer reported discovering a box with arrows pointing down when they should have been up. The box was labeled 'FLAMMABLE LIQUID' and 'CARGO AIRCRAFT ONLY.'

Phenom 300 Captain reported a serious degradation of his ability to navigate due to suspected GPS jamming while descending into SLC. Captain received radar vectors from ATC to the airport; however; they experienced several RAs from an undetermined source during the approach.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT in trail of an A321.

Corporate jet with passengers attempted a landing at an uncontrolled airport with heavy traffic in the pattern. Additionally; windshear conditions existed prompting a go-around. Because of the heavy workload there was a lapse in the coordination with ATC.
Passengers started boarding before the flight attendants were ready for them.
Tower Controller reported inadvertently giving an incorrect heading to a departing aircraft resulting in a conflict with an aircraft on final.
PA46 single pilot reported an uneventful diversion due to an in-flight encounter during cruise with an unknown object resulting windshield damage.

An EC-135 pilot reported when using Night Vision Goggles (NVG) and flying an aircraft which requires the instrument panel night vision external light shielding; the chin bubble mat blocks all downward vision thus obstructing obstacles during steep landing descents. The glareshield curtain permits downward vision but also filters the light from the instrument panels.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that Dispatchers were not advised by their company of missile launches which could have affected the safety of flights operating near the launch area.
Air carrier Captain reported having a passenger who was coughing throughout the flight.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported concerns with crew rest facility restrictions during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Two dispatchers reported requesting an additional Dispatch desk be opened for late night Caribbean departures and arrivals due to forecasted weather; but the requests were ignored by management.
B737 First Officer reported the #1 engine and some leading edge devices were damaged from a bird strike on approach.
A Maintenance Technician reported that the Auxiliary Power Unit was serviced with Turbo Oil 2197.
Air carrier Captain reported being given multiple schedule changes in a short period of time; which caused fatigue and distraction.

A CVG Controller reports of losing a total loss of electrical power. The Controller attempts to use the backup radio but it does not work. The Controller plugs the backup radio into a different outlet and it then works. The Controller reports that he/she is appalled at the lack of maintaining the equipment at the airport.
B737 flight crew reported a track heading deviation during approach to MCO airport.  Similar sounding fix names CRUZE and HUWES were cited as a contributing factor.

B-747 crew; after checking enroute charts and querying ATC; discovered that the holding pattern in their FMC was incorrect. Crew used manual heading to enter the holding pattern and iPad information to reprogram the FMC. Crew mentioned a lack of training in locating holding data in the iPad.

PA31 pilot and local FBO at a non-towered airport heard no transmissions on CTAF by a Gulfstream V aircraft holding short of the runway nor from a landing commuter jet. Reporter expressed concern with the practice of corporate and commuter aircraft communicating only with the local ATC Center and not utilizing CTAF.
B737 Captain reported on arrival into BOS he received 3 reroutes in 5 minutes. Due to unfamiliarity with the routing he requested help with the fixes and was told to look it up himself.
Captain reported that Company DGS [Docking Guidance System] system is not recording flight time correctly for FAR compliance.
Maintenance employee reported that employee buses are full and social distancing on them is not being properly addressed.
A student at a flight school reported that the school's aircraft are not maintained in a safe condition.
B737 First Officer reported experiencing a TCAS Resolution Advisory which resulted in a Go-Around from his approach to Runway 27 at San Diego.
Air Carrier Captain reported concern with overweight and oversized baggage being allowed on the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported a medical issue that caused a large distraction for the pilots.  The reporter recommended that Medlink calls be handled by the cabin crew.
Dispatcher reported that a flight was delayed without notification of the Dispatcher.
CRJ-200 Maintenance Technician reported that allotted time is insufficient to allow safe and proper inspections on the aircraft fleet.

Air carrier flight crew reported encountering multiple delays as passengers and crew members deplaned and went through a foreign country's required COVID-19 protocols. Crew also reported concerns with the layover accommodations.
A11 TRACON Controller reported a low altitude notification for an aircraft flying the KNIK2 SID out of ANC airport.
A Piper Arrow made a gear-up landing on a highway due to an engine failure. The engine failed due to loss of oil because of an incorrect 'quick drain' oil plug installed.
MD11 First Officer reported accepting the aircraft and flew with 13 listed MELs.
B787 Captain reported that an MEL weight penalty resulted in a maintenance action and flight cancellation.

The Local Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff. The crew of the aircraft told the Controller they would wait because it appeared a taxiing aircraft had not cleared the runway. The Controller's ASDE-X (Ground Radar) indicated the taxiing aircraft was clear of the runway.

B737-800 Captain reported that; according to ATC; during descent the aircraft was 700 feet high over a crossing restriction of 8000 feet. The Captain stated the aircraft was in VNAV PATH the whole descent with no scratchpad advisories.
G550 flight crew were knowingly given an improper count of adult and infant passengers by the aircraft owner in order to accommodate more adult passengers than the crew would have accepted.
Large Turbojet flight crew reported a taxiway excursion at LFPG resulting in damage to two main wheels.
A pilot reported a Ground Controller scolded him on frequency for not complying with their taxi instructions in an expeditious manner.
Small aircraft pilot reported mislabeled SAV airport runways 1 and 19 on the Garmin G3X database.
EMB-135 Captain reported a traffic conflict with a helicopter near the final approach corridor.
ZSU Controller reported a loss of separation and an NMAC even though they complied with the rules in the FAAH 7110.65.
Air Carrier pilot reported confusion during taxi due to inaccurate airport diagram.
Flight attendants reported being forced by management to continue a flight after voicing safety concerns due to an inoperative keypad for the flight-deck door.

NCT Departure Control Coordinator reported a departure contacted the TRACON on the wrong frequency and neither the trainee or the Supervisor observing the trainee noticed the wrong aircraft had called them.

CRJ900 flight crew is issue direct TORNN on the GUSHR3 arrival to IAH then issued a hold as published at TORNN. The pattern does not look correct; but is correctly entered. ATC agrees and issues vectors to reenter the holding pattern at TORNN. It is discovered that the charted out bound course of 243 degrees is off by 100 degrees at CHMPZ and TORNN. It should be 343 degrees.

B737-800 Captain reported 'tremendous turbulence' from jet wash while landing on Runway 12 at MIA that was associated with a B777 departure on Runway 8R. Runway 8R and Runway 12 are close enough that the runway centerlines intersect near the runway thresholds.
Maintenance reported they installed an incorrect bearing on an MD-11 nose gear wheel assembly.
A pilot reported the need to take evasive maneuvers to avoid an amateur rocket launch.
A321 Captain reported difficulty interpreting and troubleshooting Fuel System messages due to an MEL item related to the fuel system.
Citation pilot reported that a descent on their arrival was a factor for other traffic departing a close by airport.
B777 flight crew reported three Flight Attendants were slightly injured in a wake turbulence encounter at FL320 near TNT VOR in England.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering an EGPWS activation during a visual approach.

Pilot reports of flying into SDM and not being familiar with the jump zones that are close to the airport. Controller vectors aircraft for a visual approach; leaves the aircraft high; and then ships the aircraft to the Tower. Aircraft turns away from airport to continue its descent and Tower Controller advises pilot he is going into a jump zone. Pilot finally lands at SDM. Pilot reports that this is an unsafe operation and offers suggestions to make flying into SDM better.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reports improper use of Engineering Authorization to defer maintenance action.
Ground Personnel reported that the jetbridge overhangs the safety zone at several gates; resulting in a collision hazard.

One sector pointed out an aircraft to another sector and handed it off to the subsequent sector. The sector the aircraft was being handed off to had conflicting traffic and attempted to coordinate control instructions to the Controller working the aircraft. Due to weather complexities; sectors that were combined were being split to alleviate the workload. In the ensuing confusion of closed sectors being opened and busy traffic; the aircraft was not pointed out to one of the newly opened sectors.
B737-700 Captain reported a laptop in the cabin that was sparking and smoldering was placed in a containment bag by a Flight Attendant.
Crew reported that local operations delayed a flight without contacting Dispatch.
S46 TRACON Controller reported a non-mode C target tracked toward the localizer course while another aircraft was inbound.

Three B737 Flight Attendants reported their air conditioning was unable to sufficiently cool on the ground or in flight because of extreme heat. The Lead Flight Attendant received emergency medical attention because of the prolonged triple digit heat exposure.
ERJ-190 Captain reported the nose swung 20 to 30 degrees left on the takeoff roll in response to a wind gust or wake from the previous takeoff.
The ERJ Captain reports that the EFB is installed in a poor location that is subject to water damage among other things.
D10 Controller reports of another position being inundated by traffic and taking late handoffs. Controller requested a handoff for the position but none was provided.
Flight Attendant reported confusion about airline policy relating to the face mask policy.
Flight instructor reported there was no NOTAM for a runway closure at JAN airport.

A Flight Attendant reports an aggressive and loud encounter with an unidentified uniformed airport person on the ramp after opening the aircraft rear service door upon arrival at the gate. This person demanded to see the crew's IDs and claimed that opening the service door prior to the departure of all passengers was a security violation. The door was opened to reduce the smell from day-old rotting meals in one of the service carts.
B777 First Officer reported there were no company instructions or procedures for two unfamiliar device chargers in the cockpit.
Customer Service Representative reported that the airport is pumping artificial fragrances that are irritating and can cause headaches.

A320 First Officer reported not noticing the 6000 ft minimum vectoring altitude between MARUX and the IAF on the MMSD VOR DME-2 Runway 16 Approach. The reporter stated the altitude is not coded into their navigation database.
Flight Attendant reported experiencing fatigue from extended work hours and additional duties due to change in flight schedules in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Corporate pilot reported that TAF's and METAR's used were inaccurate and could use improvement.
Technician reported that an A321 flight crew reset circuit breaker and did not document this maintenance action or perform follow-up system tests as required by the maintenance manual.
A300 flight crew reported that shortly after touchdown the aircraft lifted off again; possibly from a wake encounter or a wind gust.
Center Controllers reported the Traffic Management Unit failed to take any action during a period of known weather obstructions causing their sectors and workload to be unmanageable.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reported a personnel lift safety rail failure caused damage to aircraft.
A320 Captain reported extreme fatigue resulted from his three day assignment that included a 'red eye' leg on day two.

B737-800 Captain reported he recommended the crosswind landing limitation for scimitar winglet equipped aircraft be amended to allow for the use of the normal crosswind landing technique without potentially damaging the aircraft.
ZKC Controller reported they did not properly coordinate a priority aircraft due to not receiving a sector briefing.
Flight Attendant reported concerns about safety and security issues relating to new COVID-19 jumpseat arrangements and social distancing guidelines.
B737 Captain reported the JeppFD-Pro app on his iPad malfunctioned while on approach. The problem was fixed on the ground once the iPad was connected to Wi-Fi.
SF340B flight attendant reported Captain allowing what may have appeared to be an overload to weight and balance.
Center Controller reports of an aircraft returning to departure airport; which could not be handed off to Approach automatically; and had to be handed off manually.
Air Carrier Captain reported of possible confusion when interpreting JEPP RNAV SID charts for LAS due to the placement of inset information.

ZME Controller reports of a Mode-C intruder target moving NE towards their airspace at a fast rate. Controllers checked with pilots in the vicinity to see if they saw it on TCAS and they did not. Controller referenced it to something like ring-around but said it was not because the targets did not correlate to ring-around. Controller reported the incident to Front Line Manager (FLM).
Cirrus SR20 pilot reported receiving a traffic advisory from ATC; locating traffic and descending approximately 400 feet to the avoid conflicting traffic.
BJC Tower Controller reported an aircraft making evasive actions due to a VFR aircraft trying to shoot the gap between the Class D and Class B airspace.

A Large Transport flight crew reported being assigned a runway different from what they expected while taxiing out for takeoff. They neglected to brief the revised taxi route and missed the airport sign 'all aircraft hold here' short of Taxiway F. They did not hold short and ATC called to admonish them.
Technician reported the aircraft was parked and the parking orders never updated requiring a further maintenance delay.
It was reported that Boston Tower used a frequency that is not published in any publication.
B737 Flight Attendant reported catering was loading items into locations with weight limitations that were being exceeded.

N90 Controller reports of losing RADAR and missing an aircraft when it is finally changed to another RADAR feed. The Controller describes how he found the aircraft and what he did to identify it again.
A Center Controller reported a NMAC; after opening a Radar Assist position at combined sectors the two aircraft began flashing conflict alert.
Air carrier First Officer reported confusion regarding the location of the #5 Holding Point at GEG due to incorrect depiction of the location on Jeppesen Low Vis Charts 10-9B; C; and D.
A Controller at Miami Center Oceanic area reported approving a deviation into New York Center Oceanic Area airspace without coordination; resulting in a potential traffic conflict.
Airport Ground Agent reported the employee bus is very crowded and is concerned social distancing is not being enforced.
Captain reported that it is unclear if company policy allows brake release to be done prior to receiving clearance to push.
B777 First Officer reported that a company-issued app used by pilots on their iPads is confusing to use.
Captain of A319 reported that he was ordered to continue flying with a sick FO and was becoming ill and fatigued in the process.
Airbus Captain reported executing a go-around at MIA after encountering jet blast from a departing Heavy aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported they lost the localizer signal while conducting a Visual Approach to MCI airport.

MIA Approach Controller reported of a VFR aircraft skirting below the MIA Class Bravo. While legal; this causes separation conflicts with traffic inbound to MIA. Reporter suggested changes to the Class Bravo.
Dispatcher reported excessive workload and lack of relief is causing safety of flight issues and work rule violations due to COVID related staffing cuts.
Dispatcher reported not properly planning for a Center to be ATC Zero for COVID cleaning.
SR22 pilot reported avoiding a deer on landing resulting in a runway excursion; nose gear collapse and prop strike.
A Cessna 421 pilot reported penetrating Class B airspace due to weather and unable to maneuver within the airspace.
An ERJ-175 First Officer was concerned after the fact that he and Captain; both as low time pilots; had been paired together.
A Captain reported that the 'Pilot in Command' and 'Second in Command' persons are not recording their time per the FARs.
A Northern California TRACON (NCT) Controller reports of being told that he had a wake turbulence loss of separation according to the Traffic Analysis and Review Program (TARP).
A Center Controller reported insufficient action was taken by the Traffic Management Units to prevent sectors from being overloaded when forecast weather manifested.

Pilots reported of flying an approach; but did not see a mandatory crossing altitude on the approach. The pilots briefed the approach but did not see the mandatory altitude. ATC corrected the problem.

An Captain reported that he has the takeoff minimums for Runway 28 at NGU have been changed to N/A on the Jeppesen 31-1 plate and believes that this is incorrect. The previous version stated; Adequate Vis Ref 1/4; and STD 1 (1 & 2 Eng); with the addition of stadium lights that should not have affected the takeoff minimums.
Center Controller reported an airspace violation due aircraft hand-off issues with a TRACON facility.
EMB-145 Captain reported being notified by the Flight Attendant that the aircraft was short 1 child life vest prior to departure.
Tower Controller reported loss of radios and equipment requirements that were not being followed.
A TRACON Controller reported management forced  the Controllers to accommodate a photo mission aircraft that flew into confliction with airport traffic.
Technician reported that Company training does not meet FAR criteria for qualifications required.
Midnight shift Dispatcher reported the workload was close to violating FAR 121.395; and that even with furlough recalls and new hires; dispatchers are overloaded.
BE35 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence on approach in trail of a Ford Trimotor that resulted in a hard landing and damage to the landing gear.
Maintenance Controller requested that deferral applicability for duct louvers be clarified.
A Maintenance Technician reported the ramp and hangar area lightning warning system does not work in a timely manner.
SoCal TRACON Feeder and Final Controllers reported a handoff that was not made correctly which resulted in an aircraft having to be resequenced due to not seeing traffic.
Corporate flight crew reported responding to two RA's for traffic they had not been advised about when switching from Approach to Tower frequency.
Flight Attendant reported inadequate time for their preflight responsibilities.
A First Officer of an A320 reported his concern about the application of an MEL procedure for engine anti-ice.
SCT Controller reported a conflict between a Cessna 172 pilot and an unmanned aerial vehicle within 50 feet of their aircraft while on a south downwind to CRQ.
A Boeing 737 Maintenance Technician reported that an Engineering Authority document was written and it was in direct contradiction of FAR's.
GA pilot reported the PAPI was not working properly at PUB airport.
A Maintenance Supervisor reported that an oxygen servicing booster blew a brass cap due to extreme heat.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below the charted assigned altitude of an RNAV arrival. The controller stated this is a recurring issue.
Pilot reported his flight departed out of an airport in a complex and congested airspace environment and inadvertently entered towered airspace without clearance.

Air carrier flight crew reported encountering multiple bird strikes shortly after takeoff from SFO resulting in spurious flight instrument indications. After dumping fuel; a return to the departure airport was safely accomplished.
Pilot reported a bird strike resulted in aircraft damage.
ZLA Controller reported forgetting that a local restricted area was about to become active and descended an aircraft into the airspace.
A80 controller reports of two aircraft with the same call sign in the airspace; one an arrival and the other was a departure. Controller added a letter to the end of one of the calls signs.
First Officer of a turbojet reported experiencing a marginally stable approach at night into an airport with mountainous terrain.
GIV flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence from preceding MD-11 on approach to LAX.
Air carrier Captain reported manual update required due to difference between Jeppesen chart and company pilot procedures.
Air Carrier Boeing aircraft Captain reported that a pilot bulletin to address the Smoke or Fumes Checklist contains numerous errors and the checklist was unavailable in the Electronic Flight Bags.
B737-800 Captain reported the Company iPads are at times unable to obtain flight plans and weather information because the allocated data plan had been exceeded.
ROA TRACON Trainee Controller and an instructor pilot reported the pilot was assigned 4;000 feet which is below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
ZTL Controller reported of turning aircraft but some were not in his airspace. Controller reported that he did not get control of all aircraft due to time constraint and staffing.
Air carrier Captain reported a GPWS event during approach in visual conditions.
A319 Flight Crew reported that during pre-flight found over wing emergency slide panel had two missing fasteners as well as some fasteners that were glued in place.
Embraer Jet flight crew reported a NMAC with two hang gliders.
Center Controller reported an ATC Zero event at another Center facility resulted in communication issues and an airspace violation.
Pilot reported insufficient navigational signal coverage at the changeover point on V23; between EUG and BTG VORTACs; while at the MEA of 5;000 feet.
A320 flight crew experiences GPS jamming in ZLC airspace resulting in a momentary track deviation. The jamming was NOTAMed but the aircraft's reaction to the jamming was unexpected.
Embraer 505 Captain reported an in-flight failure of the integrated flight instrument system.
Air Carrier Captain reported diverting to the alternate airport due to a UAV blocking traffic at the primary airport and holding fuel becoming an issue.
Air carrier Captain reported the ramp lights on the north end of the SEA North Satellite are blinding while taxiing on Taxiways B and W between Spots 66; over Spot 55 and beyond.

Flight Attendant did not want to sit in the appropriate jumpseat for takeoff/landing for social distancing concerns. A Supervisor was contacted and the Flight Attendant was given the option to move to a different jumpseat or to be pulled from the flight. The Flight Attendant moved to another jumpseat.
ATC Center Controller reported traffic management initiatives during a known weather event were late and insufficient to help manage the traffic flows to the airport.
A321 flight crew reported the company adjusted schedules to avoid exceeding the hour limitation in the FARs.
C172 pilot reported momentary loss of control on the landing roll related to prop wash from an aircraft taking off on a nearby intersecting runway.
Two company aircraft with similar call signs were taxiing out and departing at the same time. As one aircraft was lifting off it was cleared for takeoff by the Tower Controller.
Ground employee reported social distancing concerns during peak times at the airport.

A flight instructor reported being informed that a city noise abatement altitude had not been met. The pilot was not informed of the noise abatement restrictions nor were any procedures found following the notification.
Air carrier First Officer suggested a moratorium on the use of oxygen masks in the flight training devices in order to minimize the possible transmission of COVID-19.

An air carrier Captain reported receiving a terrain warning received during a visual approach at night. The warning was triggered by the uniqueness of surrounding terrain; man-made structures; and the descent rate during approach. The crew executed mild evasive procedures and landed uneventfully.
EUG TRACON Supervisor reported they observed an aircraft IFR below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A SFO Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of an airborne conflict he observes that local is having a problem with. Coordination with Northern California TRACON (NCT) is attempted but the resolution is denied by NCT. In order to save 1;000 feet between the aircraft the FLM instructs local to have the aircraft maintain a specific altitude below the higher aircraft. The altitude to maintain was below the MVA which had recently changed by 100 feet higher; causing an airspace deviation.
A320 First Officer reported his iPad didn't charge when plugged in on the ground. This resulted in a flight being conducted with only 1 operational iPad.
A320 Captain reported concerns over a Minimum Equipment List procedure.

A B737NG crew crossed the SFO DYAMD 2 RNAV ARHCI waypoint at 7;000 ft because they had not seen the NOTAM constraining the crossing altitude above 8;000 ft to prevent descent below SFO Class B airspace.

Air carrier Mechanic reported that wearing a mask and safety glasses causes the glasses to fog up; resulting in safety concerns when working on ladders; lifts; and in tight places. Mechanic also expressed concern that while working with chemicals; they could inadvertently be transferred to the mask.
Pilot reported conflicting traffic pattern information provided at FA83; resulting in inflight conflicts.

Air Carrier First Officer reported the pilot crew failed to set the correct altimeter setting causing an altitude deviation. Pre-flight distractions; including the current pandemic situation; were cited for the error.
B-777 Captain reports an 'echoing' problem on an ATC frequency. No corrective action is reported.

The flight crew of a De Havilland Canada DHC-8-200 reported that maintenance authorized the deferral of the autopilot when both of the flight guidance computers were inoperative; which would make this an illegal deferral.
B737 flight crew and ATC clearance confusion associated with the West transition versus the WESLA fix on the East transition of the SFO SERFR1 STAR.
R90 TRACON Controller reported a recurring ADS-B problem that indicates erroneous groundspeed readouts.
E170 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing EWR in trail of a H/B777.
B737 First Officer reported a VHF/NAV 1 anomaly resulting in a missed approach.

A Dispatcher is informed by one of his flights that they have been assigned FL380/M85 on North Atlantic (NAT) A instead of FL350. The computer software will not accept this altitude and a flight plan with accurate times and fuel burns cannot be generated.

DAB Controller reports of an automation problem with the wrong scratch pad information showing up in aircraft data block.  This lead to confusion for the Controller; pilot and resulted in an airspace deviation.
Technician reported difficulties while delivering aircraft training to technicians via distance learning.
GA pilot reported error in VFR Charts.
Technician reported conflicting information in the IPC with regards to effectiveness of Nose Wheel assemblies authorized for installation on aircraft.
Dispatcher and flight crew reported a night landing without runway lighting.
Air carrier Captain reported Ground Personnel at FLL airport have microphones turned on at all times during pushback causing communication issues.
Pilot informed to Go-around by Air Traffic Control due to a glitch in a new system.
A319 flight crew reported Terrain Warning on approach.
Reporter reported conflicting guidance on the BUF ILS 05 instrument approach chart referencing autopilot use.
B737-800 First Officer reported overshooting a charted altitude on arrival into MMMX. Reporter also stated they received a GPS-L invalid message.
Air carrier First Officer conducting a Visual Approach into ONT reported receiving a Terrain Alert.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a runway incursion at ORF due to a lack of runway markings and signage.
B737 Captain reported several aircraft with similar call signs were taxiing for departure at the same time causing confusion on the frequency.
Helicopter ambulance Captain reported an empty baby incubator caught fire while in flight.
Technician reported large bore valve stems installed on nose wheel assemblies and not documented correctly.
There was an inconsistency in the various manuals related to carriage of poisonous Dangerous Goods loaded close to foodstuffs.
A319 flight crew reported that a large bird strike just prior to V1 resulted in a rejected takeoff and a minor wheel fire.

PDX Tower Controller reported instructing a VFR pilot to maintain visual separation from an arriving aircraft on approach. The pilot acknowledged the instruction; but the resulting conflict was logged as an NMAC.
A Flight Attendant reported that she became extremely ill after getting up from her crew break. The reporter noticed this because she was coughing and congested after her break in the crew bunks.
GA pilot reported reduced Tower services at a Class D airport due to COVID-19 has resulted in an unsafe environment and described several conflicts occurring while the Tower was closed.
Air carrier First Officer reported loss of communication with ATC prior to landing.
B777 Captain reported a non-standard fire protection panel installed in crew rest area; resulting in a delay and 5 seats to be reserved for crew rest.
A MKE TRACON Controller reported they vectored two approaches to parallel runways with less than standard separation.
Air taxi flight crew reported a bird strike while taking off from MAZ. They indicated that the bird hazard at this airport is significant and needs to be mitigated.

B737 Captain discovered that the nosewheel steering was inoperative after pushback and called Maintenance. Maintenance found that the nosewheel steering lockout pin had sheared off at the head. The push back crew removed only the head and the streamer; but not the shaft.
Dispatcher reported the problems with their computerized flight planning system.
Air carrier pilot reported an airspace error with regards to the Runway 9 ILS approach to MIA airport.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with a low wing aircraft above them.
A US carrier aircraft operating internationally with ADS-B installed did not have the ADS-B Operations Specification on file indicating its aircraft were authorized flight above FL290.
Flight Attendant reported there should be a change to the in-flight beverage/snack service to limit exposure time to Flight Attendants while passengers have their masks off.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft departed an airport IFR and was immediately in conflict with two VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC. The departing IFR aircraft responded to an RA.
Air carrier First Officer stated that NOTAM was poorly worded and required several hours of plotting to comply with; causing NOTAM to be violated.

While conducting an approach the flight crew encountered unknown VFR traffic in close proximity which was not displaying on TCAS; possibly due to no or inoperative transponder. ATC was queried and requested help in identifying the aircraft type. No RA was generated by this event.
A B737 First Officer reported the distraction caused by a newly issued iPad window tray mount protruding low into his elbow area while hand flying.

A general aviation pilot reported his Garmin 530W database provided by Jeppesen did not include several Canadian waypoints which were part of his IFR flight plan between two domestic U.S. airports. Reporter stated some Canadian waypoints are available; but others are not.
B737-700 F/O reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL350 10 miles in trail of a B777 that resulted in a pitch down and left and right rolls.

A Dispatcher reported a flight's Captain was removed at his FAR 117 duty time limit. Two hours later during the takeoff roll; the First Officer was notified via ACARS that he had exceeded his duty time limit. Reporter stated they should have received notification earlier and ACARS messages during takeoff should be prevented.
An aircraft descended on a visual approach and received a GPWS alert.

Air taxi Captain reported difficulty locating the BIL CRAZI1 Arrival in the Volume 1 of FAA US Terminal Procedures Publication. It was not where he expected it to be and not in alphabetical order. The reporter descended approximately 1500 feet below assigned altitude while searching.
A fuel indicator on a B737 was deferred using an incorrect MEL reference.

A ZDC Controller was assigned to work two sectors combined. The Controller felt there was too much traffic and too many complex situations for combining sectors. The Controller allowed another Controller to descend an aircraft in his airspace with opposite direction conflicting traffic.
B737 Flight Attendant reported that an employee; who was riding on the plane's fourth jump seat; appeared to be overweight for the seat and had difficulty getting the seat belt fastened.
Air carrier Captain reported being distracted by a lighted sign on a street adjacent to DEN airport.

Ground employee reported that the addition of a temperature check station nearby an equipment room creates congestion; making it difficult to social distance during busy times when many people are in the hallway.
Maintenance Technician reported that Safety is no longer the primary goal.
A B737 Captain discovered excessive carbon monoxide and exhaust fumes inside the aircraft at PHL prior to passenger boarding.
Air carrier First Officer reported the crew did not notice taxiway designator revisions in the Jeppesen charts causing confusion and a taxiway incursion.
EA50 Captain reported seeing incorrect depiction of TFRs on EFB display.
B777 flight crew reported two flight attendants were injured as a result of a wake turbulence encounter.
B777 Captain reported pressure to accept an aircraft with an inoperative APU that he had refused for an ETOPS flight.
B737 Captain reported they parked at the gate without wing walkers to comply with a local unwritten procedure for their particular gate.
First Officer reported a hot microphone on an oxygen mask; causing communications to be rendered inoperative.
PA-28 pilot reported an NMAC that required significant evasive maneuvering.
Flight Attendant reported feeling dizzy along with another Flight Attendant and attributed it to having to wear a protective mask.

SCT TRACON Burbank area Controller reports of a busy shift; lack of staffing and a combined position he is working. Controller almost has a loss of separation due to thinking an aircraft is VFR while actually it is IFR.
Maintenance person reported that when a lift was used to open the engine cowling it surged up and damaged the lower aft cowling.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported confusion about the size of device that should be allowed to be out during taxi and takeoff.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger circumvented having to wear a mask by eating and drinking throughout the entire flight.
A Center Controller reported that the adjacent Center Sector was handing off an aircraft below the Minimum Enroute Altitude.
A319 Captain reported being requested to exceed legal duty time.
Air carrier First Officer reported they did not perform the Before Takeoff Checklist.

Omaha TRACON Controller reported a problem with crossing course criteria and a possible loss of separation. The Controller did not like that the FAA has a tool to determine crossing course after the fact.
An A320 flight crew reported that on final approach they extended the downwind and continued on the base leg when they got a 'Low Altitude' alert.
Flight Attendant reported having to inform a passenger about the no vent mask policy and questioned whether the policy is still being adhered to.
A Maintenance Technician reported that on a B757-300; when the inlet cowl is removed; there isn't anyplace to secure the fan cowl hold open rod.
A321 Captain reported a crew scheduling error that had them exceed their duty period.
Air carrier Captain reported issues with scheduling of all night flights which result in fatigued pilots.
Air carrier Captain reported difficulty receiving ATIS in a timely manner at JNU airport and requested that Digital ATIS be installed.
Dispatch did not inform an EMB-145 crew about decreased fuel requirement during an extended preflight and the crew had to advise low fuel with ATC because total landing fuel was about 1;300 LBS.
Maintenance Technician reported confusion on who is responsible for cleaning bio-hazards in the interior of the aircraft cabin.
Flight Attendant reported there is not enough room to social distance between the FA jump-seat and passenger seats.
A Pilot departed on the CHS PLFMD 1 RNAV and noted Note 1 states 'At or above 560'' while the INITIAL CLIMB instructions state to 560'. Both are minimum constraints climbing to 4;000'.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported several of the polar route flights he was working had communication difficulties with Magadan.
A321 flight crew reported being cleared for takeoff with another aircraft crossing the runway.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a company issued tablet battery was observed bulging during the bus ride to the airport.
An aircraft responding to an RA in the traffic pattern at EDF entered the MRI Class D airspace without clearance.
MDT Controller reported numerous conflicts and delays have resulted due to traffic density; complexity; type; and airspace design.  Reporter recommended airspace be classified as Charlie for safety.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation with traffic off of LGB and traffic inbound to LGB.
COS TRACON Trainee reported they vectored an aircraft that had descended to avoid VFR traffic below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight crew reported being unable to operate because of inadequate deice equipment and training at the Santiago airport.

Pilot reported of multiple laser lights of red and green color entering the cockpit while in flight. Pilot determined that they were holiday decorations that illuminate houses with possibly a misdirected light not hitting the house. Pilot would like the general public educated that these types of lights might pose a danger to aircraft; pedestrians; and automobiles.
A Captain of a widebody turbojet in part 121 operations reported ambiguity in charting while approaching BFI.

A11 TRACON Controller reported losing communication with an aircraft which descended below the MVA before climbing and reestablishing communications. Later the aircraft requested to join up with a flight of aircraft and the Controller reported they (ATC) did not know the procedure for a flight join up.
Air carrier Captain reported the COS marshaller arrived with no lighted wands to guide the aircraft in at night and during intense rain.

Air carrier Captain reported UPP VOR is not labeled as an IAF on the KOA ILS or LOC Runway 17 Approach like it is on other approaches. However; there is an IAF on the transition from UPP VOR. It does not appear to be a charting error.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) reports about supertugs towing two CRJ Regional Aircraft that were unpowered; no beacon or navigation lights on; no one in the cockpit; in IMC field conditions with snow; limited visibility and low ceilings with twilight commencing. Technician noted that both aircraft tows were in violation of FAA and their Airport Operations regulations.
Air carrier Captain reported being unable to obtain PPE (Personal Protective Equipment).
Air Carrier Captain reported the time required for the COVID-19 test delayed the departure of their flight.

Air carrier flight crew reported they experienced a GPS signal loss during a NOTAMed military operation on an oceanic flight. The crew and their Dispatcher had language difficulty explaining the navigation capability loss to the foreign ARTCC.
Air carrier First Officer reported traversing through an old; unused and uncharted taxiway which had taxiway lights installed in the pavement but were unlit; posing a potential hazard.

Air carrier flight crew reported they had observed an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) go by the aircraft at about 1000  feet; while conducting an approach at the Philadelphia International Airport (PHL).
CRJ-900 Captain reported executing a go-around from their approach to BNA after spotting a party balloon on their flight path at about 500 feet AGL.
Air carrier First Officer reported the crew was not notified by the company of exposure to a COVID-19 positive employee.
Cessna pilot reported a TFR was not displayed on the preflight TFR map or on the aircraft GPS; resulting in a possible airspace violation.
Flight Attendant reported placards for cabin interphone handsets are all incorrect.
Beech 90 pilot reported of similar sounding fixes. The pilot entered PUMKN; located to the west in Iowa; rather than PMPKN; north of PWK; which made Chicago TRACON question where they were going.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger had to be told to wear a mask while on board.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft and adjacent facilities could not make contact; possibly due to fatigue driven sleeping during a mid-shift.

A Maintenance Support person reported that temporary repair procedures for the B767-300F main cargo door seal depressors comes from different sources and are inconsistent; and the tracking of these repairs is not standard.
Ground employee reported large numbers of aircraft are being moved in and out of storage without proper procedures in place.
Air carrier First Officer reported a particular aircraft is allowed to keep flying without ADSB using an outdated MEL.

Corporate jet pilot reported that they were informed by ATC that the route they took on the Cottonwood Two Departure out of EGE was for emergency; engine out procedure only. The pilot advised that there was no such notation on the chart downloaded from a commercial flight planning website.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering a terrain warning at night on approach to BTV and took evasive action.
A320 First Officer reported they could not reach their destination station operations on radio frequency nor through dispatch.
Flight Attendant reported Flight Crew jump seats do not have adequate seat belts installed.

ZAN Front Line Manager reported of an aircraft had traffic with another target that was unidentified. FLM reported this was the same aircraft that was ADS-B equipped. FLM reported this type of nuisance conflict alert happens quite often and would like something to be done about it.

Two maintenance technicians reported that while retracting the Main Landing Gear on a Bombardier CRJ200 for maintenance; with the MLG door disconnected; the bellcrank was out of the proper position causing the linkage to break and damage to the aircraft.
Flight crew of a large turbojet aircraft reported a ramp fire occurred before departure on a cargo pallet before it was loaded into the cargo hold.
EMB145 Captain reports having to cajole his Dispatcher into providing enough fuel to safely fly his flight to an IMC destination.

A military instructor pilot reported NPA and NDZ outlying training airports NBJ and NFD are subject to daily VFR civilian traffic over flights below 1;500 feet without CTAF traffic calls. Both airports are heavily utilized and traffic conflicts occur daily. Better VFR charting notices may help.

RDG Local Controller reported a false ASDE-X warning. An arrival was holding short of an active runway with a departing aircraft when the warning sounded. The Controller visually confirmed the arrival was holding short and the takeoff was uneventful.
ZMP ARTCC Controller reported problems with aircraft related to Temporary Flight Rules (TFR); aircraft intercept and losing a data tag on aircraft involved.
CRJ First Officer reported experiencing navigation errors possibly due to GPS signal disruption.
A320 flight crew on final approach reported a heavy B787 turning short final above them. The flight crew received a RA to descend; and executed a go-around.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during a night visual approach to SLC.

EMB-175 Captain reported his Dispatcher refused his request for additional fuel in violation of FAR 91.3. The Captain requested additional fuel because he believed weather at his destination airport would be worse than forecasted.
Upon completion of QRH procedure for a cracked windshield; crew of CRJ-900 remembered a company memo with clarifying info.
Air Carrier flight crew reported multiple bird strikes on departure led to a diversion.
Dispatcher reported a concern with maintaining operational control because of the large number of flights assigned to them.
B757 Captain reported that a new bridge near EWR has sequencing white strobe lights that could be mistaken for Runway End Identifier Lights.
SEA TRACON Controller reported the procedure to protect a missed approach at one airport from arrivals to an adjacent airport is unsafe.
Captain reported that aircraft are being operated with known mechanical discrepancies to minimize delays and cancellation by direction of Company Management.
GTF Controller vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Attendant reported Captain and another Flight Attendant verbally abused passengers and removed them from airplane.

A general aviation pilot reports that; while maneuvering in the traffic pattern and communicating his intentions on the CTAF frequency; he was cut off by another aircraft which was not radio equipped. This conflict resulted in a go-around.
C90 TRACON Front Line Manager reported assuming a position with no briefing and the controllers scrambling to regain control of the TRACON sectors.
B737 First Officer reported severe fatigue due to flight scheduling.
Palo Alto Tower Controller reported phantom target issue.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near miss with two gliders while on approach to RNO in thevicinity of PYRAM intersection.
Air Carrier Captain reported there is an unlighted tower SE of RDG on the approach path to Runway 31; which does not have an instrument approach. A Notam for the lighting outage existed at the time.
A320 Captain reported the aircraft logbook appeared to indicate a previous flight crew had operated the water service door which is prohibited by the Flight Operations Manual.

Private pilot reported that during a recent flight at dusk a tower light was inoperative; two drop zones not in use were still listed on the sectional chart; and inadequate traffic warnings were issued by two local airport Towers.
Glider pilot reported an NMAC with a military aircraft.
Flight crew missed turn onto Taxiway B at FRG because it was not on the Jeppesen chart. They had to use an alternate route to get into position for takeoff.
Airline ramp employee reported company personnel agreed to work an unfamiliar aircraft model without appropriate training.
ERJ-190 F/O reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to DCA five miles in trail of an E175.

Local Controller reported a false ASDE-X warning of conflicting traffic on the runway while an aircraft was on short final. The controllers in the Tower scanned the area; but were unable to find a reason for the erroneous warning and the aircraft landed uneventfully.
B737NG flight crew reported a rejected takeoff following a bird strike in the Number 1 engine. Maintenance found major damage to the engine.
C172 Instructor reported deviating to miss a possible drone resulting in TFR encroachment.

A Controller observed a Beacon Target squawking 1200 with a Mode C indicating 29;300 feet. Numerous aircraft were vectored away from this target. No aircraft reported seeing any traffic at the position of the beacon target.
B737-900 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX in trail of a heavy jet.
B737 First Officer reported the taxi lead-in line does not align with Jet Bridge.
A B737 Captain reported that a new pre-departure/taxi procedure resulting in the First Officer being distracted and 'heads down' during taxi.
B737 Captain reported an anomaly with the cockpit oxygen masks that did not seal properly.
B737-700 flight crew reported returning to the departure airport after a bird strike in the Number 1 engine that caused significant vibration.

Flight crew reported about confusion when departing the airport. Their PDC had two SIDs and the crew was flying one and controllers were issuing headings. The pilots are concerned that the PDC format is unclear.
Flight Attendant reported seating issues and concerns with the close proximity of passengers when boarding the flight.
Seaplane pilot reported Long Beach Tower advising pilot that their operation was too close to swimmers in the area.
P80 Controller reported vectoring off course and delaying descent clearance for two aircraft on approach due to an unidentified VFR aircraft conflicting in the vicinity of the Initial Approach Fix.
Pilot reported hitting a bird while on downwind.
CRJ-900 Captain reported concerns regarding FAR 117 limitations.
Air carrier Captain reported new ramp tractor lights are so bright that pilots are blinded to everything on the ramp.

ZLC Controller reported of an interpretation change of the FAA Order 7110.65 at the ARTCC. The reporter stated 'routes of aircraft not covered by radar could not be amended.'  Reporter wanted a safe solution that allows the assistance of aircraft that may need to deviate for weather or approaches.

PCT Controller and an air carrier Captain reported some confusion with clearances that resulted in the aircraft missing the crossing restriction. As a result conflicting traffic was vectored into the Washington DC Flight Restricted Zone.
A TRACON Controller was not notified of a runway closure. The Controller states that this is a routine occurrence.
Super tug driver reported being physically injured by the stiff suspension and bouncy ride that it produces.
A Maintenance Technician reported that tooling used for repair on an Airbus 319 exhaust nozzle was not as depicted in the AMM and was improperly marked.
B737 First Officer reported a bird strike on takeoff that resulted in a maintenance action at the destination airport.
E175 Flight Crew reported the air hose from the air starter unit unexpectedly disconnected from aircraft; causing damage to the aircraft.
Flight crew reported a taxi deviation occurred when Ground referenced a taxiway not charted on their 10-9 page.
B777 Captain reported incorrectly completing MEL procedures for an inoperative APU.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft missed a turn and came within less than the required separation from a tall obstruction.
C208 pilot reported a loss of the HSI in IMC conditions resulting in a diversion.
Air air carrier pilot reported they overlooked a runway closure in the NOTAMs.
S46 Controller reported vectoring an aircraft off of an ILS in order to avoid conflicting unidentified VFR traffic.
Air Carrier Captain reported maintenance and ground personnel with no experience on specific aircraft types are being utilized due to staffing cutbacks.
EMB-175 Mechanic reported attempting to fix a Deferred Maintenance Item; but lacked the necessary equipment. Supervisor said to put the aircraft back together and defer it again.
Air Traffic Controller reported a VFR aircraft did not comply with instructions resulting in a NMAC.
MD-11 First Officer reported a go around receiving an Anti-Skid R Fail Level 2 Alert.

S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft being vectored for an ILS Approach had conflicting VFR traffic climbing through the localizer course. The Controller had to vector the ILS aircraft off the approach to maintain separation.
B737-700 flight crew could not find checklist for Leading Edge Devices Not Extended.
Flight Attendant was told by an unaccompanied minor that the child's mother had COVID-19 and the child was on the way to stay with family.
B737 pilot reported communication problems associated with the 8;000 foot change at intersection ARCHI.

CRJ200 dispatchers described a computer outage that resulted in the loss of operational control of the airborne aircraft they were flight following. Management did not want to delay any flights that were ready to pushback at the hub; even though the dispatchers could not communicate with or monitor the flights they were responsible for.

B737 flight crew experiences loss of GPS signal at FL350 in ZLC airspace due to NOTAMed GPS jamming by the military. Several other crews report GPS interruptions to ATC and ATC detects some track deviations.
After returning to departure airport with unreliable airspeed indication; the Captain suggested moving necessary charts to the relevant portion of the QRH for ease in location and use.
NorCal TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation with a departure off of Livermore airport.
B737 Maintenance technician reported required tools were not available to complete repair.
Technician reported that Work Cards associated with the use of Biobor JL in aircraft fuel tanks during storage are missing critical information and should be revised.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy and also moved to a better seat without asking FAs.
BE35 pilot reported that during the landing flare his aircraft was affected by the rotor vortices of a nearby helicopter.
Customer Service Representative reported crowding and social distancing issues near company provided kiosks in the terminal area.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving altitude alert from ATC on approach to DEN airport.
Airbus Captain reported missing published crossing restriction on the Jeppesen plate; due to the lack of contrast.
Air Carrier pilot conducting a Visual Approach reported receiving a terrain alert from the aircraft and ATC.
B757 Captain reported problems with his iPad apps while trying to obtain company information.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported smoke outside the aircraft after the cabin doors had been closed. This caused some worry in the cockpit and cabin until it was discovered that the smoke was coming from a belt loader.
Maintenance Technician and a Flight Crew reported the log book was not signed off by maintenance and did not comply with an Airworthiness Directive.
Technician reported an aircraft operation in Part 91 flight without all documents signed and the log book update correctly.
B787 flight attendant reported reduced staffing in the cabin creating an unsafe situation.
A Pilot expressed concern about an RNAV Arrival which requires the aircraft to descend below Class B airspace and reduce speed to 200 knots unexpectedly.
A flight crew discovered three tires weighing 300 pound had been offloaded upon arrival; but had not been accounted for in the weight and balance prior to departure from CLT.
Cessna pilot reported aircraft was struck by an object during initial climb causing a dent in aircraft.
A319 Captain reported departure was delayed when an iPad began a download during taxi out that inhibited access to the departure plate.
B737-700 First Officer reported that a bolt was installed in a gear pin slot for the main landing gear.
LR60 Captain reported overshooting cleared altitude in climb to FL340 after encountering wake turbulence from a wide body transport aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported that approach charts at certain military airports do not have a note requiring cold temperature altitude corrections.
A319 Captain reported encountering turbulence at FL360; probably either wake or clear air turbulence related.
A Maintenance Technician reported that ramp personnel are moving aircraft with unauthorized personnel and not following procedures.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC perceived an altitude excursion; possibly due to ground equipment problem.
Pilot reported airfield lighting at PDK is poor and erratic. Surrounding ground lights not associated with the airport compound the issue further.
Air carrier F/O reported slowing below ATC assigned speed when directed to do so by the Captain; who saw a potential conflict occurring with the aircraft preceding them.

A corporate jet pilot reported the VNY Runway 16R ILS/LOC frequency 111.3 erroneously reported an 86.3 DME on a 6.4 NM final at 3;450 feet. The I-VNY ILS/LOC does not transmit a DME signal and so the false DME can be confusing for some flight crews.
Seattle Center Controller reported a situation where an aircraft was below the MIA and above the MEA; resulting in the MSAW to alarm.
CRJ-200 Captain reported that the First Officer failed to follow FARs and company policy by looking at his cell phone while on an active taxiway and during sterile cockpit.

B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft with an MEL for center tank fuel quantity due to the erroneous indications produced affecting the FMC and PFD airspeed limits. An inoperative center tank fuel quantity would have been acceptable.
BE33 pilot reported multiple bird strikes during landing rollout.

A320 Captain reported donning on oxygen mask after experiencing mild fumes during descent. The Captain further expressed concerns about hesitation of Flight Attendants to call-in unfit for duty due to fears of reprimand from management.
An E170 First Officer reported that there was an inset on the approach plate which included another IAF (initial approach fix) that was missed.

First Officer reports his EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) failed and requested the applicable trip charts to be emailed. His Dispatcher reported he later determined either an EFB should have been borrowed or the pilot replaced. An EFB is required.
Pilot reported an issue with an overlying TFR at 47N.
A general aviation pilot reported the challenge of approaching and landing at a busy airport particularly with unfavorable wind conditions.

A Flight Attendant suffered anaphylactic shock following a full day's flights on an aircraft returned to service that morning following two days of maintenance. The aircraft had areas of wet paint; but the etiology of the anaphylaxis is unknown.
Flight Attendant reported observing multiple events of non-compliance with face mask wearing among passengers and crew members; and cited a lack of policy enforcement as a contributing factor.
EMB-140 flight crew reported responding to a TCAS Resolution Advisory during climb triggered by a military fighter.
B787 flight crew reported they were enroute to their destination when Dispatch asked them to divert to a different city due to routing needs.
ZDV ARTCC Controller reported being unable to communicate with aircraft due to faulty transmitters during mid-shift while working combined sectors.
Mechanic reported that management disregarded Maintenance Manual procedures and assigned a maintenance task to be accomplished in conflict with published procedures.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that while searching for a passenger's phone the passenger was left on the plane without the minimum crew required on board.

A ramp agent reported having to unload a Boing 737 without enough help. Also he didn't have a tail stand available so he took extra time and hooked up the tug to keep the nose on the ground. All of this led to him exceeding the expected unloading time of 25 minutes and he was approached by management about why this had happened.
A Maintenance Technician reported that used consumable parts are tested and re-certified using bogus procedures.
Dispatchers reported problems with taking an aircraft out of storage and an associated MEL that was not applied before takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude warning from tower despite cockpit instruments showing that they were above the RNAV glidepath.
ZDC Center Controller reported a loss of separation between two aircraft while working 6 sectors.
A Center Controller reported being required to issue lengthy reroutes to aircraft around Mazatlan Center due to equipment outage.
Center Controller reported that under-staffing due to COVID-19 has resulted in numerous aircraft reroutes.
Mechanic reported that lack of proper equipment may lead to a major fuel spill when obtaining the fuel sample for the pre-flight fuel freeze test.

A flight crew tried to help a Flight Attendant solve a dilemma posed by a passenger wishing to bring aboard a number of lithium batteries. The flight attendant Manual had a chart which provided guidance but unfortunately; they misinterpreted the chart.
Flight Attendant reported the disinfecting spray is making individuals nauseous.
B737 flight crew reported an FMS anomaly in its database resulting in non-standard power reductions after takeoff.
P80 TRACON Controller reported an IFR arrival had to respond to a TCAS alert with a VFR parachute jump aircraft even though the Controller had attempted to vector the aircraft away.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while 8 miles in trail of a heavy in the vicinity of SMO on arrival to LAX.
Air Carrier office employee reported the office area for work would benefit from the installation of a higher-grade air filter to combat COVID-19.
Technicians reported log book entries were not made before returning the aircraft to service after scheduled maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported returning to gate because a child passenger would not conform to face mask policy and the passenger's father was confrontational with the flight attendants.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with aircraft cleaning and sanitizing procedures.

New York Center Controller reported at the end of a night shift they had an overwhelming workload and put an adjacent facility into holding because they were forced to work the entire area airspace combined due to lack of staffing.

During descent a B737 Leading Edge Flap Transit light illuminated. The rushed crew failed to complete a new iPad Boeing QRH procedure requiring they inhibit the GPWS and Flap Inhibit. The GPWS Warning sounded on final.
Controller reported that a pilot reported encountering a drone while on a visual approach to New Orleans Lakefront airport.
Approach Controller reported that radar targets were showing rapid erroneous speed readouts and were jumping around the radar displaying false heading changes.

A ramp crew member reports his concerns about a safety hazard caused by the 100 percent Oversteer Warning System siren sounding off inside the cab of their supertugs during aircraft towing operations. The loud; shrieking siren sound overwhelms the Ground Controller; Ramp Controller and Tug Operator's efforts to communicate with each other; especially when crossing active runways and taxiways. The siren cannot be turned off unless the aircraft is disconnected from the Supertug.
PWM TRACON Controller reported their information display system had out of date approach chart information which caused them to allow an aircraft to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

PCT Supervisor reported failing to not make the correct system macro entries after de-combining a sector. The Restricted Area status displayed not in use on the Controller's radar display when it was actually in use. The Controller allowed an aircraft to enter the Restricted Area.

Pilot reports of confusion caused by similar sounding waypoint names while on the FOOXX4 Arrival into TPA. The pilot hears; reads back; and proceeds to the wrong waypoint after being cleared by Approach Control.
A320 series pilot reported that they did not receive a response to their ACARS message or the operations radio frequency.

The flight crew of an air carrier twin jet departing MKE on the ACCRA RNAV SID failed to note the restriction to maintain 250 knots until advised by ATC. They cited the format of the chart as a contributing factor.
ZLC ARTCC Controller reported relieving a sector that the previous Controller had approved an aircraft on a non-radar route below the minimum enroute altitude.
B737 Maintenance Technician reported observing incorrect installation of life rafts on the aircraft.

Captain reported that Lithium Metal batteries can be carried onboard the aircraft under IATA shipper exemption UN 3090 Section II but not declared on the Dangerous Goods paperwork. The reporter recommended a change in policy.
Maintenance Inspector reported concerns with company signing off of annual inspections of aircraft with several known maintenance issues and discrepancies.
A Controller reported a loss of separation following a military formation flight break up.

Air carrier First Officer reported the taxiway between Taxiway D and the ramp; between Spot 9 and 10 is labeled as D5 on airport signage and on the 10-9 airport diagram but labeled as D2 on the 10-9B and 10-9D charts.
Air carrier flight crew reported they flew a different route than expected and Center stated the mix-up was an ATC error.
A319 Captain reported observing snow accumulation on the flaps after de-icing. Returned to gate for second de-icing.

A Captain reported receiving a traffic alert (TA) during climbout. This was followed by an RA which commanded a deviation from the cleared climb profile. After the RA was resolved; a normal climb was resumed.
Heavy transport aircraft First Officer reported GPS failures on the approach at this foreign international airport.
ZMA controllers and flight crew reported ATC display indicating erroneous mode C of a CL300. ATC issued control instructions for separation based on their display information.
Pilots report of an equipment stuck microphone that was used between the cockpit and the cabin. The problem could not be rectified so they had to divert and landed in an emergency condition.
Tower Controller reported Quality Assurance staff suggested a pilot initiated go-around due to preceding traffic was an ATC Operational Error.
ERJ flight crew reported that failure to correctly configure the aircraft for towing resulted in damage.
Technician reported electronic logbook allowed him to sign off an entry that required two technicians for completion.

Pilot reported an ongoing problem with loss of radio communications with Anchorage Approach while flying near a commercial radio station that reportedly bleeds through the ATC frequencies.  Reportedly; the radio station is located 5 NM south of PABV airport.
B737NG Captain reported conflicting and confusing speed restrictions on the chart for the SNSHN THREE SID.
ZOB Controller reported an issue with another controller berating a D-Side; stating that the aircraft in question did not need to be pointed out; when in fact the aircraft did require a point out.

A Captain reports their A320 MEL and Flight Manuals are not specific enough to determine if an MEL 29-30-03F of an ECAM Hydraulics Page Indications; System Pressure is appropriate for the system failure being addressed. First Officer (FO) had noticed on the ECAM HYD Page that a line extending up from the blue pump was green instead of amber as it should be with no Blue System pressure.
A320 Captain reported that MEL items applied to the aircraft were confusing and did not apply to the situation as written.

An air carrier crew departed and struck a bird flock between 5;000 feet and 6;000 feet. Because of engine and airframe vibration the crew returned to departure airport where damage was found to the radome; #2 engine fan blades; the right wing and winglet.
Captain reported that use of a company-required app on iPad depletes the battery rapidly.

CE560 Captain laments his company's policy of rapid schedule changes that in this case; may have led to an airborne conflict when the crew is told to return to their departure airport twenty minutes after takeoff.

The pilot of a low wing aircraft towing a glider reported a near miss with a high wing aircraft at 2;600 feet. The glider pilot detected the traffic visually but was unable to quickly alert the tow pilot. The glider's FLARM collision avoidance system was also OFF.
Corporate Captain reported that parking outside of the MIA customs office was not authorized due to the size of the aircraft.
First officer and Captain reported of a mistake in typing an intersection name that caused ATC to question where they were going. Similar fixes separated by 27 miles and only different by one letter.

Boston Tower Controller reported of a problem with an aircraft not getting the correct clearance and turning unexpectedly off the route many hours later. Reporter stated that while a Traffic Management Coordinator (TMC) is absent from the control room; the flight data position will take over those duties. Reporter did not like this and wanted the procedure changed.

An ADS-B equipped aircraft in oceanic airspace was deviating for weather approximately 100 miles off course. The Advanced Technologies and Oceanic Procedures (ATOP) system is a radar Data processing system for ATC to use in Oceanic airspace where there is no radar coverage. It relies on Automatic Dependent Surveillance (ADS) equipped aircraft transmitting position reports and forecasts routes and possible conflictions. Due to the extensive deviation of this flight for weather the ATOP system was unable to accurately track the position and projected route of this aircraft. The controllers realized there may be an error in reporting and descended a different aircraft along the route. Once they verified an accurate position of the deviating aircraft they realized it would have been in confliction with the other aircraft which they had descended.
