Controllers figured something was wrong and called the other ATC facility to check on the release. The other ATC facility sterilized the airspace and the aircraft became airborne. Controller wants the land lines fixed so this will not happen again.

TRACON Controller expressed frustration with being cited on an operational error based on a wake turbulence separation violation. The Controller believed the 5 miles separation did not apply due to the aircraft passing 1000 feet above and across the heavy aircraft's trail; not following it directly as the regulation states. The Controller pointed out that there is no published standard for this situation in the 7110.65 and suggested that the FAA address this.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported operational issues could arise as a result of software anomalies.
Pilots reported departing without clearance in what they thought was an uncontrolled airport resulting in runway incursion and airspace violations.
ZTL Controller reported inadequate spacing between two arrivals near CLT and procedures should be revised.
SLC Tower Controller reported that in addition to the ASDE-X being out of service; controllers were short staffed and overwhelmed causing an increasingly unsafe environment.
EMB-145LR first officer reported a NMAC with a possible UAV during descent.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported runway lights and taxiway signage inoperative.
ZLA Center Controller reported insufficient radio coverage in their airspace caused confusion when vectoring an aircraft that was diverting due to weather descended below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
A Super Tug driver reported exhaust fumes entering the tug causes them to open the windows making radio communications difficult.
A B737-900 Captain reported a Dispatch alert not to deplane passengers at the destination until advised because of a potential 'tipping aircraft.' No one seemed to have details about the issue.
A concerned citizen reported the aircraft warning light on some cell towers have not been operating.
SDF Controller reports that numerous aircraft were reporting in with an incorrect ATIS code which they had received from ACARS. Controller was confused as to why he didn't know about this.
Airline crew departing LAS in vicinity of ROPPR intersection encountered several rocket launches that were a distraction and potential hazard.
Air Carrier Captain reported non-adherence to proper aircraft maintenance procedures.
B757 flight crew reported that they smelled smoke in the aircraft; which dissipated after a while.
Captain reported that vital information regarding the visual approach to MSO is inconspicuous in the company issued charts.

Air carrier Captain reported his First Officer became very nauseous during flight after eating a meal prepared in MPTO by the same vendor; not refrigerated; and without time of preparation/expiration specified.
SCT Controller reported intentionally turning an aircraft below the MVA because the aircraft was over the ocean and not in unsafe proximity to terrain.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving two GPWS warnings; followed by a low altitude alert from ATC while on final approach.
An air taxi flew the SIT VOR DME-A Approach and after executing a missed approach overshot the missed approach altitude because of terrain warning; possibly false; and conflicting traffic.

B737-800 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple bird strikes on departure. After landing 'significant damage' was found to the right engine cowl and fan blades.

A B737 pilot flying a SEA Runway 16R visual approach in the ILS APP Mode noted a track deviation and while manually flying back to centerline noticed the Runway 34L ILS identifier was active. ATC reported the 34L ILS was supposed to be off line.
B737 Captain reported confusion after receiving a speed assignment from ATC and described difficulty to meet crossing restrictions after having the speed reduced.
Tow tractor driver reported the tow tractor brakes failed during push-back.
A commercial pilot reports the current Jeppesen airport page for BRO does not reflect the current identification of the airport runways.
SJC Tower Controller reported they took a handoff from TRACON of an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and received a Low Altitude Alert.
A flight crew and Dispatcher reported that the flight crew were pressured into flying a B737 with inadequate cockpit lighting.
Lear 55 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL320 in ZLA airspace.
A Center Controller reported losing radio contact with numerous aircraft during a busy period in an area with known poor radio coverage that has not been fixed.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with wearing masks during pre-flight crew briefings.
Air Carrier Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on short final at DEN.
ERJ-175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence at touchdown in MIA that resulted in a left and right roll so a go-around was executed.

The charted altitudes on UM423 between GNA and BVI differ between two sets of information. One shows the MORA (Minimum Off-Route Altitude) to be 15200 feet and the other says to descend to 10000 feet.

ELM TRACON Controller reports of an airborne conflict that was observed and resolved. The Controller did not point out the aircraft to an adjacent facility which owned the airspace that was descended into and did not get control of the aircraft from the overlying Center sector.
GA flight instructor reported a slight course divergence due to strong cross winds could cause a possible loss of separation.  Reporter recommended the departure procedure be redesigned.

ZFW Controller reported trying to relieve another controller decided he should work as that Controller's Assist instead. The assist controller observed the radar controller commit several airspace violations; incorrect frequency change procedures and an inability to work the radio equipment.
B737 Flight Attendant reported lacking the adequate time to safely perform required cabin service on short flights in turbulent weather.
Maule MX7 pilot reported a 'close proximity' event to skydivers in the vicinity of Petersburg airport.
Air carrier Captain reported that the flight crew count on a trip pairing had been reduced and cited multiple issues associated with the reduction.

A pilot reported that while taxiing to the gate he was notified by a Flight Attendant of a fire in the cabin. The aircraft was stopped on the taxiway and the source of the fire was identified as an E-cigarette device. The fire was quickly extinguished and the aircraft proceeded to the gate where the passengers disembarked in an orderly manner.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a RA on approach to LGB Runway 30 from GA traffic.
B747 Captain reported an aircraft was dispatched with a faulty radio altimeter; causing erroneous GPWS warnings.
A Center Controller reported the Approach Control descended an aircraft to a different altitude than what was coordinated which placed the aircraft below the Minimum Safe Altitude.
Flight instructor reports identical frequencies are used at several airports in close proximity.
TRACON Controller reported an unsafe operation along with pressure induced by a relieving controller.

ZDC Controller reported being so busy that the operation became out of control. Aircraft were arriving from two sectors; one of which was not handing some of their aircraft off. The operations managers were not aware of the situation.
B737 Captain reported a bird strike that damaged the #1 engine while on approach in IMC .
B737-700 Captain reported difficulty finding their assigned standard taxi route after landing at ATL; citing unintuitive indexing practices used in their EFB as contributing.
Air carrier Captain reported multiple system switches are set in the wrong position on aircraft coming out of storage.

Flight Attendant reported a very sick child in the back of the aircraft during deplaning; but they could not contact the forward Flight Attendant because the power had been turned off by the flight crew. Climbing over seats was the only solution.
Multiple high transmitter towers within vicinity of an airport lying under Class C airspace contributed to a Part 91 flights possible inadvertent airspace intrusion.
Flight Attendant reported that during arrival in LGA they had to hold the aircraft door handle down to prevent the gate agent from opening the door before it was disarmed.
A B737 Captain reported concern over his company's dispatch procedure for Caribbean Island flights related to fuel and estimated time over waypoints.
Light Sport pilot encountered a UAV near a MOA.
LGB Tower Controller reported not detecting that an inbound aircraft had lined up to the departure end of the runway due to unreliable radar coverage.

Air carrier Captain reported they were assigned the MDW FISSK4 RNAV Arrival; STERE transition to Runway 22L. His aircraft's FMC no longer contained the RNAV Z (GPS) 22L because the 238 degree approach track transition to the runway created the potential for a low altitude unstabilized approach.
Flight Attendant reported the flight crew was replaced for fatigue following a stressful in-flight issue; but the flight attendants were not.

ZID Controller reported losing the functionality of their D-side's keyboard. This lead to being overworked while the D-side had to figure out why the keyboard was not working. It was determined that someone had entered a security function that made the keyboards stop working.
Corporate pilot reported ATC questioned the crew on the clearance during climb on the SJC LOUPE4 departure.

A B737-800 Captain and his flight's Dispatcher have different opinions about applying an ice accretion weight penalty to the landing weight when ice was only encountered during climb and the Flight Operations Manual (FOM) does not provide clear guidance.
A VFR pilot reported observing an air carrier inbound to a nearby airport and altered course to avoid it.

A B737-800 departed with two seats too few for passengers; crew; two trainee flight attendants and a Flight Attendant jump seater. Gate agents failed to account for the trainees and jump seater having given their seats to passengers.
ZAB Center Controller reported of a mid-shift that was busy with weather deviations. Controller reported being so busy that he could not coordinated as needed.
PA-20 pilot operating on skis lost directional control on the icy surface while clearing the runway and struck a runway edge light.
ZDC Controller and B777 First Officer reported the wrong aircraft accepted a climb clearance due to similar sounding call signs.
ZAB ARTCC Controller reported staffing issues; TMU lack of airspace knowledge and social distancing concerns.

A flight instructor and his student encountered a UAV/Drone while practicing training maneuvers. They announced its presence on a local CTAF and; along with other aircraft in the practice area; departed as a precaution.
CRJ-200 Captain reported flying with an incorrect seat lap belt part installed.
A ZAU Center Controller reported that sectors were not being staffed in accordance with guidelines.
B777 Captain reported a center of gravity anomaly prior to taxiing which could not be resolved to satisfaction.
Air carrier flight crew reported that ramp agents are not following Standard Operating Procedures.

A Captain on approach to Bagram reported a TCAS RA 'LEVEL OFF' command from a Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) as his aircraft was vectored for landing Runway 03 while the airport was under a rocket attack.
A pilot reported that on approach there appeared a pole with a red light mounted atop it; ahead of the left wing.

A319 Captain reported slight damage to the static wick on the left winglet resulted from contact with a catering truck during the parking maneuver. Reporter stated the automated park system (APIS Safe Park) gave no warning that the truck was in a potential collision area.
Air carrier flight crew reported sighting a UAV while conducting the ILS Runway 6 at RSW. The drone was estimated to be within 100 ft of the aircraft; however no evasive action was required.
BE300 Captain reported receiving two RAs while departing HHR from separate aircraft. The pilot believes he should not have been IFR released with traffic in the area.
EMB-145 First Officer reported incorrect logbook entry and time pressure caused Maintenance Personnel to attempt to apply an incorrect MEL; resulting in a flight delay.
GA pilot reported a NMAC that required an evasive maneuver while operating between the ceiling of Delta airspace and the floor of Bravo airspace.

San Antonio TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft flying in close proximity to the final for SAT; caused him to take aircraft off final and sequence again to avoid traffic. Reporter suggested the Class C needs modification or develop a Class B for the area.
Flight Attendant reported being advised about a troublesome passenger before the flight and stated the passenger refused to wear a mask claiming a medical condition.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported they could not respond to a flight crew in a timely manner due to weather and excessive workload.
B737 Captain reported tow bar had released from the nose gear connection point during pushback; necessitating the gradual use of brakes to bring the aircraft to a stop.
Air Carrier flight crew reported departing without knowledge the release was to an alternate destination instead of the planned destination.

Maintenance requested approval from Engineering to remove and cap a damaged hydraulic line and deactivate the associated brake. A written approval was issued but there was some confusion as to where to disconnect and cap the line and also as to which MEL reference should be used. Maintenance only received part two of the approval and did not receive part one; which led to the confusion. The brake was deferred properly and measures were taken to ensure this doesn't happen in the future.
PC12 flight crew reported that confusion over similar sounding waypoints resulted in a clearance deviation.
A Center Controller reported a recurring problem of an adjacent Center sector handing them off aircraft on converging heading at the same altitude; causing an airborne conflict.
A pilot performing acrobatic maneuvers reported a NMAC in the area southeast of HND.
Air carrier First Officer and an airline employee reported IOE training was done by a Captain who had not received his certification by the FAA to be a check airman.
Captain reported an ECAM message in flight; HYD G RSVR LO LVL and HYD G ENG 1 PUMP LOPR; causing an inflight shut down and a precautionary landing.
Helicopter pilot experiences an NMAC with a UAV during a night departure from EMT; about 200 feet AGL. Evasive action is taken.
An instructor reported a close encounter with a drone (UAV) at an altitude of 5;000 feet; requiring an evasive maneuver.
Air carrier First Officer reported confusing taxi and parking information and non-standard communication procedures at LGA airport.
ATC Center Controller reported the work area was understaffed for the level of traffic.
S46 TRACON Controller reported failure to notice an aircraft's incorrect altitude readback; resulting in a loss of separation.
Pilot reported a confusing depiction of a crossing restriction on the VOR Runway 10 approach plate for CLL airport.
Saint Louis TRACON Controller reported that the Saint Louis ASR and Mode S was released to maintenance. An aircraft reported a Near Mid Air Collision with a VFR aircraft whose Mode C was fluctuating.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported being task saturated by multiple distractions while flight planning multiple long range ETOPS flights to include a work around required when generating flight plans in order for the flight plans to generate correctly.
Following a track deviation detected by ATC; an air carrier crew began NAVAID navigation along J18 and determined the military had deactivated the GPS. ECAM and NAV accuracy alerts were received.
ZOB ARTCC Controller observed adjacent sectors changing the assigned altitude in the data block of an aircraft still under the Controller's jurisdiction in violation of FAAH 7110.65.
Turbojet flight crew reported encountering communications difficulties with Center while approaching a mountainous terrain airport.

A pilot received a clearance from ATC using non-standard phraseology and was unsure if they could begin their descent on the approach. The pilot was also issued incomplete crossing traffic information. The aircraft received a TA and stopped its descent for the traffic that the pilot reports passed 700 feet below them.
A320 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing a bird strike climbing through 5;000 feet. Ground inspection revealed damage to the left engine.
B747 Captain reported iPad malfunctions in flight and during taxi.
Boston Center Controller reported an air carrier flew through Boston Center's airspace for 135 miles unidentified.
A C680 Captain recommended that his company add a procedure in the QRC for an engine failure on final approach.
E175 Captain reported declaring minimum fuel to expedite ATC handling during a divert due to weather at destination.

At about 1;000 feet after departure; a B737-700 encountered a bird flock which caused left engine surging. The aircraft returned to the departure airport where severe left engine damage was discovered as well as bird remains in the functioning right engine.
Air carrier First Officer of a large turbojet reported descending below an MSA during a visual approach to OKC.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing an airborne conflict on approach to landing that required immediate evasive action to avert a collision.

Local Controller reported the same call sign being used for an aircraft on final as well as another aircraft taxiing for takeoff. Reporter states this is an ongoing problem at this facility and causes confusion for the controllers and pilots. Furthermore; the departure aircraft data tag will not acquire on the departure controllers radar display when this occurs. This is distracting and causes confusion for ATC. Contributing to the situation is dispatchers using the same call sign for an outbound aircraft when the aircraft that is supposed to fly that outbound leg is delayed while inbound.
Air carrier First Officer reported an uncommanded roll shortly after takeoff due to the exhaust stream from a parked aircraft with engines running pointed toward the departure runway.
Challenger 300 First Officer reported navigational problems relating to FMS and GPS issues.

A Captain reported having trouble hearing the Controller on a frequency he was assigned. This frequency is also an arrival frequency for LAX arrivals. The Captain reported of bleed over the frequency and wasn't sure if he was receiving all the transmissions from ZAB Center.
LAX Tower Controller reported one of the Ground Control frequencies receives bleed-over from another frequency from certain aircraft.
Center Controller reported work overload and forgetting to issue advisories due to lack of staffing.
Maintenance Technician reported an aircraft departed the previous station with an open write-up that was not properly addressed.

Aircraft landed on taxiway at night. Pilot asked Tower if they were lined up with the runway. At night with the current light settings it appeared to the reporter that the aircraft was aligned with the runway.
PA-31 Instructor Pilot reported an altitude deviation occurred following a wake turbulence encounter at FL250.
B737-700 Captain reported a slight heading deviation resulted from a wake turbulence encounter on approach into ATL.
Denver TRACON Controller reported the ATC Computer allowed two aircraft to be tagged up on the same code; which led to confusion and airspace violations.
Maintenance was unaware of wiring behind structure when drilling out holes to install panel.

An Aircraft Maintenance Technician recommended his Air Carrier fix the malfunctions with their IPAD/Digital communication software program that cleared a 'System-2 Regulator' fault discrepancy instead of deferring the Logbook item.

B737-700 Flight Attendant reported that the new storage location for the emergency medical kit does not allow the kit to be accessed in flight due to conflict with the slide bustle when the door is closed.
A319 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after a bird strike damaged the left engine on departure.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger came on board the aircraft with a non approved face mask. A proper face mask was provided to the passenger.
Small aircraft pilot reported a NMAC with a UAV.

CRJ-700 Captain reported a wake turbulence encounter departing DCA that resulted in loss of roll control for several seconds at low altitude just after takeoff. Reporter suggested a minimum of one minute separation between aircraft on departure.
Air Carrier flight crew reported being confused by crew scheduling if they were legal to fly.
After being cleared for the SERFR1 STAR and then the Tipp Toe Visual into SFO; Medium Transport FO noted a 1;000 foot difference in the MENLO crossing altitude between the two approaches.
Beech 99 Captain flying into SLC reported executing a missed approach at 500 feet AGL after encountering wake turbulence from the preceding A319.
Captain reported disagreement with MEL52-XX being accurate.

N90 TRACON Controller reported of a Near Mid Air Collision. The reporter stated this was the fault of the Tower not advising departing traffic of IFR traffic in their flight path. Reporter would like a change in the HPN airspace and better procedures for the HPN controllers to follow.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft maintenance manuals did not have a step to open fan cowls while working on thrust reversers.
Two Anchorage Center Controllers reported problems related to SIGMETS and volcanic ash.
TRACON Controller reported they mistakenly issued an altitude to an aircraft which resulted it in flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 Captain reported concerns with the company's new fuel policy of decreasing fuel loads under the average for the last few years.
Air carrier flight crew reported issues with a Center being ATC Zero and having to divert to obtain more fuel to reach their destination airport.
B737 Captain reported he was required by company to refuse the aircraft before Maintenance would change a main gear tire that had cord showing.
Dispatcher reported being out of the loop when a MEL was not listed in the Dispatchers software and was missing from the flight release.
B737 Captain reported that the fuel system condition did not match the requirements in the fuel pump deferral.
Helicopter pilot questions how to transport prisoners that may become or are combative during or before a flight.
DA42 pilot reported they may have entered SFO Bravo airspace without a clearance while troubleshooting the G1000 traffic information system.
HIO Tower Controller reported a problem with the ATIS recording equipment and the new requirement to transmit the full ATIS information when the recording is not available.
ZDC Controller reported a sector was overloaded at the end of the mid-shift and no one was there to help the Controller.
Technician reported that two life vests were found in the aircraft galley during boarding and resulted in a flight delay so that an inspection can be performed.

Corporate jet Captain reported before departing ATL on the KAJIN 2 SID they were questioned about their first fix multiple times. On departure; Tower commented about them not following the procedure; but the aircraft had not reached the point where the automation planned on starting the turn yet.
Air carrier Captain reported the paper weight and balance forms used on charter operations; believing that an iPad application would be much less error prone.

Air Carrier crew reported receiving GPWS terrain warning during vectors to ILS runway 27 [into] GEG. Crew performed escape maneuver. Altitude cleared to was below the Minimum Sector Altitude for that sector.
Air carrier Captain reported PDC uplink to aircraft FMC was incorrect based on route required by ATC. Problem was reported as ongoing at this location.
Pilot reported that the Flight Service Station (FSS) frequency associated with CIU Airport is not correct in FAA and Jeppesen charts and needs to be updated.
Air carrier Captain reported that Jeppesen RNAV Arrival charts for MMUN do not include the additional waypoints after the transition points.
GA pilot reported a rejected take-off resulted in a runway excursion and contact with a perimeter fence.
An AMT's thumb was amputated when an actuator on a passenger seat he was working on inadvertently moved.

Air Carrier flight crew reported they were cleared for the RNAV (GPS) Z Runway 17 approach to MTJ in visual conditions. They deviated from their IFR clearance to avoid an aircraft on final without obtaining clearance for a visual approach from Denver Center.
Tailwheel pilot reported a prop strike occurred during taxi when he encountered wake turbulence or jet blast from several B737 aircraft operating in the vicinity.
A319 Captain reported that the Pilot Reconciliation Report can be accessed electronically.
PIC reported encountering a drone approaching SLI VOR.
Pilot reported ATC being too busy to communicate with them while attempting to request a clearance to descend.
ZOB Center Controller reported sectors in their area were unsafe during weather deviations due to insufficient flow control and lack of Supervisor's proficiency.
Air carrier First Officer reported a GPWS event during approach to ROA while in visual conditions.
Captain reported a lack of consensus regarding his decision to deny boarding to an intoxicated passenger.
Albuquerque Center Controller reported of parachute operations that were being conducted within the arrival routes and the confusion surrounding the operations.
Air carrier Pilot Flying reported refusing aircraft due to streaks of oily substance being present on the aircraft's exterior despite Technician stating otherwise.
ATC reported flight flew through final approach course and descended below their assigned Minimum Vectoring Altitude; ATC Low Altitude Alert did not activate in a timely manner.
ZGSZ Ground Control cleared a B777 to taxi K2; A; Hold Short of A5 then via Route 1; D; C1; Holding Point 15. The crew found no Route 1 or published taxi routes on any available charts.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a terrain warning during approach to ROA.
CRJ-900 maintenance technicians reported they did not have the proper equipment to safely replace an APU battery.

A pilot in a flight of two C-337's at 13;000 feet reported detecting the other pilot's conversational hypoxia symptoms so they descended and diverted. The Mountain High O2D2 regulator was a decade old and not serviced regularly.
B737 flight crew reported the flight deck door came open upon rotation due to a switch being in the wrong position.
A flight crew reported that their aircraft was struck by an eagle during the takeoff roll. The takeoff was successfully rejected and the aircraft returned to the hangar for damage assessment.
B777 Captain reported that when the autopilot autoland was tested on several aircraft; the aircraft touched down to the right of centerline on EWR Runway 4R.
E170 Captain reported aircraft flew six flights with an open write up.

ZFW controller reports of an aircraft that doesn't climb because of a false target being displayed on their TCAS. Controller had climbed aircraft to requested altitude and then became traffic for another aircraft because it didn't climb to requested altitude. Pilot told controller this problem happened with the aircraft before. Event lead to a loss of separation.
COS Tower CIC reported an airborne conflict when an arriving aircraft on final approach disappeared from the controller's radar display due to a scheduled radar outage; limiting radar coverage.
A B737-700 Captain reported the left wing fuel tank transferred fuel into the Center tank creating an imbalance. A checklist procedure did not exist for the anomaly.
New EMB-170 Captain reports being assigned to fly the majority of her flights after training as a First Officer and believes this is an unsafe practice due to inability to consolidate the learning.
B757 Captain reported being dispatched with a lost cell phone in the cabin; and expressed concern they did not have enough information to make a decision on whether to accept the aircraft.
SR20 pilot reported while climbing to avoid traffic they inadvertently flew into SFO Class B airspace.
A B737 flight crew reported they encountered unforecasted turbulence and several flight attendants were injured.
A319 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff on ORD Runway 22L at approximately 85 kts after encountering probable wake turbulence from aircraft landing on 28C.
JFK Controller reported two aircraft with nearly identical call signs caused the wrong flight to be coordinated.
ZME Center Controller reported a radio problem that is ongoing and has not been corrected.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported passenger carry-on bag containing 80 lbs of Lithium Ion batteries caused concern due to weight and insufficient containment bags available to extinguish a Lithium Ion battery fire in the event of an in flight fire. Reporter further stated there is no guidance in Flight Attendant Manuel regarding weight/quantity restrictions.
Ramp worker reported their Supervisor made them temporarily open a closed ramp to park an airplane during a period of lightning strikes.
BOI TRACON Controller reported commercial fixed wing failed to turn adequately to avoid higher MVA. ATC sector does not display weather to assist aircraft safely avoid weather.
Technician reported that employees are not being issued PPE; especially masks; which are needed to avoid contracting the COVID-19 virus.
An A320 pilot had been unable to update his IPad at several different locations. An FAA inspector; during the course of a line check; showed him how to use the airport Wi-Fi to update his IPad.
S46 TRACON Controller reported canceling a Visual Approach clearance to avoid VFR traffic that was not in communication with ATC.
B737-700 Captain reported the Decision Altitude for the RNP Z runway 30L increased to two different values after two different NOTAMS were applied.
Air carrier Captain reported that a passenger who was approved to fly displayed disruptive behavior before and during the flight.
ZLC Center Controller reported an unsafe situation when the sector was understaffed by two people and the radios went out.
While on approach into Las Vegas; flight crew experienced ambiguity in required altitudes between STAR and RNAV Visual approach charts.
A320 Captain reported MMMX 05R Approach does not load properly in the FMCGS.
Air carrier Captain reported temporary loss of ADS-B and GPS likely due to jamming. Captain recommended disseminating information to all flight crews.
Widebody transport Captain reported that they were instructed to go-around due to base under attack.
Air carrier Captain reported the fix AEROS is missing from their Jeppesen database.
A320 Captain reported many interference calls on ACARS; which blocked ATC calls on communication 1.
Air Carrier First Officer reported missing a clearance during taxi-out due to possible radio interference at FLL airport.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict resulting from similar sounding callsign confusion.
Ramp Supervisor reported an aircraft being towed with a towbarless tug came out of its cradle and went out of control. The aircraft came to a stop before making contact with any objects.

Air Carrier Captain reported crossing into LTAA airspace at VESAR; then detected a note on the VESAR way point requiring a call ten minutes prior to entering Turkish airspace. The reporter suggested that important notes be highlighted in some way on the chart; so as not to be missed.
Helicopter pilot reported being distracted during preflight due to concern about avoiding COVID-19 and left the chocks at the departure heliport.

ZME Controller reports of an overdue aircraft that he becomes aware of. Controller checks information and finds later that this aircraft is at a different airport than he expected. Controller noted that two fixes may have helped the automation problem; RUNDE and RUNOE.
Center Controller reported there was no official FAA guidance regarding how to handle traffic in the airspace when the facility went ATC zero for COVID-19 cleaning.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS warning while on approach over terrain at night.
ZLC ARTCC mandated that controllers cannot approve weather deviations for aircraft not in radar coverage unless the aircraft is given special attention.
Flight Attendant reported the flight crew did not wear face masks on the flight deck while the Flight Attendant was assisting with lavatory breaks.
Two flight attendants reported having to work a flight a few days later after being on a flight with a passenger who had tested positive for COVID-19.
Ground crew reported that during pushback of an aircraft the tow bar malfunctioned and a breakaway occurred.
Flight attendant reported exit door placards are out of compliance and that over sized baggage was stored improperly in violation of FAA regulations.
Air Carrier Captain reported landing without a clearance and attributes the event to lack of flying and complacency while flying with another Captain as the First Officer in this flight.

SAV TRACON Controller reported accepting an automated handoff of an aircraft with 'AMBQ' in the data tag. The actual position of the aircraft was ninety miles northwest of the position of the data tag the controller accepted for handoff.
Pilot reported limited communication with ATC while on the ground leading to unsafe situations.
Corporate pilot reported being distracted by the bright lights from a new golf center near Spirit of St Louis Airport.
Flight Attendant reported that the kit on the aircraft did not have required sanitation supplies or masks. In addition; these supplies are not being replenished in between flights.
First Officer reported issues relating to NOTAMS and towers closing for COVID-19 cleaning.
Air carrier flight crew reported overshooting an assigned altitude during departure and cited the rate of climb due to the aircraft being light in weight as a contributing factor.
CLT TRACON Controller reported that his Supervisor assigned some of his airspace to ZTL Center without his knowledge.
Air carrier Captain reported ZDC controllers tried to clear them with a failed FMS to a VORTAC (PXT) that was out of service.
Dispatcher reported they changed an aircraft's call sign due to a similar sounding call sign departing at the same time.
737-800 captain reported a UAV at 2500 ft. (MSL).
B777-200 Captain reported the charted minimums on the temporary RNAV (GNSS) U Runway 14 chart; for SBGL; are too high to enable a safe landing from a VDP.
Denver Center (ZDV) Controllers report of a loss of separation due to NON RVSM aircraft going through the airspace refueling with a tanker.

A flight crew accepted a visual approach to an airport where they were aware of the high terrain surrounding the airport. On base; they received a terrain warning commanding them to climb. They complied but were still in an adequate position to continue the approach. They stabilized the aircraft on final and landed safely.

The flight crew of a large transport reported that the recent implementation of a new before takeoff checklist resulted in greatly reduced situational awareness by the pilot performing the checklist; and increased the potential for taxi conflicts or mishaps.
Air carrier First Officer reported being exposed to COVID-19 and cited concerns with the company not notifying other employees who may have also been exposed.

Controller reported issuing traffic to an aircraft on a missed approach. Both aircraft were below the MIA and the Controller could not turn the aircraft to avoid traffic. Controller later questioned if he should have done something different.
A light transport flight crew reported entering RVSM airspace with incomplete RVSM paperwork. They realized their mistake; notified ATC; and were then climbed above RVSM airspace.
First Officer of a large passenger aircraft reported getting low on the approach profile while conducting a visual approach at night.

MIA TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft skirting just outside of the Class B airspace and below MIA arrivals was causing issues with the arrivals. The Controller recommended reshaping the Class B.
CRJ-200 Non Flying Pilot reported a system wide GPS and ADS-B failure.
Beech V35 pilot reported noticing unmarked and uncharted power lines have been installed near the final approach course for Runway 35 at CWC Airport.
B787 flight crew reported multiple bird strikes on departure.
An A320 Captain is informed that Taxiway U2 has been designated as U1 at BWI but the runway available from the takeoff data message does not reflect the new designation.
Air carrier flight crew reported returning to departure airport due to a bird strike shortly after takeoff that resulted in failure of the left engine.
Technician reported that a records check discovered an unqualified technician had performed maintenance tasks on an aircraft and did not make the required log book entries.
Maintenance Inspector reported that a Radio Frequency leakage occurred during maintenance ADS-B ground test.
Air carrier First Officer reported missing the IFR release time due to general aviation traffic in the pattern and the Tower being closed.

SFO Controller reported a company restriction to using the TRUKN2 SID that the Controller was not made aware of. Controller reported Supervisor instructed company aircraft to disregard company policy; and reprimanded Controller while on control position for complying with pilot request.
ZBW Center Controller in charge reported an adjacent sector was not complying with flow control instructions to regulate traffic flow due to weather deviations.
C680 flight crew reported an uneventful air return for bird strike during takeoff.
B737-800 flight crew reported that an MEL was not applied correctly causing a fuel imbalance.
Technician reported that incorrect parts were installed on both thrust reversers.
Guam Center Controller reported staffing issues at their facility.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing SNA in trail of an A321.

NCT TRACON Controller reported when two aircraft activated Conflict Alert on their radar display they were unable to separate the overlapped data blocks to issue appropriate control instructions and a Traffic Alert.

ZKC Center Controller reported conducting a flight break up after aerial refueling according to previous procedures; but was advised of a procedural error due to a new interpretation of the FAA guidance.
Air carrier Captain reported a greater than planned fuel burn on an international flight; likely due to erroneous weight and balance calculations; along with deviations for weather.

The Tower did not comply with those requirements.

Flight Attendant expressed concern that attending a scheduled recurrent training class with a group of flight attendants could increase the risk of spreading the COVID-19 virus to other crew members and passengers.
B737-700 Captain reported that the company MELs contain excessive data on unrelated conditions or data pertinent to other aircraft models.
EMB-175 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after ingesting birds into the Number 1 engine. Ground inspection revealed damage to several fan stage blades.
737-800 First officer reported the new checklist; 'Triggers and Flows;' created confusion.
Captain reported that after refusing aircraft; he discovered aircraft assigned to a different crew without any Maintenance actions or logbook entries.
GA pilot reported a NMAC with several jumpers near S33 at 12;500 feet that required evasive action.
A ZBW Center Controller reported they were forced to work several sectors combined during a busy period because the Supervisor would not assign an overtime shift.
C182 pilot reported; on a night takeoff; entering IMC when ATIS reported VMC.
Air carrier flight crew reported that CLT has an inaccurate taxiway signage and diagram.
An Air Carrier pilot reported responding to a EGPWS warning while being vectored at night at the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on a Visual Approach was handed off to the tower while a VFR aircraft departed an adjacent airport and flew into the the path of the Visual Appproach.
B737 First Officer reported the aircraft was stabilized on the PAPI; but was indicating low on the ILS Glideslope.
Tracon Controller reported issuing a vector into a higher MVA.

ZMP Center Traffic Management Unit Supervisor reported the Traffic Management Command Center (ATCSS) would not enact their requests for aircraft reroutes around known weather areas. The Supervisor stated a Center sector was overloaded and unsafe due to the workload created by the weather deviations.
Ground employee reported there is not enough alcohol gel available in the office for employees to use during the COVID-19 pandemic.
PAO Tower Controller reported that an unreliable; aged; failure prone communications panel is unsafe for use.
Pilot reported NOTAMS are five years out of date.
Air carrier Captain reported odor emanating from human remains shipment prior to pushback.
CE560XL pilot reported encountering a navigational error in the company's FMS database and recommend that the vendor providing FMS database be notified of the problem.
Large turbojet flight crew reported communication difficulties with ATC while attempting to land at an international airport. Flight diverted to another airport and landed normally.
C172 pilot reported an incorrectly set altimeter led to a near mid air collision.
An Air Carrier pilot reported their company had four flights with similar sounding call signs departing within a ten minute time period.  The reporter states this problem occurs at all their hubs.
A Center Controller reported taking handoffs on two aircraft they did not recognize were converging at the same altitude due to stress from the government shutdown.
MD-80 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B737-800 on arrival into DFW.
A321 Captain reported encountering a UAV after departing LGA.
A TRACON Controller reported they may have observed a false radar target on their display.
B737-8 Technician reported not replacing a gasket on an oil line during an oil cooler replacement.
First Officer reported windshield wiper failed out of park position; causing a reduction in performance penalty that the crew was unaware of.
Airline Captain reported NMAC with a UAV during the turn to final approach.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a GPS EICAS alert related to possible jamming from White Sands.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) states that aileron flight control cables had frozen on an ERJ-175 aircraft after he had deferred a Forward Galley coffee maker for lack of water. Technician noted the lack of any information in the MEL about deferring coffee makers and the possibility of flight controls freezing needs to be addressed.
EMB-175 flight crew reported exceeding 200 kts below LAX Class B airspace while flying the OHSEA2 RNAV arrival into SNA.
BOI TRACON controllers reported radar vectors were issued to an aircraft into an area below the MVA.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with lack of flying and the possibility of being paired with a First Officer who has also not flown in months.
A B737 Captain reported bright reflections from three solar fields southwest of their departure airport caused temporary blindness during climbout.
B767 Captain described the conditions under which flight planning is completed at a foreign airport with no Company Operations in a small room with inadequate communications.
CRJ-700 Technician reported installing wrong main landing gear piston and axle assembly on aircraft.
Maintenance Technician reported reduced bus schedules are causing overcrowded buses and increasing the possibility of catching COVID-19.
B737 flight crew reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL360 in 8-mile trail of another B737 that resulted in loss of 500-600 feet of altitude.
MLI TRACON Controller reported an aircraft executed a turn wider than they expected and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A319 Captain reported being issued a go-around by CLT Tower below 50 feet. When queried as to the reason for the go-around the Tower stated 'ground radar alert.'
SoCal TRACON Controller reported an airspace violation attributed to the lack of radar through the Banning Pass.
B737 Captain reported that company policy directs the crew to accept to specific international SIDs even though they cannot meet certain altitude restrictions.
Ground employee reported the use of shared headsets exposes ramp employees to COVID-19.

SDF TRACON Controller and SDF Tower Controller reported a close airborne conflict between an IFR aircraft conducting an approach and a VFR aircraft in the vicinity of the final approach course which had just contacted the TRACON Controller. Both Controllers insist the airport needs more restrictive airspace to protect aircraft on the Instrument Final Approach course.
MD-11 Captain reported the company was not taking advantage of maintenace opportunities to clear the many MEL items on the subject aircraft.

B737-700 Captain described the aircraft's condition after cold ground air conditioning operated all night condensing humid air. The flight station; passenger seats; and equipment were dripping liquid which was evaporated with warm APU air conditioning.
A flight crew had difficulty having load planning change maximum take off weight to reflect real time weather conditions.
L30 TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation due to not having an aircraft maintain visual separation while using FUSION Radar.
A general aviation pilot reported ATC advised of an adjacent airspace violation after being given a clearance to land by the Tower with no restriction cited.
Pilot reported flying over a sporting event during a game and wondered if they had violated a TFR.
A TRACON Controller reported radar went out of service unexpectedly and that it is a recurring outage.
Controllers reported a situation where aircraft could not land at their destination due to the unavailability of parking space. Contributing to the problem was poor communication signal.
Controller reported that workload and a system malfunction caused a delay in issuing a low altitude alert to an aircraft that had descended below the MVA.
Dash 8 flight crew reported starting an engine for cabin heat on the ground; then leaving the engine unmonitored when they left the cockpit.

An aircraft departs TRACON Airspace; enters ARTCC Airspace; departs ARTCC Airspace back into TRACON Airspace. Automation software does not process the flight data properly; handing off the data tag to the wrong sector. This also causes confusion for controllers as to what if any verbal coordination is required for aircraft on these type of routes. This is a common problem in ATC facilities everywhere. As of this report software developers have not been able to or have not been tasked with figuring out a remedy to processing these type of flight plans.
NCT TRACON Controller reported working four sectors and failed to notice an aircraft descending to an altitude into conflict with another aircraft; resulting in a loss of separation.
Flight attendants do not believe that their current ground school training philosophy is in compliance with the flight attendant manual procedures.
Maintenance Technician reported confusion and conflicting information regarding an aircraft at their station that was possibly contaminated by Coronavirus.

SCT Controller reports that hundreds of incidents of radar targets jumping away from the aircraft's actual position on the radar display have occurred since the installation of new STARS ATC processing software. Other facilities using this software have also reported the same problem.
CMH TRACON Controller reported descending an aircraft that had a fuel issue below the Minimum vectoring Altitude so they could visually acquire the nearest airport and land.
A320 flight crew reported cockpit windshield heat was MELed and Captain's side window froze allowing no visibility from left hand side windows.
A Maintenance Technician reported that a log book entry may have been signed as accomplished when the work was not done.
ATC Controllers reported after 'ATC Zero' was enacted in their building there were no procedures to assist controllers to safely close sectors.

B777 Captain reported a discrepancy between FOM and Ramp Operations Manual regarding loading dry ice and live animals in cargo compartments. The specific incident was rectified but the conflict between the manuals requires a revision.
A Center Controller reported being instructed to clear an aircraft through an Altitude Reservation area; in conflict with the guidance in 7110.65.
Mechanic reported experiencing questionable maintenance practices.
GA flight instructor reported a wildlife taxi hazard on a taxiway.
A VFR aircraft was not aware of a temporary MOA along its route of flight; until advised by the ZID ARTCC Controller.
Flight Attendant reported communication issues related to wearing a face mask.
DCA FSS Specialist reported a system outage affecting flight safety and described their working environment as stressful due to staffing issues.
Maintenance person reported heavy smoke was noted in the left hand main landing gear area accompanied by smoke that filled the bay after connecting ground power.
Helicopter pilot reported an unsafe situation involving reduced proximity to pedestrians and automobile traffic while operating into a public parking lot for a charity event.
Air carrier Captain reported a confusing published speed restriction on KUSTR RNAV Arrival to BIL contributed to a track heading deviation.
The Tower supervisor reports controllers are not setting the airport lighting in the proper position when they close the Tower at night.
Maintenance Technician reported confusing MEL requirements for crew rest seats.
B777 First Officer reported the autopilot veered off course with a hard bank to the right while on final approach to VABB.
ZAN ARTCC Controller reported their Front Line Manager refused to disseminate pertinent SIGMETs to the appropriate sectors.
B737-700 Captain reported inconsistencies in the procedures regarding Lithium Ion battery powered assisted devices provided to passengers and flight crew's FOM [Flight Operations Manuals.]

An aircraft was dispatched to a foreign destination with the TCAS inoperative but the crew did not realize it until later that the Ops SPEC and MEL prevent dispatch to a foreign destination with the TCAS inoperative.
The Captain reported that Dispatch filed the SKORR SID out of JFK that was rejected by the ATC computer resulting in confusion for the flight crew when receiving their clearance.
G-V Captain reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL320 that resulted in an uncommanded 40 degree bank.
Air Carrier Captain reported being pulled back to the gate to receive 3 passengers who were not on the flight yet due to being transported in a different bus for COVID-19 distancing.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was suffering from the chills and a runny nose during flight.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to wear a proper face mask citing a medical condition as the reason for not wearing it.
Center Controller reported an airborne conflict while working combined sectors and cited handoff related issues contributed to the event.

FAI FLM (Frontline Manager) reported of collision alerts that are caused by the aircrafts ADS-B. FLM states there are multiple nuisance alarms due to aircraft equipped with ADS-B. FLM would like some type of a software upgrade or an equipment upgrade to stop the spurious alerts.
Air carrier Captain reported being confronted with operational issues with a B777 model with which they were unfamiliar. Pilot handbooks provided scant information on the aircraft differences.

A321 Captain reported that a Flight Attendant advised of a passenger loading limitation affecting weight and balance which the Captain was not aware of and which was not noted on the takeoff performance data.
ZJX Center Controller reported needing a D-Side Controller but none were available due to a lack of staffing creating an unsafe workload on the sector.
B757 Captain reported that the Flight Attendants were required to work a 30.5 hour duty day.
Embraer 120 Captain reported that a Convair's wingtip made contact with the rudder of the Embraer as the Convair was taxiing behind the aircraft.
CHA Controller reported being distracted and forgot to turn an aircraft onto the final approach course; causing it to descend below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A321 Captain reported that his company's fuel dispatch protocol does not provide an adequate buffer for inflight exigencies.
An air carrier Dispatcher reported multiple problems with his software program that prevented him from properly handling a flight that encountered weather and diverted to another destination.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft had an NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft in the vicinity of LGB.
B757 Captain reported having difficulty staying awake and alert on an unaugmented transatlantic flight and stated this type of operation is unsafe.

Flight Attendant reported they found a 3/4 used cigarette on the aircraft while doing initial inspections and wondered how the cleaning crew missed it.  Also; there were no alcohol based cleaning/sanitation supplies on the aircraft.
Due to weather conditions and wrong parts sent; Maintenance was unable to make necessary repairs to the damaged wire harness.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an airborne object; either a balloon or UAS; near 10;000 feet while departing MSP.
Ground employee reported concerns about peers not wearing face masks.
An air taxi pilot reported questions related to company scheduling procedures and FAR rest requirements.
Maintenance Technician reported flight crew generated a write up when they found the circuit breakers for the pitot system with CB collars installed. The aircraft had been sitting for three days.

Pilot reported that the Hammond (HMU) VOR has been out of service for an extended time; nonetheless; the NAVAID is still depicted as usable on the current sectional charts.FAA NOTAM states HMU VOR OTS 1801052049-18007052111EST.
Air carrier airport operations crew is confused on who to contact for a landing gear contaminated with snow/ice.
P80 Controller reported numerous unidentified VFR targets routinely fly in close proximity to the final approach course; and suggested that the airspace be evaluated for a redesign.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing difficulty communicating on JAX ARTCC frequency 134.85 since it was blocked by Dulles ATIS; which is the same frequency.

Regional Jet Captain was pressured by management to fly with an APU problem that required a slower speed at altitude very close to the margins. Captain instead required Maintenance to make an adjustment and logbook entry that allowed the use of the APU & normal speeds.
Transport Captain reported an area with lack of communication coverage with ATC.
B737-700 First Officer reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B757 on arrival into DTW.
Maintenance Technician expressed concern over company policy regarding duties of wing walkers during aircraft tow.
Air carrier Captain reported on pushback from CLT that ground crew was utilizing wireless headsets and the Captain was communicating with a push crew from another gate.
Air carrier Captain reported shortcomings in the iPad device that is intended to replace paper charts.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while attempting to land at an airport where the tower was closed due to COVID-19 staffing.
Air carrier pilot reported being distracted by the Tower Controller working Ground and Local Control.
B777 Check Pilot reported the Navigation Display and FMS had different altitudes displayed when compared to the Jeppesen chart for the DIVLA 2A STAR.
B737 Captain reported a discrepancy between the CDU Data on the aircraft and the Jeppesen plate for the SEATTLE SIX departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a CFIT event during approach to MMGL airport.
Balloon pilot reported making contact with a power line on descent for landing. The power line was not seen by the pilot; but was observed by a passenger too late to avoid contact.
Maintenance Technician reported the external equipment hoist failed and damaged the aircraft.
A B-737 flight crew was reported by company as having had a hard landing. The Captain under instruction reported being distracted by the HUD display.
Denver Center Controller reported an issue with similar sounding call signs and a consistent frequency problem in the area.

A Tower Controller was assigned to work combined Ground Control positions during a busy period. A taxiing aircraft reported to the Controller they had to 'slam on the brakes' to avoid conflicting traffic on the taxiway.
CRJ Captain reported being concerned about flying aircraft with too many MELs open.

Air Carrier pilot reports scheduling changing the crews' report time in the computer to not reflect a limo into the first flight; in order to allow the crew to fly the last flight of the day within duty limits. The crew detects the 'error' and the last flight is refused.
Air carrier flight crew reported inadvertently departing with one less Flight Attendant than required for the flight.

Air carrier First Officer expressed concern about the erosion of skills and knowledge while awaiting OE and consolidation flights. A restrictive flight schedule due to the COVID-19 pandemic was cited as contributing.
B777 flight crew reported being dispatched with a MEL restricting airspeed to 270 knots or Mach .82. They felt that the computer flight plan did not reflect these limitations in its calculations.
A321 flight crew reported difficulty taxiing at an unfamiliar airfield due to a scheduled runway closure and lack of taxiway information.
Air carrier pilot stated that the training provided by the company for the new Lido app for the iPad; was inadequate and that critical information often requires too many keystrokes to bring up.

ZLA Controller was notified that an aircraft they cleared for approach to an uncontrolled airport attempted to land on a closed runway. ATC did not ensure the aircraft had the current ATIS information or NOTAMs.
EMB-175 First Officer reported a Flight Attendant felt pressured to continue flight despite an ear blockage.
Corporate jet pilot reported receiving several revised clearances in a short amount of time from a very busy ZLC Center Controller due to GPS jamming occurring in the area.
A320 Captain reported that he was not notified of special needs passengers aboard his flight; causing a potential safety risk in the event of an evacuation.
Center Controller reported weather deviations caused their sector to become overloaded; and Traffic Management software was not working correctly.
A Tower Controller reported allowing an aircraft to land even though the Airport Surface Detection Equipment (ASDE-X) alarmed for preceding go-around traffic.
B737 Captain reported iPad Jeppesen FD Pro taxi chart defaults to small scale; difficult to read; moving map on landing resulting in a dangerous situation.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft was operated without legally required flight data recorder.
Flight Attendant commented that a particular aircraft cabin crew jump seat is positioned so as to restrict appropriate access to oxygen bottle.
Aircraft tug driver reported multiple loss of communication incidents since transitioning to wireless headsets for pilot/tug communications.
Captain reported not being able to find guidance information regarding a passenger's portable Heart Pump device batteries.
A Part 91 flight had difficulty with clear radio communications with ATC on an IFR flight plan.

Air carrier First Officer reported there was no food available at the hotel and food was not boarded for crew to consume during the flight. COVID-19 related time constraints were cited as contributing factors.
ZSU ARTCC Controller reported an unsafe situation relating to frequency blind spot.
On a CRJ-700; an incompatible Ground Proximity Warning System (EGPWS) was installed because the parts manual was not correct as to the compatibility of the EGPWS for this aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported a 30 minute delay in response from Dispatch regarding a deviation for weather.  Dispatcher cited reductions in staffing due to COVID-19 as contributing.
B737 Captain reported a ground conflict with a tug and baggage carts as they moved through the ramp area.
B777 First Officer reported a passenger's auxiliary lithium polymer battery began smoking in flight and was hot to the touch.
B737 flight crew reported being unable to depart prior to fatigue setting in due to an equipment problem at the destination airport.
Air carrier aircraft First Officer reported that while taxiing into position for takeoff; their wing was lifted by jet blast from a departing B777.

A large transport flight crew reported missing a speed restriction note on an arrival page in their iPads. They exceeded the published 280 knots published speed restriction. ATC asked another flight on the frequency if they saw the note and they replied that they did not see the note either. ATC stated that there was another flight that day that did not see the note.
GA Pilot reported his flight plan was not closed soon enough before search and rescue was initiated.

The reporter observed that there is no written standard for the FAA commonly used phrase; 'Report a 3 mile left/right base;' and that the phrase is sometimes misunderstood by pilots; resulting in a pattern that alters aircraft spacing; separation; and sequencing.
ZDC Center Controller reported working an overloaded sector with weather deviations due to lack of traffic flow restrictions was unsafe.

A large transport's EGPWS alerted PULL UP on the BZN ILS DME 12 at 11;000 feet. The First Officer took the appropriated action and the alert ceased at 14;000 feet. The terrain beneath the aircraft rose to about 9;400 feet which may have activated the terrain closure feature.

An Embraer regional jet Captain reported his aircraft's Pack 2 chronically malfunctioned. The MEL permitted pack off flight with a functioning recirculation fan; but after he discovered his aircraft's recirculation fan was inoperative; Maintenance was reluctant to address the situation.
CRJ 900 First Officer reported that a post flight inspection revealed a blown main gear tire and associated damage to the flaps and wing.

The Captain of a Bombardier CRJ-900 reported evidence of hydraulic fluid dripping from the left main landing gear door. Maintenance called it 'dirty water'; but it was verified that was indeed hydraulic fluid.
EMB-175 Captain reported that chart holders on the DV windows have been installed such that they impede the window from being opened.
A Pilot reported several instances of VFR Flight plans being dropped from the system when filed electronically in Alaska
S46 TRACON Controller reported having to vector an aircraft off of their final approach course to avoid an unidentified VFR target.
Air carrier Captain reported the Tower issued them a 'Standard Taxi Route' which was not available in their Jepp Charts or EFB.
Aircraft was assigned 270 knots at 13;000 feet and did not reduce to 250 knots below 10;000 feet.
ATC Controller reported fatigue and safety concerns due to 6 day a week shifts and short staffing related to the COVID-19 pandemic.
An Airline Captain reported that the aircraft was not configured and had to abort takeoff.
MD-11 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence that resulted in a 'rapid roll' from opposite direction B747 1;000 ft above their altitude of FL380.

Air Carrier Captain reported that his company airport page states that Taxiway D5 north of Taxiway C at CMH is restricted and it should read that the portion of Taxiway D5 north of Taxiway D is restricted.
A320 Pilot reported being told; post flight; that a passenger had boarded the flight with an oxygen tank.
Air carrier Captain reported that continual; significant changes in crew schedules over the past months are causing cumulative fatigue.

B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX in trail of a B777 that resulted in multiple 30 to 45 degree rolls in both directions. A Flight Attendant sustained minor injuries in the encounter.
Captain reported that a ferry flight  was missing the Ferry Permit.
MSY TRACON Controller working combined sectors during heavy traffic with weather deviations allowed an aircraft to fly less than the mandatory 3 miles from an obstruction displayed on their radar.
Tower Controller reported similar sounding call signs from a local flight school routinely cause confusion and increase workload.
FAR TRACON facility scheduled a radar outage. The Controllers were not advised or briefed about procedures to implement during the outage.
C180 pilot reported loss of directional control on landing rollout when he encountered wake turbulence and rotor blast from a helicopter landing nearby.
A Glider pilot reported a NMAC with a fixed wing aircraft while in a published glider flying area that is close to a busy airport on one side and a mountain range on the other.
Flight Attendant reported the disinfecting spray used for sanitizing aircraft is irritating to eyes and nasal passages.

B737 Captain reported difficulty and stress in working with Guatemalan controllers. He was given a revised; unacceptable takeoff clearance by MGGT Tower and did not receive the winds for takeoff. Captain also stated the RNAV (RNP) Runway 20 Approach is allegedly flawed in its design. It consistently positions the aircraft low when on the glidepath at the decision altitude.
The crew encountered a fireworks display on approach to KLAN 28L.
Maintenance Technician reported confidentiality breaches by Company Management.
A Center Controller reported their sectors are being overloaded with complex traffic and Traffic Management Units are not doing anything to affect the flow.
Corporate turbojet Captain reported issues with Approach Control while landing in marginal VFR conditions.
Helicopter pilot reported concerns with 1;000 foot towers that have had inoperative lighting for extended periods of time.  The tower and guy wires are reportedly difficult to see at night.
Aircraft tug driver reported difficulty with the new wireless headsets used for communicating with flight crew during pushback.
Air Carrier Captain reported taking evasive action responding to an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft on their approach course to PAE airport.
B737 First Officer reported that MDW airport taxiways are not correctly charted during construction.
An aircraft flew into the BJC Class D airspace without an ATC clearance. The aircraft was confused by the VFR flyway planning chart and thought they were outside of the Class D.

NCT Controller reported of having an aircraft inbound to a destination and descending it to an incorrect altitude. Center called and reminded the Controller of the MVA. Reporter tried calling aircraft three times to give it a correct altitude. Aircraft went back to centers frequency because reporter's frequency was coming in broken.
A B737 Captain reported his Dispatcher would not return a requested call about winds inflight because their carrier's dispatchers are overwhelmed and understaffed.
B747 Flight Crew reported the Flight Management System directed an incorrect departure procedure.
A Boston TRACON (A90) Controller reports of having difficulty with an aircraft transponder. The Controller stated the data tag would go into a coast mode and he had to tag up the aircraft often.
Two air carrier First Officers reported during climb from SBGR airport taking evasive action to avoid a balloon.

After landing on and clearing EWR Runway 29; a flight crew was cleared across the open ramp to Taxiway J. The Captain was confused because the commercial EFB airport depiction did not look like the airport's massive open ramps and indistinguishable taxiways.
BE33 pilot reported battery failure in iPad and landed at the wrong airport.
Ramp procedures were not followed properly at EWR; and ramp personnel wandered close to a running engine intake while parking which forced the crew to shutdown the engine prematurely.
A319 flight crew reported that when flying the noise abatement departure from LGA Runway 13 the track appears to diverge from the TNNS6 departure.
A TRACON Controller reported an unidentified VFR aircraft climbed to an altitude below Class C airspace; but was still in confliction with another aircraft on an approach to the Class C airport.
CE-525 pilot reported impact with bird on takeoff roll and rejecting takeoff.
First Officer reported maneuvering for a TCAS RA; while being vectored for an ILS to EWR; that resulted in a second RA with another aircraft.

Tower Controller reported the Full Route Clearance (FRC) indicator printed in the remarks section was located where it may not be visible to the Controller resulting in an aircraft not receiving the correct departure clearance.

An A-319 crew inadvertently struck an air conditioning cart with their number one engine while taxiing in to their gate. Both piloted report that there was no left side wing walker present. The Captain stated that the lead marshaller continued to direct him forward; but that he stopped when he noticed the concerned expression and gestures of another company employee who was observing the parking operation.
SCT TRACON Controller reported observing a false radar target on a conflicting heading with traffic they were vectoring for an approach.
B777 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence during cruise flight.
Gulfstream IV flight crew reported a wasp was discovered in the cockpit after takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported having to use bedding and pillows during crew rest that were previously used.

A single piloted aircraft on takeoff saw another aircraft further down the runway. The pilot rotated early; nearly missing the other aircraft. The reporter mentioned that there were 'many foreign students in training...' and that that fact contributed to his not detecting the traffic conflict before takeoff roll because the other aircraft did not respond to his departure announcement.
Flight Attendant reported moving to an unassigned jumpseat for social distancing purposes. The move conflicted with FAR regulations.

A321 flight crew received a TPS for departure from Runway 19L at SFO indicated flaps 1 was required. The normal and engine out procedures for this runway both required a 30 degree banked turn immediately after takeoff which required a flap setting of 2/3.
N90 TRACON Controller and ZBW ARTCC Controller reported disagreeing on the interpretation of the facility directives regarding handoffs from the Center to Approach Control.
Flight Attendant reported being asked to continue flying after being notified of being in contact with a crew member who had tested positive for COVID-19.
A passenger reported being initially startled by a Flight Attendant PA banter on landing until he realized the theatrics were not aircraft related noise.

A Controller radar identified a departure and noticed they had converging VFR traffic. The IFR departure was below the Minimum IFR Altitude so the Controller could not vector the aircraft to avoid the traffic.
Pilot reported the Tower closed due to COVID-19 staffing; and was not sure as to what they should be squawking.
PAO Tower Controller reported a NMAC between an aircraft in their pattern and an aircraft deviating from the adjacent airports final approach course.
B767 First Officer reported that they received information of a possible Flight Information Region closure while in cruise.
C172 pilot reported his aircraft became airborne when it entered an area of jet blast; rose about five feet in the air; then nosed over and incurred a prop strike.

The Flight Data Processor (FDP) was out of service at this facility. The back-up processor had already been out of service for about two weeks. FDP is how the facility receives; updates and processes all the flight plans automatically. When this is out of service all data must be accomplished manually.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported Operations wouldn't stop boarding for medical emergency.
Maintenance Technician found smoke detectors in cargo bays covered with oily substance.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC descended them below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
EC135 pilot reported that FOD became caught in rotor blades during landing.
B767 Captain reported that the O2 masks were not compatible with the smoke goggles and did not provide a good seal.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation.  Controller reported FUSION Radar contributed to the inability to catch the error sooner.
ATC Center Controller reported supervisory personnel ordered the controllers to split a sector in the middle of a busy traffic period.
Observer reported filing report regarding paperwork requirements for another Mechanic that received a threat from the Supervisor.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that customer service representatives are staging wheel chair passengers in the jetway at some stations in order to speed up boarding once the flight attendants are ready. He believes this is unsafe and a violation of company policy.
Maintenance Technician reports that the EROS Flight Crew Oxygen Mask has a defective supply line.
Embraer Regional Jet Flight Crew reported that the aircraft did not turn toward the correct fix on departure.
P80 TRACON Controller cancelled an arrival's STAR and vectored the aircraft to avoid an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.
B757 First Officer reported that an anti-skid MEL issue was not able to be included in a takeoff performance data calculation which caused the crew to takeoff with incorrect data.
B737 Captain reported ramp personnel opened the cargo door with the left engine still running.
ZAU Controller stated that having two people scheduled for the midnight shift is insufficient for staffing.
HUF Local Controller reported of staffing issues that were blamed for the operational error of an aircraft turning base before it was supposed to.
A VFR aircraft departing VNY was issued instructions which confused the pilot. ATC restricted their climb and in order to avoid terrain they entered BUR airport's Class C airspace without approval.
Maintenance Personnel reported that incoming Boeing 737 slides have a different style lanyard and battery pouch than on previous slides. Maintenance was not notified of part number change.
First Officer reported an airborne conflict with what they perceived to be a weather balloon half a mile off right wing.
PA-28 pilot reported an airspace violation due to the inability to communicate simultaneously with destination CTAF and adjoining Class D airspace during initial approach.

A pilot reported a new 'no taxi' area just beyond the new IWA Taxiway E and where it turns onto Taxiway B. IWA NOTAM 07/040 dated 1507282315 permanently changed the ramp Taxiway to E; but airport diagrams do not reflect changes.
Technician reported that during the clearance of an MEL; he did not reinstall the light bulbs for the LE SLATS IN TRANSIT light on the overhead panel.
B767 Captain reported main landing gear extended indicator not functioning correctly.

On visual approach to a foreign airport with the First Officer flying; the aircraft was vectored in high and close such that the stabilized approach criteria was not met at 500 FT AGL. The Captain took the aircraft and elected to land.
Y90 Controller reports of an known automation problem within Y90 and N90. While training the Controller noticed an airspace deviation related to this automation problem.

Air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC with VFR traffic while on the ILS 13R approach to BFI.  The flight crew received an RA/TA; but did not take evasive action because they had the VFR traffic in sight.
DHC-8-100 flight crew discovered incompatibility with ATOG procedures off gravel surfaced runways.
Airbus A300 had a fuel center tank pump MEL deferral; but ramp personnel and Dispatch had difficulty getting the weight and balance computer to accept the MEL fuel correctly.
Small aircraft student pilot reported an NMAC in the vicinity of IWA airport.
On a visual approach to IWA 30L; a crew responded to an RA with both a commanded climb and descent which was caused by a nearby aerobatic aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported not having proper charts for navigation.
EMB-175 Captain reported missing several checklist items because of confusion caused by the interface of de-ice and normal checklists.
A Tracon Controller observed an aircraft descend below its assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

An Air Carrier pilot reported the tower controller was overloaded with traffic while working Local and Ground Control combined when they were cleared for takeoff even though they were number two in the departure lineup. The pilot stated this airport needs to be staffed with more controllers.;
Airline ground personnel reported a 'new bag needs to be added' to aircraft equipment for Lithium Ion batteries handling.

Maintenance reported that work was accomplished on a passenger seat on a B787 by a non-certified engineer.

A reporter states that a VFR aircraft; after being terminated for flight following; enters a restricted area. A call in the blind is answered by the VFR pilot and is told that they entered the restricted area. The pilot disagrees and says the altitude that is not is higher than they are. The Controller disagrees and advises the pilot. The pilot finds out that the depiction on his GPS system is incorrect.
AGC Tower controllers and pilot reported an aircraft entered an active runway at the same time as another aircraft was beginning its takeoff roll.

Las Vegas TRACON Controller reported an uncomfortable situation between a VFR and IFR aircraft. Controller reported that staffing was a problem along with the combined sectors.

Ramp Agent reported an electric pushback tractor's brakes failed as he approached the towbar. The tractor ran over the towbar; but the driver steered away from the aircraft then stopped by pulling the lever into park.

Maintenance Technician reported that station technicians modified company oxygen service carts without documentation and used unauthorized and uncleaned components for modification.

B737 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after a bird flock encounter in initial climb caused extensive damage to the right engine.
CE560XLS First Officer reported an exterior panel missing post flight.

Pilot reported encounter with heavy smoke from wildfires; resulting in descent to altitude necessitating terrain avoidance.
