B757 First Officer (FO) reports hitting a flock of geese during climbout. The engines appear unscathed; but a return to the departure airport occured. The Captain and the FO both attempt to access their iPad's for overweight landing information to find both apps frozen.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported difficulty maintaining contact with an aircraft he was working that was experiencing multiple electrical issues related to a static discharge.
Embraer Captain reported encountering wake turbulence descending out of FL320 inbound into LGA in trail of an A320.
B737-700 Captain reported a NMAC with a UAS while on approach to SNA.

Dispatcher reported concerns with current staffing levels; especially when workload is increased due to weather diverts; last minute ATC closures for sanitizing and  international plus domestic re-routes.

The crew departed from GRR while a mandatory deicing procedure was reportedly in effect; though the aircraft was clean and there was no precipitation.

Captain reported that the Jeppesen FliteDeck Pro application obfuscates the airport operation hours are not clear and fail to reference what chart holds specific airport diagrams.

The Controller had aircraft going into the airport to a closed runway thinking it was open. The Controller checked with the Airport Manager who said it was open. An aircraft had to make a missed approach due to men and equipment on the runway.

ERJ First Officer reported receiving a Ground Proximity Warning while conducting a visual approach with the terrain in sight.
The flight attendants were legal to operate the late assignment but felt that they were severely fatigued; which could have impacted their effectiveness if an emergency situation arose.

BOI Approach Controller reported they had to vector an aircraft off their published approach course due to converging unidentified VFR traffic.
During routine flight over Lake Barryessa; a pilot reported observing a paraglider in excess of 3;000 feet while under tow from watercraft causing pilot to take evasive action.

Flight crew missed the mandatory crossing altitude NOTAM change from 7;000 to 8;000 feet at an initial approach fix (ARCHI) for the RNAV (GPS) Runway 28L and RNAV (GPS) Z Runway 28R approaches to SFO.

Technician reported that unqualified technicians are performing maintenance tasks in violation of the company GMM.

Air Carrier Captain reported reported 'Indecipherable' NOTAMs regarding displaced threshold at destination airport.

A Maintenance Technician reported that improper circuit breakers collars were being used to deactivate systems.
A Pilot reported their inefficient procedures for handling an inflight medical emergency distracted the pilots from flying the aircraft.
Air carrier Ramper reported being pressured to achieve an on-time departure by loading kennels without confirming adequate live animal airspace.
B737 flight crew on a vector to intercept the Final Approach Course reported responding to an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft right after being switched to Tower frequency.
Maintenance employee reported that social distancing on the bus is not possible due to lack of spacing.

Local Controller working Ground combined reported that hour breaks are common at this facility and safety is being jeopardized. Controller was too busy at times and because of this aircraft were delayed.  Controller recommended keeping ground control open during a specific time.
Captain reported communication breakdown with Ground Control and changes to taxiways that were not on available charts or NOTAMs.
An A321 crew was forced by the company's inaction to complete a flight in violation of the FAR 117 thirteen hour flight duty period limitation.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence 4 miles in trail of a B757 on approach to ATL.
Air carrier Captain reported he was unable to update his iPad prior to departure from TIST.
B737 Captain reported ramp agent approached aircraft at remote parking spot during lightning storm to open door and triggered ACARS.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an EGPWS alert while on initial approach into an airport surrounded by high terrain.

F11 Controller reports of an automation problem with aircraft tagging up on the wrong scopes; even after clearance delivery modified both aircraft to the correct tab list.
A B767 Captain reported he had two newly qualified First Officers on his overwater international flight and feels this is not a safe procedure.

ZTL Controller received an aircraft whose routing in the ATC computer showed IVANE STAR. The previous facility made a computer entry to suppress the IVANE route from the flight plan; but this update did not get forwarded to the ZTL Controller.
CL-605 Captain reported a 'severe' wake turbulence encounter at FL310 in trail of a B777 on arrival into LAX.
Technicians reported incorrect bolts used on device to lift engine compressor disc resulted in damage to blades.
ATC Controller reported allowing an aircraft to remain on the previous (combined) frequency; then lost communications as the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A B757 air crew performed a rejected takeoff after multiple bird strikes at around 100 knots. They returned to the gate. After maintenance inspected the aircraft they once again departed for their destination uneventfully.

TBM-900 pilot reported he flew into RVSM Airspace without the required Letter of Authorization from the FAA.

B737 Captain reported software issues with company issued EFB could potentially result in a safety risk.

Air carrier First Officer reported the company training center and simulators could benefit from more sanitation and cleaning supplies for staff and trainees in order to support a safe training environment. Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that an aircraft diverted due to the crosswind limitations listed in the Flight Crew Manual not being in agreement with the limitations available to dispatchers.

Captain reported due to ATC workload and failed communications; Captain entered TFR.

ZTL Controller reports of a handoff issues with ZDC.  They couldn't handoff a specific aircraft because there was another aircraft with the same call sign that ZDC was attempting to handoff to ZTL.
S46 TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft caused an airborne conflict north of the BFI area. This is a recurring issue.

Air carrier Captain reported that several intoxicated passengers were being disruptive and not complying with face mask policy. The Captain stated that while one passenger was being removed from the aircraft; he became angry at the Captain and used profanity.
The 737 crew violated FAR 117 due to a divertion followed by a taxi/takeoff delay.

Captain reported their Disabled Checklist for loss of Center (C) Hydraulic System quantity and pressure directed the pilot to select an Air Driven (Demand) Pump (ADP) on B777 aircraft. The Electronic Checklist logic stopped the continuation of the Disabled checklist due to a momentary spike in the 'C' System pressure. As a result; the Center System hyd pumps were left on even with the Center Hyd quantity and pressure at zero.
ZAB Controller reported a military aircraft entering a higher MVA due to known area of communications limitations.
PDX Local Controller reported an air carrier on short final reporting close proximity to aircraft at nearby VUO.

An ERJ-175 Captain questioned operations before takeoff about the weight and balance accuracy and was given a revision. Inflight Dispatch advised the crew of an error and later diverted them because the load was off at least 1;800 lbs. The cargo compartment weight limitations were exceeded.

B737 Captain reported the loss of a reliable GPS signal which he attributed to military activity in the area. ATC provided headings until GPS guidance was reestablished.
B767 flight crew reported rejecting the takeoff following a bird strike in the right engine that resulted in a compressor stall.
Air Carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation.
Maintenance personnel could not complete an 'A' check on an MD80 in the time allotted. Staffing issued were cited as contributing factors.
A Center Controller reported an unknown aircraft descended into the sector with less than the required 5 miles lateral separation.
Air carrier Captain reported illness when company technician failed to follow safety precautions after fumigation using highly toxic substances.
Air carrier Captain reported no sanitation equipment was available for use while at a foreign destination during the COVID-19 pandemic.
During flight in a B737-700 the crew climbed to FL260 when they were restricted to FL250 due to a single pack operation.
Flight Attendant reported a hand sanitizing dispenser is necessary when the temperature check system with a touch pad is used.

Air carrier flight crew while descending through 31;000 feet responded to a 'DESCEND' RA. A Gulfstream above them was descending because of rapid decompression. They responded to the RA and were then 'clear of conflict.' 'Training and the system worked perfectly.'

A80 controllers report of using fusion radar on the final sectors.  Controller turns an aircraft into a hole; the aircraft did not turn as the controller thought it would; then jumped showing it on a different then assigned heading.;  The controller had visual separation so all was good separation wise; but didn't like the way the aircraft jumped.  Controller exclaimed and another controller also saw what happened.  Controller thought the jumping of radar targets on final is unacceptable.

A320 Captain reported problems with the Accupark system at the gate.
Glider pilot reported encroaching on the adjacent Wave Window airspace due to the failure of onboard navigation equipment.

Initially no anomalies are detected but then the flight attendants report a strange sound and a strong vibration coming from the left engine. The crew also feels the vibration and elects to return to the departure airport.

A Maintenance Controller reports how their understaffed Airbus desk and pressure by Operations to move aircraft 'On Time'; contributed to deferring and collaring a popped Brake Fan Circuit Breaker (C/B) for a Left Main Gear harness that had previously arced; left burn marks and a Tachometer-1 fault. Airbus A320 diverted after takeoff due to an Autobrake Fault.
B737 First Officer reported the SJC ALMDN3 RNAV SID listed two top altitudes with the first at 15;000 feet and the second at FL190. ATC was uncertain about which was correct.

BE300 Captain states that his company has not been operating in compliance with FAR 135.157 due to only 9 passenger oxygen masks being available while carrying 9 passengers plus one lap child. Captain reported that the load planning assumptions in the weight and balance computer and the station lading procedures results in nose-heavy flight characteristics on takeoff and recommends a review of those assumptions and procedures.
B737 First Officer reported a 'strong jolt' of wake turbulence at FL340 when they passed another aircraft going in the opposite direction at FL350.
Air carrier Captain reported having to divert due to ATC COVID-19 issues at landing Center facility.
ZMP Center Controller reported the adjacent sector handed them off two aircraft at the same altitude on converging courses.
Air Carrier Captain reported chart instructions do not agree with ATC ground instructions for push-back.
A AFW Tower Controller reported an arriving aircraft on an instrument approach had a NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft departing a satellite non towered airport.
CE-560XL First Officer reported several instances of pressure from his company to ignore maintenance items during preflight; including corrosion issues.
PC-12 Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAV on initial approach to BED.
E145 Captain reported a GPWS warning on approach with the terrain in sight.

Air carrier Captain reported losing both GPS signals; which he attributed to military activity in the area. He successfully switched to an alternative means of navigation until the GPS signal was restored.
A Maintenance Technician reported that an aircraft departed with an entry door handle out of alignment.
A Center Oceanic Controller reported proper procedures and equipment requirements were not implemented during a planned radar and radio outage period.
A321 Captain reported a flap overspeed resulted from a wake vortex encounter in climbout from PHL behind an MD-11.
EMB-135 Captain reported returning to departure airport following fuel quantity display anomalies due to fuel contamination.

An A320 First Officer reported that multiple traffic targets in the vicinity of Whiteman (WHP) caused TCAS alerts and necessitated maneuvering that prevented the A320 from achieving a stabilized approach by 1;000 feet AGL on the Visual Approach to Runway 15 at BUR. A go-around was performed and the aircraft subsequently landed uneventfully at BUR on runway 08.
B737 Captain reported discrepancies on the application of an MEL procedure.
A319 Captain reported deviating from assigned track as a result of communication difficulties related to AUS ATCT degraded capabilities.

JCF Controller (Edwards AFB Approach) reports that the facility experienced ATC Zero due to equipment issue. No losses of separation were noted and the airspace was given away. Reporter stated the facility needs to come up with a contingency plan for this problem.
CE680 Captain reported requested an inoperative TCAS system be repaired prior to returning to revenue service.

An MD-80 pilot reported that following a logbook write up for a fuel temperature control problem; Maintenance personnel deferred the indication. Communication between the pilots prevented the flight from departing.
Boeing 737 flight crew reported being unable to locate the takeoff warning test switch.
Wide body air carrier Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after ATC vectors and deteriorating weather resulted in burning more fuel than planned.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL310 7 miles in trail of a B777 on arrival into EWR.

An MD-82 flight crew; exiting the runway on a highspeed taxiway encountered a regional jet established on the parallel taxiway. ATC did not inform them and the bright lights of the construction area and terminal area obscured the RJ until the MD-82's landing lights illuminated it. The Captain applied heavy breaking and avoided colliding with the RJ.
B737 Captain reported that Flight Attendant Audit Inspection disrupted and delayed crew briefing; preflight responsibilities; and boarding.
First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence when ATC vectored them over a powerplant that was emitting vapor plumes.
Air Carrier First Officer reported receiving an EGPWS caution for uncharted towers in the vicinity of SBN airport.
B777 Captain reported that while following a parking guidance system they still overran the stop line by 3 feet.

A controller reported discovering an unprotected flight due to the entry of an incorrect fix estimate time for a flight plan through non-radar airspace causing the flight plan to be removed from the system.
A320 flight crew reported receiving an 'ENG 2 AIR SYS FAULT' on ECAM during descent. The published procedure did not provide sufficient information; so the crew landed with the engine at idle thrust.
B737 Captain reported there were three company aircraft on the same frequency with similar call signs; which caused problems for both the flights and ATC.
DH8-400 Captain reported confusion regarding logbook maintenance entries and MEL procedures caused aircraft to be flown with an open item in the logbook.
B737-700 flight crew reported several aircraft system malfunctions due to multiple bird strike on approach.
A flight Crew of a B737-700 reported that on initial approach they were cleared to follow traffic for a visual approach to the runway. Due to their speed they were unable to maintain spacing.
Technician reported disagreement over policy and procedures not complied with during a road trip to remove and replace a main entry door slide.
Ground employee reported problems associated with sharing headsets.

During a repositioning flight; the flight crew was asked to an Operational Check Flight (OCF) and deploy the Air Driven Generator (ADG). There was some confusion weather the flight was dispatched properly.
Cessna 182 pilot reported the rudder pedal separated from the rudder control post during taxi.
Technician reported installing the incorrect part on aircraft due to misreading the manual.
Ramp agent reported that an extremely loud warning signal in the tug being used for a push made it diffucult to hear Ground Control's instructions.
A320 Captain reported his schedules often do not offer an opportunity to eat prior to departure.
ZNY Center Controller reported they were not advised their sector was decombined and the ATOP Sector was being worked by a different Controller.
Employee at air carrier reports management intimidation; falsification of reports and unethical maintenance practices.
Instructor pilot reported confusion regarding a new note the FAA has attached to the RNAV approach at POF.
HUF Local Controller reported an unsafe situation due to the D-Brite being expanded to 60 miles for use by the Radar Controller.

A Montgomery (MGM) Approach Controller describes advising an aircraft that the runway direction is changing; and an arresting cable needs to be lowered. The pilot elects to land over the cable after being given a few options by the Controller. The aircraft is switched to Tower and lands impacting the cable and damaging the aircraft.
Airbus pilot reported on challenges regarding the company's new checklists.
Air carrier Captain reported no published procedure for pushback from gate.
Air Carrier Captain reported concerns regarding company COVID-19 related plans pertaining to sick leave; vaccinations; training schedules; etc.
SR20 pilot reported an airborne conflict with another aircraft requiring evasive action. The traffic was detected by an onboard TCAS system and also reported by ATC.
ZAN Controller described problems associated with non-radar and radar aircraft; and separation requirements. Controller was unsure of what standard applied to the aircraft involved.
CMH TRACON Controller reported their NIDS (NAS Information Display System) routinely fails to depict current adverse weather conditions.
Embraer 175 Captain reported returning to the gate after receiving 'ENG REF ECS DISAG' caution message when preparing for takeoff.
AVP TRACON Controller reported an IFR aircraft with avionics trouble descending below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 flight crew reported an engine failure on takeoff due to a bird strike. After the crew accomplished checklist items; a successful single engine approach and landing was made.
Crew reported that Dispatch was not notified of an aircraft delay.
B737-700 flight crew reported rejecting takeoff due to bird strike.
The Captain of a Bombardier Dash 8 reported that when the aircraft was being dei-ced; he did not have any communication with the de-ice truck.
Air Traffic Controller and trainee reported a loss of separation between aircraft deviating for weather.

An aircraft departing TRACON airspace climbed through the overlying ARTCC sector without coordination. The automated handoff was in effect with the next ARTCC sector along the aircraft's route of flight.

A Corporate Jet flight crew filed to JFK was cleared direct the ROBER waypoint; but heard RUBER resulting in a track deviation. ATC informed them about their error and reissued the correct waypoint. RUBER and ROBER are about 50 nm apart in the New York area.

C172 instructor pilot with his student; performing a simulated forced landing south of BUY; reporte detecting three towers at N35 56 49.5 W79 26 09.8 near the location of twin towers noted on the sectional at 1845 MSL. The third tower appeared taller than the other two and was unlit.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger boarded the aircraft with a mask that had holes.
CRJ200 flight crew reported being informed by ATC that they were off course. They informed the crew that there was reported GPS signal jamming in the area.
Air carrier Captain reported a Control Tower that was ATC Zero; leading to a disruption in communications.

D10 Controller reports of a loss of separation between two aircraft. Controller did not turn aircraft soon enough to avoid other traffic and reported he was unsure of what airspace he had due to an airport configuration that he was not too sure of.
A Captain; preparing for an Advanced Qualification Program (AQP) oral; reports on the many shortcomings of the IPad as a replacement for traditional aircraft flight manuals.

GA pilot reported being stung by an unseen; unidentified insect while fueling his aircraft at POC. Light-headedness and loss of unconsciousness occurred shortly thereafter and required hospitalization. He recommended at least a 30 minute delay for pilots who may be stung by an insect prior to any departure.

Flight attendant reported that there are two types of CPR devices available on board; however flight attendants are qualified to use only one; which requires removal of the personal protective mask in order to use. Reporter requests training on the other device to keep everyone safe in a COVID-19 environment.
Technician reported installing wrong effectivity part during MEL procedures for inoperative Number 1 VHF Comm.

Controller reports facility staffing issues and working combined positions contributed to a probable loss of separation/runway incursion situation that was prevented by timely questioning of clearance by a locally familiar pilot.
B737 Captain reported his aircraft rolled right and descended after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B777 at FL340.
Skydiving pilot and TRACON Controller reported an aircraft was permitted to fly through the skydiving operation active jump zone.
Flight Attendant reported ill passengers on the flight and was concerned regarding lack of COVID-19 testing for the crew.
IAD Tower Local Controller reported they had to send an aircraft on final approach around because their Supervisor approved Maintenance Personnel to work close to the runway without a radio.
Air carrier flight crew reported being pushed back with only 1 wing walker which nearly led to a collision with another aircraft.

Center Controller reported a possible loss of separation and cited COVID-19 related staffing issues as a contributing factor.
A330-300 First Officer reported airspeed fluctuation on approach probably due to wake vortex encounter caused by a heavy aircraft ahead.
Air carrier Captain reported being scheduled a flight at full passenger capacity and was concerned about social distancing and exposure to COVID-19.
A Controller reported they vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft reported a Wake Vortex encounter and diverted due to injuries.

E175 First Officer reported pressure from maintenance to not write up an engine problem.

TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict.
Dispatcher stated that there were insufficient dispatchers to handle the workload.
A Tower Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff. The Controller did not notice an aircraft was in the path of the departure due to their view being blocked by Tower Display equipment.
BFI Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict that may have been prevented if Class D was larger or TRACON had been more proactive.
First Officer reported NOTAMS at destination are ineffective in providing a useful taxi tool to navigate a huge airport construction area.

Air carrier Captain reported that many of the first officers he flies with are not current; especially in the landing environment.
Flight Attendant reported being notified by company 10 days after another Flight Attendant tested positive for COVID-19.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach leading to a go-around; which then resulted in an altitude deviation. The Captain cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
B737 First Officer reported that the crew oxygen mask was not the right type.

Air carrier flight crew reported an aircraft swap caused a departure delay for an international flight. Some flight attendants go illegal and were taken off the flight as reserves were summoned. B737 flight crew reported a bird strike below 1;000 feet on departure which caused a momentary compressor stall before resuming normal operation. Flight returns to the departure airport for an overweight landing.
Air carrier Captain reported communication issues with Center and attributed it to COVID-19 ATC staffing of facilities.
Air carrier Captain reported aircraft had inoperative cabin cooling and was re-assigned to another flight crew.

Ground employee reported the break-room is not properly being cleaned.
Flight Attendant reported a blocked jumpseat was subsequently used possibly violating FAR rules.

Scientist/Observer reported that during a smoke in the cabin event; only one oxygen bottle with two masks was onboard with three crewmen; resulting in a crewmember becoming incapacitated; requiring medical treatment.

S46 TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation due to confusing coordination and complex airspace between adjacent airports.

B757 Captain lamented the lack of any indication on arrival charts of where an aircraft landing at a secondary airport will pass below or arrive below the Class B veil; requiring a 200 knot speed restriction.

Air carrier Captain reported experiencing jamming of GPS signal at FL340 while flying in Iraqi airspace.
ZMP Controller reported descending an aircraft. A different aircraft with a similar call sign of the same company descended.
Flight Attendant complains of a headache and is informed by another Flight Attendant that the pilots had been instructed by the company to provide less oxygen to the cabin.

B737 Flight Attendant reported passengers were evacuated to the terminal via aircraft entry doors after a start cart caught fire on the ramp next to them.

Air Carrier Dispatcher described a confusing situation that developed when his dispatching computer automatically assigned a 'D' suffix to the flight number of a stubbed flight taking the place of a late inbound flight with the same flight number. ZMA Controller reported TMU and facility management forced controllers to work aircraft through weather.
GA pilot reported communication issues with ATC while attempting to receive a Class Bravo clearance.

B737 flight crew reported they were informed of possible damage near the top of the vertical stabilizer. After maintenance examination; the aircraft was taken out of service.

B767 Captain reported they diverted because the filed destination airport had been NOTAMed closed during the scheduled arrival period; but neither the Captain nor Dispatch caught the closure notice.
B767 pilot reported a company practice of issuing similar sounding callsigns that resulted in ATC confusion.
A Fractional jet pilot reported cancelling their trip due to fatigue due to the scheduling practices of their company.

A general aviation instructor pilot reported noticing that the CJR RNAV 22 approach could; if flown as depicted; possibly intrude on the IAD Class B airspace. Reporter recommended an adjustment to the procedure turn to preclude a possible airspace violation.

Air carrier Captain reported a problem obtaining critical flight information from his Microsoft Surface EFB in a timely fashion. He stated that the problem is chronic and is directly related to the brand of electronic device chosen by his company.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft checked in on the frequency that had not been handed off by Center due to computer issues.
F11 TRACON Controller reported working a busy combined sector that failed to notice an overtake between two aircraft resulting in a loss of separation.

Helicopter Pilot reported misgivings due to suspected unapproved procedures used to return an aircraft to service.

Technician reported that a lack of qualified personnel is causing delays and excessive workload.
Captain reported navigational course error due to published Navblue charts depicting an erroneous course westbound from ELX on V-100 airway.
Air carrier flight crew reported being targeted by a green laser.

Air carrier flight crew reported a concern that fuel reserves required to fly to SKBO are less than required to fly to a domestic destination in the system; which makes no sense considering terrain; limited alternates and language difficulties.

A Center Controller reported of a radar display indicating an aircraft had the correct routing but the information on their EDST (Enroute Decision Support Tool) did not default to the proper routing display for the type aircraft. B737-300 First Officer expressed concerns with TRACON's excessive clearance changes during arrival to SFO airport.
Tower Controller working combined positions described safety of flight concerns due to added workload issues.
B737 pilot reported the JeppFD-Pro display blocks from view the altitudes required by some SIDs when the display is rotated.

Maintenance Technician reported wording in maintenance instructions needs to be changed to accommodate for COVID-19 policy.
Air carrier Captain reported the NOTAM format needs many improvements for safety reasons.
Pilot reported a misapplied MEL was used and the aircraft flew in revenue service.
Air carrier Captain reported they routinely encounter aircraft with similar sounding call signs.

A solo pilot made his CTAF communications before departing SGU then after takeoff noticed a large aircraft taxiing off the far end of the runway. He had not heard a response from that aircraft which apparently did not hear him either.
B747 First Officer reported finding improperly loaded hazmat during their pre-flight check.

On taxi out; A321 crew received 'RIGHT WING OVERFLOW' ECAM message. After completing the QRH procedure and visually confirming the venting fuel; crew shut down both engines to stop the fuel venting and the aircraft was towed to the gate. Cause of fuel venting was not reported. MEL for fuel pump or valve had been cleared before the flight.
Corporate Captain reported experiencing an undershoot of a crossing restriction and cited lack of recent flying experience due to the COVID-19 pandemic as a contributing factor.

The Center Controller in Charge (CIC) noticed radar contact was lost with an aircraft that should have been within radar coverage. The CIC attempted to call the Radar out of service but was told not to.
B737 Captain reported tugs that are outfitted with LED headlights blind the flight crew resulting in difficulty seeing the safety zone and/or the Marshaller.
B737-800 First Officer reported that departing out of BWI; the aircraft is unable to make the 17000ft. restriction at FOXHL on TERPZ 6 departure.

A Dallas Fort Worth (D10) Controller reports of an aircraft being cleared for an approach that they later find out was out of service. The facility was not aware that the approach was not working correctly.
B737-800 flight crew reported that during approach a laser light beam entered the cockpit.
Hayward Tower Controller reported losing radios and having to use one back up radio between all the positions in the Tower.
Air Carrier Captain reported the requirement of wearing a face mask with a Flight Attendant on the Flight Deck is a safety concern; especially if there is an event such as a rapid decompression.
B767 Captain reported two block turn backs caused by deferred maintenance; resulting in a flight delay.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a sick passenger boarded a flight; but was removed from the flight prior to departure.

A B777 crew; pilots and flight attendants; describe the confusion after discovering the flight attendant crew is understaffed in flight. The events surrounding the procedural and human errors are discussed.

B777 flight crew reported a greater than planned fuel burn on an international flight resulting in a diversion. Further investigation revealed the drag factor in the flight planning software was incorrect.
Air carrier Relief Pilot reported removing himself from a flight due to a disagreement with the Captain about wearing masks in the flight deck.
A Captain reported confusion with the SJC BMRNG1 procedure chart.
B757 Captain reported on three separate occurrences; the EFB app deleted its content while the aircraft was in flight; depriving the flight crew of all charts.
EMB-175 flight crew airspace reported taking evasive action and receiving an RA for an unidentified VFR target operating just below the base of the Class B airspace.
CRJ-200 Captain reported concern over the level of training at his company.

A321 Captain reported flap problems on approach that required checklists that were; in the Captain's words; 'way too long and are inefficient and largely unusable.' They were unable to complete them because of their fuel situation.
TRACON Controller reported that their frequency stopped working for several minutes; resulting in a NMAC.
A TRACON Controller reported that the Center was handing off aircraft recovering from a military exercise with less than the prescribed spacing specified in the Letter of Agreement.
The pilot received a TA and leveled off with military aircraft in sight after departing DTS with a clearance from Elgin.

S46 TRACON Controller reported of a conflict between and IFR arrival and a VFR aircraft. Controller reported this is an ongoing problem with VFR aircraft using a corridor that conflicts with IFR aircraft arrivals at the same altitudes.
ERJ-175 Captain reported a Flight Attendant was slightly injured during a wake turbulence encounter on approach to LAX.
Air carrier Captain reported a GPWS alarm while flying into the Burbank airport.

A Flight Attendant reported that ramp personnel were not accepting oversized bags during boarding. When confronted with policy and FAR violations; the lack of ramp management oversight and lack of ramp employee concern for their actions' consequences was cause for concern.
SLC Tower Controller reported runway and taxiway closures increased their workload causing them to have to issue an arriving aircraft go-around instructions.
Dispatcher reported a flight departed early into a Flow Constrained Area (FCA) and questioned company procedures that allowed this to occur.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a Helicopter descended below the MVA while issuing a wildfire report.
Air carrier Captain reported on descent into FSD the ZMP frequency 128.0 had a loud side-tone that was almost deafening and made communication with the Center difficult.
A319 First Officer reported instructing Pilot not Flying on the procedure to activate runway lights.

B737 First Officer discussed the complexity some aeronautical charts present when pilots are required to mentally calculate the required climb gradient in feet per mile on SIDs. R90 Approach Controller reported frequency outages; at this location; during a peak period.
B747 flight crew reported that ATC advised them of military activity ahead. The flight crew got a TCAS RA after a fighter jet intercepted them.
B737 Captain reported not making some of the crossing restrictions while flying the CHSLY3 STAR to CLT.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reports that the Survival Kit Lanyard is placed in an inaccessible location on the aircraft.
Flight crew reported a low visibility taxi chart discrepancy.
B737 Flight Attendant reported the crew and passengers were unknowingly exposed to pesticide spray in the form of a heavy fog when they boarded the aircraft.

Legal separation was continued but the Controller is reporting that this type of conflict happens all the time in the Class C environment. The Controller is concerned about the 'boy yelling wolf' scenario that pilots might lose faith in the RA System.
Technician reported unserviceable test equipment caused a misdiagnosis of a pitot/static problem and an aborted takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported lack of taxiway signage and an outdated airport diagram resulted in confusion during taxi at PIT airport.
Two B737 flight crews reported that a localizer overshoot and cloudy conditions resulted in the inability to maintain visual separation.

ZKC ARTCC sector requested an aircraft be routed around its airspace due to weather and traffic saturation deviating for the weather. The request was denied. An aircraft was issued a vector to avoid another aircraft but the vector was not enough to maintain required lateral separation.
Aircraft Technician reported wrong fuel filter was installed.

An airline Dispatcher reports inadequate company policy for delineating required procedures for changing destination field conditions; especially required aircraft landing performance calculations during extremely cold conditions.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) describes why he believes competing interests on the ramp make it difficult; if not impossible; to comply with his air carrier's policies and procedures. Center Controller reported an airspace violation and traffic conflict while opening a sector from one Controller working three sectors which were unmanageable due to weather deviations and volume.
CE560 Captain reported a near miss with a UAV.

Air carrier Captain reported interference from a local church broadcast interfered with his communication with the tug driver at BOI airport.
Newly trained A321 Captain reported he was uncomfortable with the small amount of flying he has been doing since training.

A Flight Service Station Specialist reported receiving a report from an aircraft that a construction vehicle entered the runway while they were landing.
A TRACON Controller reported being issued an unsatisfactory performance report by their supervisor; but feels they did nothing wrong.
Air carrier First Officer reported that flight dispatchers are filing flight plans that use the wrong arrival into an airport; causing problems for flight crews during arrival.
B737 flight crew experiences a false TCAS RA to climb apparently generated by two B-1's above the B737. After initially climbing the RA switches to a descent.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported ATC was not notified about their aircraft which was operationally restricted to 200 knots.

A B777 Captain attempted to comply with his company's policy to determine which aircraft contained AIMS (Airplane Information Management System) BP-16 upgrade; but was unaware of the process to access the data display.

B737-800 First Officer reported concern with the new company checklist and policy changes related to crosswind training.

Several flight crews refused to conduct a gear down; overwater ferry as doing so would violate provisions for conduct of such flight. The operation was ultimately shopped to a crew who agreed to do so despite the refusal of others.
Technician reported an aircraft he was assigned to work was previously not evaluated properly for ground damage and subsequently flew in revenue service in a non airworthy condition.
E170 Captain reported that a towbar broke during pushback; resulting in a return to the gate.
B757 flight crew reported exceeding 200 knots below Class B airspace.

Light Transport pilot reported that while inbound the A80 TRACON Controller acknowledged the plan to depart VFR and pick up an IFR clearance in the air; but later the subsequent controller denied the request.
CE-560 flight crew reported that declining a ferry flight due to fatigue.
Flight Attendant reported a mother and young daughter would not comply with face mask policy.
A319 Captain reported the ground crew was not aware the APU was inop and did not understand hand signals for connecting ground power to the aircraft.
BE99 flight crew reported a gear up landing while training; as a result of inattentive supervision and an incomplete abnormal procedures checklist.
Air carrier ramp employee reported jet fuel still leaking onto the ramp from fuel carts even after a safety report.

During climb out from DTW; Cleveland Center cleared the aircraft to resume normal speed; but the crew did not know about the 280 kt restriction above 10;000 ft until they changed their iPad screen size. Similar issues are seen on iPad ORD and LAX SID speed constraint placements.

A300 flight crew experiences a bird strike on takeoff that seems to be in the nose gear area. Holding is initiated to contact Maintenance and ATC informs that another crew saw fire from the left engine at rotation. The crew elects to return to the departure airport where three bent fan blades are discovered by Maintenance.
C172 pilot noticed flashed outside and initially thought it was a laser. On closer inspection they realized it was flashes from holiday decorations.
Air carrier Captain reported having no pillows on flights due to COVID-19 and not being able to adequately rest.
A Center Controller reported an air carrier descended without complying with a crossing restriction and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
SAN Tower Supervisor was notified by the Center that the clearance issued to a departure via PDC was different than the clearance shown by the Center.
B737NG flight crew reported a trim problem during climbout resulting in a return to field.

IAD support specialist reports of a flight inbound and outbound to/from IAD that is using the same call sign at the same time. He reports this has been happening for over 7 months and nothing has been done about it.
CRJ flight crew reported a Nose Gear warning message on takeoff resulted in a return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported operations was forcing the flight crew to try to extend their Flight Duty Period when known delays were in effect due to weather and a ramp closure.
Air carrier flight reported due to ATC staffing issues the flight would have to be delay vectored. They opted to land at an alternate airport and receive more fuel; then continued on to destination.
PA32 pilot reported not being able to restart engine after an unsuccessful air-start attempt.

Denver Center Traffic Management Coordinator reported of a false target that looked like a Near Mid Air Collision. Controller stated it was the scariest thing he had seen and wanted someone to work on the RADAR/FUSION problem.
ILS coupling during approach to 22L EWR resulted in oscillating pitch changes above 2000 feet.
PAO Tower Controller reported an airspace deviation with an aircraft due to adjacent facility not allowing penetration for sequence.
Air carrier Captain reported during an inflight medical emergency with an unconscious passenger; neither two physicians nor the flight attendants could operate a new model blood pressure cuff.

Light Transport flight crew reported experiencing communication issues with Miami Center and Nassau Approach. Flight crew elected to divert due to departing traffic from the original destination and the inability of the ZMA Controller to give them a missed approach clearance.
EMB175 Captain reported failure to follow procedure for an unstabilized approach.
B737 flight crew reported being chastised by an FAA inspector post-flight for not making a standard announcement when the seat belt sign was turned off in cruise.
Flight Attendant reported being unable to log into the electronic manual during a medical emergency.
An Air Carrier pilot reported while on short final to their filed diversion airport Dispatch requested they divert to a different airport.
B737-700 Captain reported the island at position 8A on the Jeppsen 10-9 chart is not depicted on the 10-9B chart; causing confusion and possible taxiway incursion.

ZLA Center Controller receiving a handoff told an adjacent sector to stop the aircraft's climb for converging traffic. The Controller making the handoff was distracted by other tasks and forgot to comply with the instruction causing less than required separation with the converging traffic.
B737 flight crew reported NMAC during climb out.
B787 Captain reported they taxied on restricted taxiway after landing roll.
Pilot reported an NMAC at a closed towered airport.
Air carrier Captain was concerned about the removal of special qualifications for operations into SEQM.
ZAB ARTCC Controller reported a NORDO aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737NG First Officer reported rejecting the takeoff at 110 kts after experiencing multiple bird strikes on the takeoff roll.
Technician reported work environment concerns that aren't being taken care of by management.

A Lead Technician with multiple years of experience as an Aircraft Maintenance; Engine Start; Run-up; and Taxi Instructor; reports about the recent policy changes to the training and re-qualification requirements in their Procedures Manual. They believe the changes are flawed and will increase 'at risk' behaviors due to the lack of not accomplishing fleet specific proficiency Check rides on each type of fleet aircraft as previously required.
Air carrier flight crew and Dispatcher reported ATC routed the flight offshore beyond the non-EOW (Extended Over Water) limit.
A helicopter pilot reported tower wire markers are placed on lower wires and higher wires do not have markers.

Customer Service employee reported there is a lack of proper staffing in the lobby; which makes it difficult to follow CDC social distancing recommendations as passengers bunch up in groups while waiting in line.

Atlanta Center (ZTL) Controller reported a Near Mid Air Collision he attributed to lack of sufficient radio coverage. Controller had to use a backup emergency radio and transmit into the blind. Aircraft heard transmission and turned away from traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported that the Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) freezes while using Jeppesen FliteDeck-Pro application; and functionality is only restored after a restart of the EFB.
LJ60 Captain reports sighting a UAV at 5;000 feet while on downwind for Runway 18L at CVG.
B737 Captain reported possibly encountering a drone that flew by the right wing while descending through 9000 ft on approach to ATL near POORS.
SR22 pilot reported experiencing GPS signal interruption on successive approaches to Santa Monica airport.
Captain of a sky diving aircraft reported losing consciousness on a flight at FL250; even though he was on oxygen.
Small transport Captain reported the First Officer taxied the aircraft off the taxiway onto a non-movement area.
A Center controller reported an aircraft was overtaking a leading aircraft even though the ground speed readouts indicated the trailing aircraft was slower.
Flight Attendant reported changing seats during takeoff and landing in order to stay a safe distance from a passenger who appeared ill.
Small aircraft pilot reported striking uncharted wires while performing low altitude training.
Tower Controller reported a 'plethora of deals' due to working with small arrival holes and not using the designated departure runway.
Captain reported that a new Takeoff Checklist policy could result in departure delays and interfere with ATC departure sequencing at SFO.
B747 pilot reported the scheduling practices used at her international airline were extremely fatiguing and unsafe.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC on approach with aircraft that penetrated BNA airport airspace.
A Tower Local Controller reported the ASDE-X alerted on traffic that was already airborne and past a vehicle holding short of the runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported an unreliable localizer signal for SBY Runway 32.
Tracon Controllers reported a Frontline Manager not taking control of a situation; due to staffing and the Frontline Managers lack of training.
PDX Tower Controller and an A320 Captain reported a traffic conflict on the Localizer due to VFR traffic at VOU.

A crew departed the BOS North Cargo Ramp with clearance to Taxiway B via Taxiway L but turned left after leaving the Cargo Ramp and taxied via the General Aviation Ramp to Taxiway B. The event occurred during night conditions with snow. More accurate North Cargo airport charting was suggested.
MOB TRACON Controller reported lost communications with two VFR aircraft which were attempting to avoid adverse weather in an area where radio communications are routinely lost.

Tower Controller reported poor quality connection on the crash phone; and no one taking responsibility to repairing it. Controller also cited a need for training on coordination procedures to the downtown Sioux City response; or eliminated from tower line.
B757 Captain reported fatigue on international trip.
Pilot reports the AWOS system at VKX includes lengthy foreign language reports which block out UNICOM frequency communications.

Flight crew missed the mandatory crossing altitude NOTAM change from 7;000 to 8;000 feet at the final fix (ARCHI) on the DYAMD TWO RNAV Arrival to SFO. Due to the amount of time that the NOTAM has been in effect; crew recommended that the charted altitude be changed at the next revision.

The reporter describes an instance of an aircraft conflict alert activating when there is no traffic. Reporter stated this is a common problem at facilities that have upgraded to FUSION radar display software.
S56 TRACON Controller reported an A320 on a vector to intercept a Localizer flew through the Localizer course placing the aircraft below the MVA.
B737-700 flight crew reported 2 MELs that when combined did not provide sufficient bleed air to properly pressurize the aircraft during descent.
B737NG flight crew reported overshooting their cleared altitude departing EWR after encountering wake turbulence.
Two maintenance technicians reported that after installing an engine in a Eurocopter it was discovered they failed to install the bushings in the aft mount.
Tower Controller reported that the ASDE-X triggered a go around due to the helicopter not being recognized by the software.

Air carrier First Officer reported difficulty communicating with the tug driver during pushback because the tug driver was wearing a mask in compliance with COVID-19 procedures. Reporter suggested the mask was not necessary when the driver was alone in the tug.
MWH Tower Cab Radar Controller In Charge reported a loss of separation after inadvertently descending an aircraft into another aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported food that is provided for passengers and crew is unfit for human consumption on specific flights.
B737-800 pilot reported inadequate training on use of new checklist procedures.
Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to a inappropriate MEL; as well as miscommunication with supervisor.
PC-12 flight crew reported hitting a turkey on departure; then returning to airport as a precaution.
GA pilot reported that on two different occasions; a loud noise on frequency in the ZLA sector resulted in a loss communications with ATC.
EMB-175 flight crew reported the ramp crew pushed the aircraft without clearance; towards conflicting taxiing aircraft.
Aircraft Maintenance Technicians reported that proper safety procedures were not followed by the contract vendor while working on aircraft.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported Raft #2 in overhead bin missing mooring line and activation pin exposed posing a hazard of accidental inflation in the passenger cabin.
ZDC ARTCC Controller reported an airspace violation; a low altitude alert; and communication problem with a local Tower.
A320 flight crew experienced a GPWS terrain warning descending to 2000 feet 5 NM south of TOLOC during a night visual approach to PDX.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with communication and possible health issues resulting from wearing a face mask for so long.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft are coming out of storage with circuit breakers and other systems in the incorrect setting/position.
Technician reported an aircraft was supposed to be deep cleaned after possible COVID-19 contamination; but when cleaners where questioned they had not been advised to deep clean the aircraft.
The crew responded to an RA after departing OKC while trying to check in with Kansas City Center.
Agriculture aircraft pilot reported a near miss with a UAV approximately 10 miles southeast of LRJ.
Ramp worker reported that a co-worker tested positive for COVID-19. The ramp worker was not notified by management and continued to work.
Captain reported commencing approach too high due to TFR; resulted in unstabilized; misconfiguration to a go around followed by visual maneuver at night in high terrain.
HUF Tower Controller working arrival radar reported efforts to provide separation utilizing a D-Brite tower scope were limited and ineffective.
Embraer Captain reported encountering a drone at 7;500 feet in the vicinity of area.
DAB Controller describes a possible situation due to the Tower TDW [radar display] maps not showing the correct position of intersections on the map.
Center Controller reported that an aircraft descended below the minimum IFR altitude in a non-radar area; resulting in CFTT.
B757 Flight Attendant reported difficulty in fighting an in-flight electrical fire with an expired fire extinguisher.
MD-80 Flight Attendant reported noticing moisture issues resulting from a company change to using regular ice instead of dry ice to cool beverages.
P80 TRACON Controller reported they had to vector an aircraft off of their approach to avoid a merging aircraft target with no transponder.
Pilot reported passing under a parachutist on his landing roll at ASD airport and suggests relocating the drop zone to a safer area.

A B737 pilot reported landing on LAS Runway 25L with taxi clearance taxiway B6 to B but after crossing 25R he entered taxiway A6 because his tablet's Jeppesen airport diagram chart incorrectly depicted A6 but no adjacent; yet separate B6 exit off the Runway 25R.
Two Hot Air Balloon fabric envelopes made contact during their approach to land because of significant terrain changes including the mix of road surfaces; open field; water; and trees.
Air carrier reporter stated there are QRH errors in the performance table accompanying the B737NG Unreliable Airspeed Non-Normal procedure.
A-320 captain points out potential fatigue issues with certain flight pairings.

A Quad City Challenger Rotax Engine wooden propeller departed the aircraft inflight. The aircraft landed safely on a nearby road. The wooden propeller has not been re-torqued after being flown several months earlier from a high humidity area to a dry climate.
SCT controller reported the new MVA requirements east of SNA do not allow controllers to get aircraft low enough for a stable approach.
Pilot reported the Ipad Electronic Flight Bag became unusable shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported that after landing in SKBO he noted differences between taxiway designations and their charting.
EMB-145 Captain took issue with the VFR corridor below the Runway 4R approach path at JFK. The VFR traffic at 500 feet causes a TCAS RA with the EMB145 passing over at 900 feet.

D01 TRACON controllers reported that an aircraft on final that was using visual separation on an adjoining aircraft went around due to a Resolution Alert. This made the scenario worse because the aircraft was heavy and flew over another aircraft.
DH-8-400 Captain reported having the APU on while being deiced when it should have been off. The checklist layout is cited as contributing to the mistake.
Ground employee reported the employee bus carries more than 10 passengers and 6 feet social distancing separation is impossible.
BE36 pilot reported a NMAC with paraglider; ATC stated it is a common occurrence for the ORL area.
Air carrier Captain stated two very bright billboards prior to the Approach Light System inbound to LAX are a distraction and a hazard to landing aircraft.
Flight crew reported GPWS warning on approach to landing and elected to continue.
A Maintenance Inspector working on a Cessna Citation reported that the Owner/Operator failed to properly document completion of tasks previously accomplished.

B737 flight crew reported confusion about the location of DAL Taxiway P on the ramp because the airfield chart does not sufficiently describe a large aircraft parking area north of Taxiway P which can be taxied into.
Air carrier Captain reported a Jeppesen 10-9 Chart for ELP was incorrect.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the first aid equipment was defective when needed for an ill passenger.

ZNY ARTCC Controller reported an ATC Computer assigned a beacon code to an aircraft 200 miles from the Controller's airspace. The inconsistency was only realized after the Controller issued control instructions to the aircraft and none were complied with. Eventually the error was caught shortly before the aircraft transitioned out of the sector.
B737-800 First Officer reported while at V1 speed encountered a bird strike so they aborted the takeoff.
ERJ-170 pilot identifies discrepancies between Jeppesen and LIDO charts at MDW.

On short final; an MD-11 was impacted by a bird; which sheared a hydraulic line in the nose gear assembly. This resulted in a loss of the Number 3 Hydraulic System and ability to taxi. The crew requested and received a tow to parking.

A Finals sector at Atlanta Approach became extremely busy. Due to weather deviations this sector was being fed more aircraft than planned for by the Traffic Management Program and controllers. The sector was receiving aircraft at high speeds and insufficiently sequenced. The controller advised the supervisor of the situation. The controller was to be relieved from the sector but felt they were too busy to take the time to give a new controller a briefing. The controller felt the supervisor was not responding to his requests for spacing from the sectors feeding him. The controller advised the supervisor he would not accept any more aircraft handoffs to his sector. Another controller was sent to relieve him but he felt too busy to give a complete briefing. The relieving controller assumed the position after observing for a while.

An Air Carrier Captain expressed specific concerns about the environment surrounding the new Runway 10R/28L at FLL which is significantly elevated in order to allow it to run over a freeway system at the east end. Of concern were insufficient lighting designating steep drop offs; mis-located turnoff signs; the failure to use red lighting to highlight threat areas; and the use of reflectors rather than red lighting as the reflectors are not illuminated by landing lights in a timely manner.

An aircraft was receiving VFR Services from Seattle Center (ARTCC). The ARTCC terminated service with the aircraft and advised them to contact Portland TRACON overlying the aircraft's route. The TRACON advised the aircraft that they had transitioned through UAO's Class D airspace. Even though this designated airspace is not yet published the pilot was aware of it. The pilot believed since he was receiving ATC service from the ARTCC he was allowed to transit this airport's Class D airspace.
Helicopter pilot reported loss of GPS signal near CRQ.
Purser reported multiple issues and difficulties during the boarding process and cited not having enough flight attendants to properly staff the flight as a contributing factor.

Flight instructor reported a confusing note on the RNAV (GPS) RWY 16 approach at DMW that informed the decision to make a procedure turn; however ATC subsequently advised the procedure turn was not authorized.
First Officer reported two EICAS messages on final; causing a go around to allow time to run the QRH; landing safely on next approach.
A Cessna Citation Captain reported a loss of GPS signal and satellite coverage along V139 over W-107B at 17;000 feet. GPS function returned a short time later.
Air carrier Captain reported taxi confusion due to conflicting information between the airport chart; ATC's instructions and safety information received from the company Ramp Control.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported experiencing physical allergic symptoms when wearing the new company uniforms.
An Approach Controller reported an aircraft did not comply with their vector in a timely manner and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Due to incorrectly applying the MEL for the 'AutoSpoilers' the crew incurred an 'AutoBrakes' aural warning during takeoff roll leading to the decision to reject the takeoff.
GSO TRACON Controller reported the radar scope went blank for a few seconds.

ZTL Controller reported a situation while using TBFM [Time Based Flow Management] into Atlanta when he observed a shift in times that made his job more difficult. Controller wants TBFM stopped until everyone at ZTL understands how it supposedly works.
Air carrier First Officer reported that the first four rows of the aircraft were to be left unoccupied for weight and balance reasons; but found out when airborne some of the seats were occupied.
A Ramp Controller reported an aircraft pushed back without contacting them at the same time there was an aircraft in the ramp waiting for parking.

ZLA Controller hands off an aircraft to another sector. The handoff; because of a Temporary Altitude in the scratch pad area; goes to another sector. The handoff should have went to the sector that the aircraft was 'temped' at; not one that is possibly a future scope.

An ERJ-175 Captain reported dry ice loaded in the aft cargo bin; a hazmat violation in this aircraft type. Additionally; ground personnel began the pushback without crew clearance after brake release. Ground personnel training appears to be a problem.
A319 Captain reported concern regarding low amount of fuel on board per flight plan.
Pilot reported a NMAC with drone at 9500 ft.

A B777 Captain described one part of his company's FAR 117 international ETOP operations compliance shortens preflight time to less than 60 minutes which is unsafe and inadequate for security; crew briefing; flight planning; and aircraft preflight.
Beechcraft Travelair pilot reported the use of a voltage converter damaged handheld supplemental electronic equipment.
Technician reported errors and omissions in Work Cards relating to portable fire extinguishers on company aircraft being returned to service after storage.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while taxiing to DAL Runway 13R at night and following another aircraft; they failed to hold short at the Runway 13R displaced threshold line. FO stated they did not expect the Hold Short line to be that far back from the Runway entry.
Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited fatigue from being understaffed as contributing factor.

The Tower Controller in Charge observed a truck entering a taxiway. The Ground Controller did not see the truck. The CIC reports this portion of the airport is blocked from the view of the Local and Ground controllers.

Embraer Legacy 550 Captain reported that his company operations attempted to alter his crew's off-duty time in order to meet flight schedule requirements. The Flight Operations Manual justification that the company was using was found to be in error.
EMB-145 First Officer reported company pressure to operate with lack of proficiency and comfort from the right seat.

A Dispatcher reported an aircraft departed NTD with incorrect ZFW numbers from CLP.  The aircraft was 19;700 lbs. heavier than what CLP was told by 'ride along.'  The air crew were given incorrect numbers prior to takeoff.
C172 pilot reported having transponder issues; then additional equipment problems having to land to determine what was wrong with the aircraft.
An air taxi flight crew reported an issue with the ILS Runway 6L signal at CLE that caused an unexpected pitch up and down with the autopilot engaged.
A320 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple bird strikes in both engines.
Flight Attendants are finding heavy duty seals on the Portable Breathing Equipment (PBE) that has to be cut off in order to use the equipment.
B767-300 flight crew reported executing a go-around after receiving a low altitude alert from the Tower during the Stadium Visual to EWR Runway 29.

At this airport aircraft on approach to parallel runways must be separated by 1000 feet vertically until established at a certain point on the approach. The controller believed the aircraft were established on the approach and discontinued vertical separation clearing the aircraft for approach. Upon a later review using more precise displays it was determined one of the aircraft were not established within the confines of the approach. The controllers working the aircraft do not have the detailed precise radar display that is used afterwards to analyze their performance.
A ZLC Center Controller reported they received a non-radar aircraft from another sector that was below the Minimum En-route Altitude.
CRJ-900 Captain reported ordering evacuation of the aircraft due to a ground equipment fire.
ZDV Center Controller reported receiving a NORDO aircraft that wasn't in radar coverage below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Boeing 737 Captain reported being unhappy with the alternate airport on the flight plan; so he attempted to contact the Dispatcher to change it. He couldn't get ahold of him on the SatPhone because he said it's sub-par equipment on their aircraft.
SEA Local Controller reported an aircraft enter their Class B airspace on a conflicting course with an air carrier arrival.
Ground personnel reported that due to hand held radios being unuseable; normal work assignments were not being accomplished.

A flight plan for an active enroute flight was removed from the ATC system. The aircraft's 'full' data block dropped off the Controller's scope and only a partial data block remained on the radar display. The controllers did not notice for approximately 50 miles and did not realize an aircraft was flying through their airspace.
SCT Controller reported a Parachute jump zone has been established which is in conflict with RNAV Arrival routes and busy VFR transition traffic.
Captain discovered that FMC's are designed to sometimes auto load a runway with a particular RNAV departure.

An Air Carrier Captain describes a series of flights over time in which similar sounding company call signs created the potential for ATC and pilot communication errors. No apparent conflict mitigation was evident.
Ground personnel reported installation of permanent posts inside the Operational Safety Zone at aircraft gate.
Flight Attendant reported feeling hot; sweaty and nearly fainting while having to wear a COVID-19 mask during flight.
A pilot reported there is no way to receive the current weather for 3B1 non-towered airport while airborne.

Air carrier Captain reported checking in and hearing the Controller tell everybody to stand by.  They continued on assigned heading and altitude; finally being told they were on the wrong frequency. Controller reportedly was working multiple frequencies.

Air carrier Captain reported experiencing jamming activity which resulted in loss of GPS position and multiple other system failures. Shortly after the Captain reported the issue to ATC; the jamming stopped and systems returned to normal.
Air carrier Captain reported bleed-through from a nearby radio station into the headset of ground crew blocked some communications during pushback at MIA airport.
CRJ-700 Flight Attendant reported the passenger entry door handrail came forward more than it should not allowing the door to close using the normal procedures due to a missing retaining clip.
EA-500 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding Airbus on arrival into CLT.
CRP Controller reports of the discontinuity of the STARS at his facility and the STARS at NGP. The interface between the two systems does not allow handoffs; which must be made manually.

CNO Local Controller reported an aircraft departing on downwind turned into traffic on the ILS final approach course to avoid ONT Class C. Based on local agreements the airspace belongs to CNO Tower during its hours of operation; but this is not indicated on the VFR charts.
When accessing the Fan Air Valve; Maintenance was unable to secure the Fan Reverser in the open position per the Maintenance Manual because the proper tooling was unavailable.

Air carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation during approach to CLT while on the JONZE 2 RNAV Arrival.  Captain stated the CDA arrivals to CLT are allegedly problematic and should be eliminated or a 'smoother process' should be implemented.
Air carrier flight crew reported finding evidence of bird strike on walk around and difficulties finding procedure limitations for their destination airport.
A pilot over San Francisco reported a commercial radio broadcast loud enough to block NCT and SFO Tower necessitating the flight's exit from Class B airspace.
First Officer reported that during pushback ground personnel used nonstandard commands and phraseology. The reporter felt this issue was due primarily to the use of outsourced ground staff.
TRACON Departure Controller reported becoming overloaded when a departure deviated from course and allowed another aircraft to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier ramp employee expressed concern about the safety of reconfigured belt loaders.
Flight Attendant reported survival kit lanyard was incorrectly installed; causing confusion over its location.

Dispatcher experiences a loss of ACARS communication with one of his flights while inbound to MMMX during a weather event. Attempts at voice communication through Commercial Radio are also unsuccessful. The communication outage lasts for over one hour; until the aircraft lands safely in MMMX.
A pilot reported their takeoff clearance was blocked by frequency congestion causing a delay and canceled takeoff clearance.

An Observer near Texas Tech University Medical Center (TA79) reported a new crane 120 ft high is lighted but its boom; over 100 ft long horizontally; is not lighted and presents a hazard for helicopters taking off and landing at the adjacent helipad.
C560XL flight crew reported encountering severe wake turbulence on the arrival into LAX in trail of a B757 that resulted in a 60 degree bank.
A general aviation pilot reported symptoms of hypoxia due to smoke from widespread fires in Northern California led to a diversion.
Maintenance Controller reported two deferrals for the MD-11 fuel system were not compatible and the aircraft was removed from service to correct the problem.

A regional airline Captain writes to suggest that Company policy be changed to require that one engine be started thirty minutes prior to aircraft boarding at stations which do not provide air carts for aircraft cooling during the hot summer months.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported diverting for IDG and APU problems. They discovered that a month earlier another crew had a similar experience with the exact same outcome.
Air carrier Captain reported entering 'KLINE' into the FMS instead of the correct spelling 'KLYNE.' The two fixes are in close proximity and the error was not initially apparent.
ZOA Center controller reported that a sectors' workload has become unmanageable resulting in delays and aircraft conflicts due to the variety of traffic situations and equipment limitations.
B737 flight crew reported a TCAS RA with a small aircraft while on approach to EWR.
EMB-505 flight crew reported confusion with ATC instructions.
F11 TRACON Controller reported a conflict after an aircraft came over at the incorrect altitude.
General aviation pilot reported two small dogs on the runway on landing rollout. Pilot took evasive action with excess braking to avoid striking one of them.
A pilot reported his B737-700 had 11 complex MELs with a 27 page print of MEL procedures needing to be checked or procedures needing to be completed prior to flight.
B737 Captain reported new company procedure calling for no APU start on taxi in are causing some safety concerns.
G-IV Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL340 from another aircraft about 10 miles ahead of them.
Air carrier First Officer reported that aircrews are not being advised of multiple taxiway closures at IAH and the NOTAMs provided are incomplete.
NCT TRACON Controller reported about confusion; an airspace violation and constant problems associated with an airport where one sector works the arrival and another sector works the departure.

A Controller reported an aircraft that lined up for the wrong runway due to lighting of the runway. The Controller reported the lights were on step one and he would have had them on step three and also stated the Front Line Managers (FLMs) are having issues with lighting at the airport.

An Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) describes his work environment where the lack of adequate Ground Power Units (GPUs) to supply electrical power to all their ERJ-170 aircraft requires the running of APU's all night in their hangar. As a result; due to the excessive noise levels; mechanics have chosen not to wear ear protection in order to communicate even though they face long term hearing loss.
B737 pilot reported similar sounding call sign on the same route could have caused confusion.
Maintenance Technician reported that a proper daily check would not be possible due to special equipment that would be needed but not available.

A Fractional pilot reported while on initial approach they received a destination change from Dispatch which did not include enough flight plan information so they landed at their original destination.
A321 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after encountering what appeared to be party balloons climbing through 8;000 ft. Remains of multiple balloons were found in the right engine.
A319 Captain reported inoperative APU and excessive temperatures; coupled with insufficient cooling systems caused unsafe operations.
P50 TRACON Controller reported that during a high volume of traffic they did not notice an airborne conflict after an aircraft descend out of Class B airspace and into Class D airspace.
Maintenance technician reported that careless placement of wheel chocks on the airport ramp created a safety hazard.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into PHX in trail of a B737.
Ground employee expressed concern over being the only employee who wears a face mask and works in close proximity with other team employees.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft flew below MVA while correcting back to localizer course.
B737 crew reported a course deviation on the STAR to LAX when runway assigned was not the runway briefed and programmed into the FMC.
ZNY Center Oceanic Controller reported they had less than required separation between two aircraft on crossing courses even though the ATOP tool did not identify a separation problem.
Flight Instructor reported that one of his students flew an approved solo flight before obtaining a Student Pilot Certificate via IACRA.
CRJ Captain and Flight Attendant reported they noticed a faint burning odor which was traced to an overheating cell phone charger in the cabin.
Two maintenance inspectors reported that incorrect fuel nozzles were installed on a Beechcraft BE-300 engines.
B767 Captain reported required documentation for hazardous material was challenged and incorrectly filled out by gate agent.
B737 Captain reported a climb gradient requirement appears to be missing from the SFO ILS Rwy 28L SA CAT II approach chart.
B777 Captain reported un-commanded aircraft movement while at the gate prior to departure.

An air carrier flight crew flew the MFR RNAV (GPS) D and was surprised by a EGPWS TERRAIN Alert while on profile at 8;500 feet. The EGPWS escape maneuver was completed in IMC; but the approach was completed normally.

Air carrier flight crew reported turning the wrong way on taxiway due to not clearly understanding Controller instructions. FO stated controllers wearing face masks could have contributed to them not clearly understanding the clearance.

A Captain reports about deficiencies in the flight planning function of the computer system and its effect on dispatch functionality. The clumsy automation increases workload and decreases effectiveness for dispatch.
Air carrier First Officer and Tracon Controller reported a missed crossing restriction on the DRMMM1 arrival into ATL.
B737 First Officer reported a ground conflict while entering the ramp due to confusing company and ATC policies.
A pilot of a Cessna 206B reported while on approach into an uncontrolled airport using CTAF came within 150 feet of colliding with another aircraft below him.
ZAN Controller reported of three operational errors caused by communication inadequacies; and automation issues.
B737-900 Maintenance Technician reported that the information in the IPC was incorrect and misleading.
ZAN ARTCC Controllers reported the current IFR Low Altitude Enroute Chart for their airspace had an MEA error.
Boeing 737 Captain reported a loss of ability to taxi due to wheel slippage caused by glycol on the tarmac surface.

General Aviation pilot flying VFR reported experiencing an airborne conflict with an airliner. Pilot stated that lighter traffic during the COVID-19 pandemic was a factor in the decision to not contact ATC; which may have contributed to the event.
Jacksonville Center Controller reported staffing problems associated with no one available to assist another Controller whose sector was in the red.
Flight Attendant reported one lavatory sink inoperative and no hand soap available on multiple flights.
Maintenance installed an incorrect tire on a CRJ-900 aircraft.

A pilot reported the NOS version of the BOS HYLND FOUR RNAV SID Runway 4R became ambiguous at waypoint STAYC because without textual or graphical description a pilot could fly to either waypoint HYLND or SHAAN.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported receiving a reassignment that would have had her awake for 25 hours before the final arrival.
A Beach Bonanza Pilot reported that ATC informed him that he triggered a terrain alert.
Air carrier Captain reported safety concern with ground crew operations during taxi.
MLI Approach Controller reported ATC denied the request of an arriving Heavy jet for the longest runway to reduce a possible departure delay for Wake Turbulence requirements.
B737 Check Airman reports that the company's documentation provided to crews concerning Controller Pilot Data Link Communications equipment needs calcification/simplifying.
First Officer reported a passenger was not complying with wearing a face mask pre-departure and was ultimately allowed to fly without having to wear it.
A CRJ-700 Captain reported not being able to find a published hold on the EFB chart. Eventually they discovered they had zoomed in too far and the hold was published out of view on the chart.

Tracon and Tower Controllers reported an airborne conflict due to limited visibility while working from an alternate room because of a wild bat in the Tower Cab. An aircraft was cleared for takeoff with a landing aircraft on short final.
HCF TRACON trainee reported observing an aircraft deviate from the expected missed approach procedure below the minimum IFR altitude and towards arriving traffic.
TPA Controller describes a loss of separation due to data tag overlap and amount of traffic during the session.

A Captain new to ETOPS operations did not have formal ground school training or a Check Airman for his first Hawaiian trip. After weeks of unsuccessful training requests he refused to fly the day of the trip.
