B737-700 flight crew reported contact with the jet bridge occurred during boarding possibly due to company policy that does not call for brakes set until later in the preflight.
Flight Attendant reported that a passenger who had tested positive for COVID-19 was removed from the aircraft; however; other passengers were not deplaned and the aircraft was not sanitized.
B737 NG Captain reported a Flight Attendant was injured during an encounter with turbulence; possibly wake-related; on descent into LAS.

B737 First Officer reported an anomaly with charting hold patterns in the ORD area. Some patterns are published on the second page and crews are not guided to look for the patterns there by zooming out on their EFB's.

Corporate jet Captain reported on departure from AVQ they were given an immediate turn for parachute activity at MZJ on check in with U90 Controller. This was not listed in NOTAMS or mentioned when the pilot called to obtain a departure frequency and squawk code.
B737-700 flight crew reported exceeding the SID airspeed restriction; which they attributed to distractions of two separated airborne conflicts encountered during the climb.
A320 Captain reported a truck drove into the safe zone while he was parking at a gate in IAH and the 'Safe Park' system failed to warn him.

A CRJ-700 was dispatched with a planned landing fuel of 3;900 lbs. With delay vectors enroute; the flight landed with 2;200 lbs which was less than expected even with delays. The crew questioned the company planned fuel burn number.
Air carrier flight crew encountered an unstable ILS glide slope during approach.
On a particular route to an airport aircraft of the same company with similar call sign numbers arrive simultaneously causing confusion.
B-737 Captain reported confusing instructions and procedures in a MEL.
EMB-145 First Officer reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 FT in response to an RA on arrival into LGA.

A Tower Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff in between two landing aircraft. The Controller did not notice that the second arriving aircraft was past the landing threshold when the departure was only 4;000 feet down the runway.
Pilot reported the frequency for operating the pilot controlled lights at PVC airport is incorrect on the RNAV 7 chart.
B737 Flight Crew reported navigating to wrong; similar sounding waypoint.
Reporter on a small transport aircraft stated they had a drone sighting during approach to OKC airport.
757 Captain reported the jetbridge is set to stop too far from the door leaving a large gap that could cause injury to a boarding passenger especially young children.
Air carrier First Officer reported that a person checked their temperature after landing with an ear thermometer and did not replace the tip between checks.

Corporate Captain reported being confused by taxi instructions from PHKO Ground to taxi via Kilo; Alpha to Runway 17. They entered Alpha via Lima thinking the 'K' on the airport diagram is a ramp designation.

A B737-800 aft cargo compartment floor was damaged after six 210 pound unsecured pallets were placed in it. The crew was additionally concerned about the unsecure cargo shifting at Vr. The pallets were removed and the aircraft taken out of service.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on a visual approach to ATL in trail of an MD-11; resulting in a 40 degree roll and 5 degrees nose down attitude.

An aircraft on a SID proceeded to a point on their flight plan after the SID transition. The aircraft are supposed to stay on the SID heading expecting radar vectors. This is not indicated clearly on the SID and causes confusion for aircraft on a regular basis.
Technician reported a Supervisor made two calls before reluctantly providing him with a mask. Technician stated they are not adequately being supplied with PPE equipment during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to the red runway incursion warning light activating during their takeoff.
EMB-175 Captain commented about new company fuel policy which does not take into account actual conditions and distances on planned routings.
AA5B pilot reported encountering a large number of balloons in flight that had been released in the vicinity of his destination; PYM.

Flight Attendant reported that a call was made that the cleaners were off the aircraft when they actually were not.  Passengers were allowed on and had to duck into rows to allow the cleaning crew off the aircraft.
An Approach Controller reported that a Local Controller cleared an aircraft to land; while another aircraft was cleared for takeoff from a converging non-intersecting runway.

ZOB Controller reports of a Controller coming to work and attempting to find out from medical if they can take some medicine and work. Time passes and there is no answer from medical. Controller complains about not being able to find out if a Controller can work while taking a specific type of medicine.
Ground employee reported that co-workers are not adhering to quarantine rules and are not wearing COVID-19 protective masks.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an RA on final approach for an unidentified VFR target at an adjacent airport.
Ramp worker reported a 'significant weight shift' during passenger disembarkation in a B737-900ER even though they had already unloaded considerable weight from rear pits.
